Sch., St. Peter's, Weston-super-Mare, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 21st Nov., 1888, and served as asst. commr. in Burma; under sec. to chief commr., April, 1892; offg. dep. commr., June, 1895; offg. acct.-gen., Punjab, March, 1900; dep. commr., Burma, Nov., 1902.

DAYARAM GIDUMAL, B.A., LL.B., Statutory C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr., 3rd Jan., 1885; employed from May, 1885, to April, 1886, in compiling a jaghir record for Sind; confd. as member of the civil service, 3rd March, 1887; 1st asst., May, 1896; judge and sessions judge, April, 1897; compiler of 'A Jaghir Record for Sind.'

DE, B.N. (see Brajendra Nath De).

DE, KIRAN CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1900.

DEANE, HAROLD ARTHUR, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Punjab Commn. (chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1874, and from July, 1885, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; placed on special duty with Chitral relief force, March, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; dep. commr., March, 1896; rest. and pol. officer, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Nov., 1896; resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1900; agent to govr.-gen., and chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901.

DEANE, THOMAS, C.B., Col., Indian Army.—First commn., 21st hussars, March, 1862; lieut., Bengal staff corps, March, 1863; adjutant, govr.-gen.'s body guard, Jan., 1871; 3rd asst. sec. to the govt. of India, mil. dept., Jan., 1877; and asst. sec., April, 1884; dep. sec., July, 1886; dir., army remount dept., Nov., 1889, to May, 1898; C.B., June, 1897; on U.S. list, May, 1898; apptd. to purchase stallions for govt. of India, April, 1903.

DEAS, JOSEPH, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 1st Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. settl. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; legal remembrancer, Dec., 1890; sec. to legisl. council, Dec., 1890; also member of the council, Jan., 1891; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1893; jud. commr. in Oudh, April, 1896; ret'd., Mar., 1900.

DEASE, PAGET PATRICK, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st April, 1871, and employed on state railways in the Punjab and Baluchistan; exec. engr., Sept., 1881; engr.-in-chief, Wazirabad-Multan railway survey, Nov., 1892; depd. to Kashmir in 1893, in connection with railway survey; engr.-in-chief, Umarkot-Pachpadra railway survey, Oct., 1893; offg. engr.-in-chief, Kotri-Rohri railway, May, 1895; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Feb., 1896; ret'd., Dec., 1899.

DEB, G. N. (see Girindra Narain Deb).

DEB, G. K. (see Gopendra Krishna Deb).

DEB, M. K. (see Manmatha Krishna Deb).

DEB, R. K. (see Ramendra Krishna Deb).

DE BEAUREPAIRE, ERNEST LUCIEN, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 31st March, 1885.

DE BOURBEL, RAOUL, Maj.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., as lieut., Bengal engrs., 1850; joined the dept., Nov., 1852; exec. engr., Lower Prov., Dec., 1853; asst. to chief engr., Lower Prov., Jan., 1857; dep. consulting engr., railway dept., and asst. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1860; supgt. engr., Gauhati, June, 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Oudh, Feb., 1870; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, 1871; in temporary charge of public works dept. in Baluchistan, 1879-80; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, 1880; chief engr., 1st class, Jan., 1884; retired, 1885.

DE BRETT, EDWARD ADOLPHUS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., Jan., 1897.

DE BROE, VICTOR EDGAR, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. manager, N.W. Rly.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 22nd Jan., 1874, and posted to State Railways; exec. engr., June, 1892; dep. manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand Rly., July, 1900; ditto, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1901.

DE FABECK, WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Entered the army med. dept. June, 1855; joined the Madras med. dept., June, 1857, and served under the mil. dept. to May, 1871, also holding various civil surgeoncies; from Nov., 1873, served in British Burma as civil surgn. and sup. of lock hospital, Maulmain; also health officer, Maulmain, Feb., 1878; dep. surgn.-gen., Nagpur force, and Central India regts. March, 1886; surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras, May, 1890; ret'd. May, 1894; served in the Crimean campaign, 1855-56 (medal and clasp) and in the Mutiny, 1859 (medal).

DEIGHTON, KENNETH, B.A., late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as proftr., Agra coll., 19th Feb., 1862; principal, Agra coll., March, 1864; inspr. of schs., Rohilkhand divn., April, 1885; ret'd., 1890.

DE LA COURNEUVE, STEWART HOWARD THOMAS (dep. commr., Burma).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1873; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1880; asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., March, 1891.

DELAWAR HOSAIN AHMED, Khan Bahadur, B.A., late Bengal Registrn. Dept.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; joined the service, 27th Feb., 1861, as dep mag., and dep. collr.; apptd. inspr.-gen. of registrn., Bengal, April, 1895; ret'd., 1900.

DELEVINGNE, WALTER NORMAN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1899; mag. and collr., Jan., 1903.

DE MARSAC, FREDERICK ROOME, late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 8th March, 1864; asst. supt., Jan., 1866; supt., April, 1880; in charge, store dept., May, 1891; ret'd., March, 1892.

DE MELLO, CHARLES HARVEY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Trained at Thomason College; apptd. to dept. as apprentice engr., 21st Nov., 1876; asst.-engr., April, 1879; exec. engr., May, 1894.

DE MONTMORENCY, GEOFFREY FITZHERVEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 6th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DE MORGAN, WALTER CAMPBELL, M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (suprg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service. 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1885; suptg. engr., Feb., 1901.

DEMPSTER, FRANCIS ERSKINE, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept. (chief supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., Oct., 1878, as asst. supt.; supt., Feb., 1893; was in charge of field telegraphs with Chitral relief force, March, 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; chief supt., April, 1902; has received the war medal with 'Chin-Lushai 1889-90' clasp.

DEMPSTER, JAMES INNES ROTHNEY, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 2nd Nov., 1870, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; services lent to Egyptian govt., 1885-89; suptg. engr. April, 1893; ret'd., April, 1899.

DENMAN, JOHN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 4th Dec., 1880, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1891; small cause court judge, Aug., 1893; district and sessions judge, March, 1894.

DENNYS, CHARLES JOHN, Lieut.-Col. Indian Army, Jud. Dept., Punjab (cantonmt. mag.).—First commr., 26th Mar., 1874; services placed at disposal of Punjab govt., July, 1881; offg. asst. cantonmt. mag., Aug., 1881; confd., Oct., 1882; cantonmt. mag., March, 1886.

DENNYS, EDWARD AUGUSTUS, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1875, as acctt.; asst. examr., April, 1876; dep. examr., Dec., 1881; examr., Dec., 1886.

DENNYS, GEORGE WILLIAM PATRICK, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Peshawar).—Joined the service, 31st Oct., 1879; in mil. employ till April, 1882, since when he has held several civil surgeries.

DENTITH, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Bulwich Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DE PALEZIEUX-FALCONNET, GRENVILLE PULTENEY, Major-Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras engrs., 1852; joined the Madras public works dept., Aug., 1854, as asst. engr.; from Dec., 1854, served in the Bengal public works dept. as exec. engr., Pegu; Mhow divn., Rajputana, Nov., 1864; offg. suptg. engr., mil. works, Allahabad, 1872; suptg. engr., Meerut, April, 1874, and

afterwards at Moradabad and Rawalpindi; offd. as inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1883-84; ret'd., 1886.

DE RENZY, SIR ANNESLEY CHARLES CASTRIOT, K.C.B., Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; entered the Bengal med. service, 1851; served through the Burmese war, 1852-53 (medal and clasp), and the Mutiny, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); apptd. first sanitary commr., Punjab, 1868; sanitary commr., Assam, 1878; served in the expedi. against the Nagas, 1879 (clasp and C.B.); dep. surgn.-gen. Punjab frontier force, 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1882; author of sanitary reports to govt. on the Punjab and Assam, and of a paper on the sanitary state of British troops in N. India.

DE ROZARIO, VICTOR PHILIP, Madras Jud. Dept. (judge, Madras small cause court).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1858, and served in various subord. posts; district munsf, Nov., 1864; sub-judge, Oct., 1875; judge, Madras small cause court, Dec., 1892.

DESAI, K. D. (see Khandubhai Gulabhai Desai).

DESHMUKH, L. G. (see Lakshman Gopal Deshmukh).

DE SOUZA, FRANCIS XAVIER, M.A., LL.M., Barr. (1899), Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 20th Jan., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1899; acting joint judge, Mar., 1900; 2nd asst., July, 1900.

DESPEISSIS, JEAN MARIE ANTONY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr. 23rd Sept., 1884, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., July, 1895; lent to Burma Rlys. Co. from Sept., 1896.

DES VOEUX, HERBERT, Captain, Indian Army (dep. commr. Burma).—First commn., 28th Sept., 1887; asst. comdt. Burma Police Battn., Jan., 1890; asst. commr., Dec., 1890; settlt. officer, Aug., 1897; dep. commr., March, 1902.

DEUCHARS, GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1894; lent to Burma Rlys. Co., Sept., 1898.

DEVENISH, JAMES ALDRIDGE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to Bengal; transf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1892, and to Bhurtpore State, Nov., 1894 to Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Dec., 1894; posted to United Prov., Oct., 1902.

DEVONSHIRE (DUKE OF), His GRACE SPENCER COMPTON CAVENDISH, K.G., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; attached to Earl Granville's special mission to Russia in 1856; M.P. for N. Lancashire from 1857 to 1868, when he was returned for the Radnor boroughs; LL.D., Cambridge, 1862; apptd. a lord of the admiralty, March, 1862; under sec. for war, April, 1863; sec. of state for war, Feb.-July, 1866; postmr.-gen., 1868-71; chief sec. for Ireland, 1871-74; lord rector of the univ. of Edinburgh, 1879; was returned for N.E. Lancashire in April, 1880, and became sec. of state for India; sec. of state for war, 1882-85;

a royal commr. for the exhibn. of 1886; chairman of the royal commn. on the labour question, 1891; chancellor of Cambridge univ., 1892; K.G., 1892; lord prest. of the council, 1895 to 1903.

DEWAR, DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at foreign schools and Jesus Coll. Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

DEWAR, FRANCIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. and settlmt. offr.

DEWHURST, ROBERT PAGET, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clitheroe Gr. Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 28th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1899; under sec. to govt., April to Nov., 1900; author of a translation of 'Nafhat-ul-Yaman.'

DE WINTON, THOMAS WALTER, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appointed to the dept. as asst. engr., Nov., 1879; exec. engr., April, 1891.

DE WINTON, WALTER BERNARD, C.I.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1883; in 1885-86 served temporarily on Madras state railway surveys, and in 1890-91 was employed under the inspr.-gen. of mil. works; chief engr. and sec. to govt., April, 1898; C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

DEY, GEORGE GOODAIR, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at North London Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1880; also served in Assam, in 1880, as offg. judge of Sylhet and Cachar; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1887; ret'd., April, 1895.

DHANJISHA NAVROJI PARAKH, L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Med. Service.—Joined the service, Sept., 1874; arrived, 5th May, 1875; employed under the mil. dept. till 1881, including service in the Persian expedn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp); served subsequently as asst. civil surgn., Poona, and in various acting appts.; surgn. to the Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., Jan., 1887; ret'd., Mar., 1898.

DHARMA RAO NAYUDU, REDNAM, Dewan Bahadur, late Madras Salt Dept.—Joined the service, 15th May, 1861, and was employed in the police dept. until April, 1869, when he was transf'd. to the rev. dept. as dep. collr.; personal asst. to salt commr., July, 1878; asst. commr. of salt rev., Feb., 1880; dep. commr., Dec., 1892; ret'd., April, 1896.

DHUR, S. C. (see Syam Chand Dhur.)

DIACK, ALEXANDER HENDERSON, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen, and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1882, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settl. officer; senr. sec. to finl. commr., Nov., 1891, to July 1892; dep. commr. and

settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; rev. and finl. sec. to govt., Punjab, Aug., 1899 to April, 1902; offg. chief sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1901, and April, 1902.

DI CADILHAC, ARTHUR MARIO AGRICOLA COLLIER GALLETTI, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after the exam. of 1899; arrived 10th Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DIMMOCK, HENRY PEERS, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Major, Indian Med. Service, Bombay (profr., Grant med. coll.).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; civil surgn., Sept., 1887; supt., Mahabaleshwar, Sept., 1889; obstetric physician, J.J. hospital, and profr. of midwifery, Grant med. coll., June, 1891; also presidency surgn., April, 1893; member of plague committee, March, 1897.

DIVATIA, N. B. (see Narsingrao Bholanath Divatia.)

DIXON, FREDERIC PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DIXON, FREDERICK THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (district judge, Punjab).—Educ. at London Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, April, 1903.

DIXON, JAMES, late Burma Police Dept.—Apptd. inspr. of police, Sept., 1870; asst. district supt., Oct., 1877, and district supt., Jan., 1883; dep.-inspr.-gen. of civil police, July, 1889; ret'd., May, 1902.

DOBBIE, WILLIAM HERBERT, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (acnt.-gen., Madras).—Educ. at the Merchant Taylors' Sch.; joined the dept., 23rd May, 1872; on famine duty, 1874-75; asst. acnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1875; from Jan., 1876, served in Bengal as acting dep.-acnt.-gen., and as asst. comptr.-gen.; served in Madras, 1878-83, and in the Punjab, 1883-84; asst. acnt.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1884; Punjab, April, 1885; dep. acnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., March, 1886; comptr., Assam, April, 1887; dep. acnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1890; offg. comptr., Hyderabad, July, 1891; dep. comptr.-gen., April, 1896; acnt.-gen., Madras, Jan., 1901.

DOBBS, HENRY ROBERT CONWAY, Indian C.S. (consul for Seistan and Kain).—Educ. at Winchester and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 6th Dec., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, March, 1895; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., June, 1896; joint mag., April, 1897; asst. to rest., Mysore, Oct., 1899; asst. pol. agent, Zohb, March, 1901; consul for Seistan and Kain, June, 1903; author of a monograph on the pottery and glass work of N.W.P. and O.

DOBIE, STANLEY LOCKER, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; supt. of lunatic asylum, Madras, Jan., 1882; served in Afghanistan and Egypt; med. officer, Utakamand, April, 1888, to Nov., 1889; supt. of lunatic asylum, Madras, Jan., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1892; ret'd., July, 1897.

DODD, CHARLES, late Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as offg. prof. Agra coll., 4th Aug., 1868; head master, Bareilly coll., Dec., 1868; prof. of English literature, Agra and Benares colls., Dec., 1874; inspr. of schools, Sept., 1884; ret'd., March, 1894.

DODD, CHARLES ALBERT, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived as ensign, 12th Nov., 1857, and served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); from Feb., 1860, served in the police dept.; personal asst. to inspr.-gen., N.W. Prov., March, 1861; district supt., Aug., 1861; from April, 1870, served as supt., govt. press, and curator of govt. books, N.W. Prov.; also supt. of emigration, Jan., 1883; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1893.

DODERET, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Nov., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settl. officer, May, 1888; 2nd asst., April, 1890; special under sec. to govt. in the dept. of famine relief, Feb.-May, 1892; 1st asst., May, 1893; junr. collr., July, 1897; pol. agent, Dangs, March, 1901; senr. collr., April, 1902.

DOIG, SCROPE BERDMORE, M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 16th July, 1867, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1872; on special duty, Ahmadnagar water-supply scheme, in 1891, and again as member of Sind irrign. commn.; sanitary engr., and member of sanitary board, Jan., 1892; returned to public works dept., as suptg. engr., Oct., 1893; chief engr., Dec., 1896; sec. to govt., June, 1899; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

DOIG, WILLIAM AITKENHEAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Dublin High Sch. and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DONALD, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to Assam as asst. commr., May, 1898; sub-div. offr., April, 1899; under sec. to chief commr., April, 1901.

DONALD, JOHN STUART, C.I.E. (dep. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Joined the service, April, 1882; extra asst. commr., May, 1884; asst. commr., Nov., 1890; accompanied mission to Kabul under Sir H. M. Durand, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; on special duty on the N.W. Frontier, 1894; was British commr. for demarcation of Kurram-Afghan boundary, May-Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Aug., 1900; on special duty with British-Afghan commn. from March, 1903.

DONALD, WILLIAM, late Finl. Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 26th Oct., 1856; from 23rd May, 1859, served in the central public works office, and the civil paymaster's office, Madras, and was apptd. to finl. dept., Aug., 1862; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Aug., 1869; dir. of incorporated bank of Madras, March, 1871; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1874, and at Madras, June, 1875; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1884; compiled the manual of rules and regulations for the use of junior members of Madras civil service; ret'd., 1889.

DONOVAN, CHARLES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after

exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Jan., 1863; from Feb., 1864, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; served in Assam, Sept., 1879, to June, 1882, as dep. commr.; judge, Benares, Jan., 1885; ret'd., June, 1889.

DORAN, EDWARD ANTHONY, Postal Dept. (postmr.-gen., 2nd grade).—Joined the service, 2nd Feb., 1880, and served in the postal dept. as clerk, supdt., and asst. dir.-gen.; postmr. gen. and offg. dep. dir.-gen., April, 1901.

DOUGLAS, EDWARD WILLIAM SPENCER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 4th Nov., 1871, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Sept., 1872; transfld. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrign. branch, Nov., 1873; exec. engr., May, 1883; served on state railways in 1885-86 and 1886-87.

DOUGLAS, MONTAGUE WILLIAM, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Punjab).—In mil. employ from 21st March, 1887; asst. commr., April, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1899; C.I.E., June, 1903.

DOUGLAS, SHOLTO JAMES, Barr. (1889), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and inspr. of labourers; from April, 1885, served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1889; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1894.

DOUIE, JAMES MCCRONE, Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at the High Sch., Edinburgh, Univ. of Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 7th Dec., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., sec. to the finl. commr. and settl. officer; senr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1889; dep. commr., Nov., 1891; offg. rev. sec., April, 1893, to Oct., 1894, and from Nov., 1895, to April, 1896; offg. commr. and supt., April, 1898; offg. chief sec. to govt., Punjab, March, 1900; confd., Nov., 1901; member of legisl. council, Punjab, July, 1900; has publ. a transl. of the Biluchinama, with a Biloch grammar.

DOVETON, JOHN CHADWICK, Col., Indian Army late Forest Dept., Central Prov.—First commn., Oct., 1858; asst. conservator of forests, Central Prov., 8th April, 1864; dep. conservator, May, 1865; conservator, Sept., 1868; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1894.

DOWDEN, THOMAS FREEMAN, Col. R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1856; joined the Bombay public works dept., Aug., 1859; under sec. to govt., 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, 1872; manager, Dhond and Manmad state railway, Dec., 1878; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); offg. sec. to agent, Baluchistan, April, 1880; acting senr. dep. consulting engr., and under sec. to govt., Bombay, Nov., 1881; asst. to dir.-gen. of railways, 1884; suptg. engr., July, 1886; dep. consulting engr., Calcutta, April, 1886; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, Feb., 1887; reverted to mil. duty, June, 1892; ret'd. from the army, May, 1893.

DOWN, JAMES ERSKINE, Bombay Police Dept. (inspr.-gen. of police).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1872; asst. supt., Aug., 1875; supt., April, 1876; acting inspr.-gen., Feb., 1898; confd. Jan., 1899.

DOYLE, BERNARD, B.A., M.B., M.Ch., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Punjab.—Apptd. to the Indian med. dept., and arrived 21st Nov., 1876; served in mil. dept. to April, 1881, and afterwards in the Punjab as civil surg. and dep. sanitary commr.; served in Burma, 1886-87; med. adviser to Maharaja of Jhind, Sept., 1887; civil surg., Ambala, March, 1890; civil surg. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Delhi, June, 1890; civil surg., Firozpur, Oct., 1892; Peshawar, July, 1893; Karnal, Oct., 1900; ret'd. July, 1902.

DOYLY, WARREN HASTINGS, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 28th May, 1858, and served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts.; inspr.-gen. of jails, May, 1876; mag. and collr., 1st grade, July, 1876; offg. opium agent, Behar, 1878, 1885-87, and 1889-90; offg. commr., Bhagalpur, 1884-85, and 1889; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

DRAKE, FRANCIS COURTNEY, India Office (senior clerk, rev. and stat. dept.).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, record office, 23rd Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India office, 22nd Aug., 1892; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, Feb., 1894; senr. clerk, July, 1898.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, DIGBY LIVINGSTONE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, EDWARD FORSTER, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surg., 1st Oct., 1866; arrived, 23rd April, 1867, and served under the mil. dept., Madras, and also in acting civil appts.; resst. asst. surg., gen. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., Madras, Oct., 1870; ophthalmic surg., gen. hosp., and prof'r. of ophthalmic surgery and physiology, med. coll., March, 1875; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1885; ret'd., April, 1894; member of India Office med. board, Aug., 1897, to June, 1900.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, ELLIOT GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Gurnsey, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 10th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1895; mag. and collr., Oct., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1900; author of a work on the administration of Birbhum.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, HENRY VERNON, M.A., LL.M., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 10th Dec., 1886, and served in the Central Prov. as acting under sec. and asst. commr.; commr. of excise, Aug., 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1894; divl. judge, April, 1897, to Oct., 1901.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, HERBERT EDWARD, F.R.C.S.E., Major, Indian Med. Service (agency surg., E. Rajputana States).—Entered the service, Sept., 1888; in mil. employ till Nov., 1891, when he was apptd. civil surg. in the N.W. Prov.; surg. to the Bhartpur State, Nov., 1895; agency surg., E. Rajputana States, Nov., 1900.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, RALPH RHENIUS EVANS, A.I.C.E., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 1st March, 1864, and joined sappers and miners; from Dec., 1864, served in the public works dept.; exec. engr., March, 1870; suptg. engr., Nov., 1888; acted in 1889 and 1892-93 as joint sec. to govt. and chief engr., gen. buildings and roads branch; chief engr. for irrig., and joint sec. to govt., irrig. branch, April, 1893; chief engr., 1st class, Jan., 1896; ret'd., Oct., 1896.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, WILLIAM DRAKE, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Served in the Punjab as sub. asst. rev. surveyor, 1852-55; joined Thomason Coll., and thence was apptd. to the public works dept., April, 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov., in the buildings and roads branch; transfed. temporarily to railway dept., 1858-60; employed on famine relief works in 1861, and 1877-78; exec. engr., March, 1874; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1892.

DREW, WILLIAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to Bombay; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; dep. manager, state railways, March, 1898; ret'd. June, 1902.

DREW, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (senr. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 14th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag., and was permanently transfed., June, 1885; inspr. of factories, town of Bombay, May, 1887; 2nd asst. collr., Nov., 1888; provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890; 1st asst. collr. and mag., May, 1891; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1896; senr. collr. and mag., July, 1897.

DRIBERG, JOHN JAMES STREET, late Assam Commn.—Educ. at St. Nicholas Coll., Lancing; joined the service as acting dep. mag. and dep. collr. in Bengal, Nov., 1861; confd., 16th April, 1862; from Dec., 1863, served in Assam as extra asst. commr.; asst. civil and pol. officer with the Bhutan Duars field force, 1864-65 (medal and clasp); dep. supt. of census in Assam, Oct., 1880; supt. of emigration, June, 1882; dep. commr., May, 1886; inspr.-gen. of jails, &c., Nov., 1891; in 1893 acted as commr., Assam valley districts; ret'd., April, 1896.

DRUMMOND, JAMES RAMSAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., Univ. of Glasgow, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 27th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, March, 1890; dep. commr., Aug., 1890.

DRURY, ERNEST NEUFVILLE, Burma commn. (asst. commr.).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt. of police, 6th Jan., 1888; asst. commr., July, 1890.

DRURY, FRANCIS JAMES, B.A., M.B., Major, I.M.S., Bengal Med. Dept. (prof'r., Calcutta med. coll.).—Joined the Service April, 1885; in mily. employ till June, 1889; asst.-surgeon, Eden Hospital, Calcutta, April, 1891; resdt. physician and prof., Calcutta Med. Coll., Oct., 1893; ditto, and govt. bacteriologist, May, 1899.

DRURY, GEORGE MARSTON, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt., 30th Jan., 1878, and served chiefly on the E. Bengal state

railway; traffic supt., E. Coast railway, Nov., 1891; ret'd., Dec., 1892.

DRYSDALE, ALEXANDER TOVEY, late Forest Dept., Hyderabad Administration.—Arrived, Dec., 1864; from April, 1865, served in the Central Prov. as head master of govt. sch.; from June, 1868, served in the forest dept., Berar, as asst. conservator, dep. conservator, and conservator; ret'd., Jan., 1887.

DRYSDALE, THEODORE, late Central Prov. Commn.—Asst. master, govt. school, Saugor, April, 1861; served in the Central Prov. commn. from 19th Jan., 1863; apptd. extra asst. commr., April, 1864; supt. of the jud. commr.'s office, June, 1864; registrar, May, 1869; dep. supt. of census operations, Central Prov., April, 1880, and wrote the provl. report; judge, small cause court, Nagpur, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., April, 1885; cantonmt. mag., Kampti, 1885-89; divl. judge, June, 1895; ret'd., March, 1897.

DU BOULAY, JAMES HOUSSEMAYNE, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd assist., May, 1896; 1st asst., April, 1899.

DUCKWORTH, EDWARD DYCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

DUKE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Arbroath High Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892; mag. and collr., Dec., 1895.

DUKE, JOSHUA, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service, and Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was employed on mil. duty and as civil surgn. at Amritsar (1881-82) and in Kashmir (1884); served in the Afghan war, Marri expedn., and with the Gilgit mission; med. officer, Malwa Bhil corps and Bhopawar pol. agency, May, 1891; acted as residency surgn., Kashmir, 1889-91, 1895, and 1898; confd. Sept., 1900; ret'd., Nov., 1902; author of 'Recollections of the Kabul campaign,' and 'Banting in India.'

DUMBELL, PERCY HAROLD, B.A., India Office (junr. clerk, political dept.).—Educ. at Wolverhampton Gr. Sch. and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, rev and stat. dept., 9th Feb., 1903; transf. to mil. dept., May, 1903; transf. to pol. dept., Oct., 1903; reading clerk to council, Oct., 1903.

DUMERGUE, JOHN WILLOUGHBY FRANCIS, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. rest. in Travancore; Malayalam translr. to govt., March, 1884; head asst., Nilgiris, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1889; district and sessions judge, Madura, Aug., 1893; collr. and mag., Chingleput, Nov., 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Tinnevelly, Jan., 1899; ditto, Canara, Oct., 1899; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, Aug., 1902; ret'd.; April, 1903.

DUNCAN, DAVID, M.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Edinburgh), late Educn. Dept., Madras.—Served in the dept. from 20th May, 1870, as prof'r. of logic and moral philosophy at the presidency coll., and was registrar of the Madras univ., 1875-79 and 1881-86; fellow of the Madras univ., March, 1877; principal and prof'r., presidency coll., from March, 1884; dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1892; member of legisl. council, Oct., 1894; vice-chancellor of Madras univ., June-Nov., 1899; ret'd., Nov., 1899.

DUNDAS, CHARLES LAWRENCE, Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Eton, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 30th Dec., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; dist. judge, Dec., 1902.

DUNLOP, ALEXANDER JOHNSTON, C.I.E. (dep. commr., Hyderabad).—Apptd. asst. to cotton commr., Central Prov. and Berar, 1st May, 1869, and asst. commr., Feb., 1876; from 1869 to 1874 held charge in addn. to other duties of a govt. experimental farm; inspr.-gen., rev. dept., Nizam's govt., April, 1885, and survey and settlt. commr., Nizam's govt., April, 1887; rank of dep. commr., Hyderabad, July, 1889; K.-i-H. medal, May, 1900; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

DUNLOP, JOHN GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 16th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Feb., 1902.

DUNLOP-SMITH, JAMES ROBERT, C.I.E., M.A., Maj. Indian Army (pol. agent, Phulkian States).—Arrived, 24th Oct., 1879; from March, 1883, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Dec., 1883; asst. commr., and settlt. collr., April, 1888; dep. commr. April, 1895; dir. of land records and agriculture, Oct., 1897 to Oct., 1899; C.I.E., Jan., 1901; pol. agent, Phulkian States, Nov., 1901.

DUNN, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

DUNN, GEORGE OWEN WILLIAM, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1886; under sec. to govt., public works dept., May, 1894 to Jan., 1897.

DUNNETT, JAMES MACDONALD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Kilmarnock Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DUPERNEX, HUBERT EMILE LEONCE PAUL, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 31st Oct., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., May, 1897; joint mag., July, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1901; author of 'The Enhanced Excise Duties' and 'Tenancies in Oudh.'

DURAND, ALGERNON GEORGE ARNOLD, C.B., C.I.E., Col., I.S.C.—First commn., 21st Dec., 1872; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal

with clasp, and bronze star); A.D.C. to the vice-roy, 1881-82; on special duty on N.W. frontier, 1888; British agent, Gilgit, July, 1889; in 1891 commanded Hunza-Nagar field force (medal with clasp; C.B.); mil. sec. to viceroy, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., June, 1897; retired, Jan., 1901.

DURAND, SIR EDWARD LAW, Bart., C.B., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th Oct., 1865; arrived, June, 1866; served with the 96th regt., and was transfd. to Bengal staff corps, Oct., 1868; A.D.C. and private sec. to lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, June, 1870; from Feb., 1871, served in the foreign dept. in Rajputana and Central India; acting pol. agent, Manipur, Feb.-Oct., 1877; acting asst. rest., Bushire, April, 1878; in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul, March, 1881; asst. commr., Afghan boundary commn., Oct., 1885; rest., Nepal, May, 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1893; author of 'Report on Fars and Sketch Map of part of the Country,' and 'Report on Pearl Fisheries.'

DURAND, RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY MORTIMER, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., P.C., Barr. (1872), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 3rd March, 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1873; from June, 1874, served in the foreign dept. of the govt. of India, and was apptd. offg. 1st asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, July, 1876; offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., Nov., 1877; pol. sec. to major-gen. Sir F. Roberts with the Kabul field force, Sept., 1879 (medal and two clasps); under sec., foreign dept., March, 1880; C.S.I., Feb., 1881; offg. private sec. to the viceroy, June, 1880; sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Feb., 1885; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889; sent on a special mission to Afghanistan, Aug., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1894; envoy and minister plenipotentiary in Persia, May, 1894; ret'd. from the Indian civil service, Aug., 1897; apptd. ambassador extraordinary and plenipotentiary to the Court of Spain, 1900; G.C.M.G., May, 1900; sworn of the Privy Council, 1901; ambassador at Washington, Oct., 1903; has publ'd. a work on the first Afghan war, 1871, and a life of Sir H. M. Durand, 1883.

DURRANT, WILLIAM SCOTT, M.A., India Office (senior clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Educ. at Whitgift, and Lycée St. Omer; B.A., London (with honours in French), 1888; M.A. (Classics), 1891; junr. clerk, India office, 18th Sept., 1879; senr. clerk, Oct., 1897; assessor of income tax, 1903.

DUTHIE, JOHN FIRMINGER, B.A., F.L.S., late Indian Botanical Survey—Apptd. supt., botanical gardens, Saharanpur, Dec., 1876; director, botanical dept., Northern India, April, 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1903; author of 'Fodder Grasses of Northern India.'

DUTHOIT, WILLIAM, D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 17th Dec., 1857; served from Sept., 1858, in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and dep. supt., family domains, Maharaja of Benares; offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn. and commr. of excise and stamps, March, 1875; mag. and collr., Shahjahanpur, April, 1877; offg. puisne judge, high court, June, 1881; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1881; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, May, 1882;

employed on the opium commn., under govt. of Bengal, 1883; jud. commr. of Oudh, March, 1884; ret'd., Aug., 1886.

DUTT, A. C. (see Atul Chunder Dutt).

DUTT, CHARU CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 7th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

DUTT, PRATAP CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DUTT, ROMESH CHUNDER, C.I.E., Barr. (1871), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Colootola Branch Sch., Calcutta, Presidency Coll., Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 28th Sept., 1871; served in rev. and jud. depts., Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., March, 1888; acted as commr., Bardwan, 1894-95; C.I.E., May, 1892; member of provl. legisl. council, Jan.-Oct., 1895; has publ'd. 'Three Years in Europe,' 1872; 'Peasantry of Bengal,' 1876; 'Literature of Bengal,' 1877, a 'History of Civilisation in Ancient India,' 1888-89; a 'Brief History of Ancient and Modern India,' 1891; 'Lays of Ancient India,' 1894; &c., &c.; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

DUTT, RUSSICK LALL, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the civil dept. from 30th Nov., 1872, in acting appts., and as civil surgn. at various stations; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

DUVAL, HERBERT PHILIP, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dep. chairman of the corporation of Calcutta).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Caius College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 9th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. to supt. of stamps and stationery, Dec., 1900; dep. chairman, Calcutta municipality, June, 1902.

DWARKA NATH MITTER, B.L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service as Munsif, March, 1869; subordinate judge, Nov., 1882; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1902; ret'd., March, 1903.

DYMOTT, DONALD FREDERICK, M.B., Surgn.-Major, late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; asst. physician, gen. hosp., July, 1884; rest. physician, March, 1885; inspr. of vaccn., and dep. sanitary commr., June, 1888; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1893; acting med. and sanitary officer, Salem, Oct., 1894; offg. jail supt., Feb., 1895; acting surgn., 2nd district, Ma.; 1895; ret'd., May, 1897.

DYSON, HERBERT JEKYL, F.R.C.S., Major, Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Jessore).—First commr., 1st April, 1885; acted as civil surgn. in various stations from Jan., 1889; acting dep. sanitary commr., Punjab, Nov., 1889; confd., Oct., 1890; sanitary commr., Bengal, Aug., 1895; civil surgn., Jessore, Aug., 1902.

DYSON, JOHN, late Oudh Commn.—Joined the service, 8th June, 1858, as dep. mag. and dep. collr. in Bengal; from Dec., 1861, served in Oudh as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; small cause court judge, Lucknow, 1869; district judge, Faizabad, Aug., 1886; offg. commr., Sept., 1886; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, Aug., 1889; addl. jud. commr., July, 1890; ret'd., March, 1891.

DYSON, ROBERT CHARLES, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, March, 1890; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; under sec., rly. branch, Bombay, Oct., 1894; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, May, 1897.

EADES, FREDERICK TALBOT, India Office (junior clerk, Store Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Jan., 1875; staff clerk, July, 1896; junior clerk, March, 1901.

EALES, CHARLES LINDSTEDT MODELLIER, Barr. (1890), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 4th Dec., 1878, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1887; small cause court judge, March, 1893; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1893.

EALES, HERBERT LOVELY, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 30th Oct., 1879, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; dep. commr., May, 1883; offg. sec. to chief commr., April-May, 1890; supt. of provl. census, 1890-92; offd. as prest. Rangoon municipality, April-Dec., 1896; and again from April, 1897; commr., Aug., 1901.

EARDLEY-WILMOT, SAINT-HILL, India Forest Dept. (conservator of forests).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1873, as asst. conservator in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; dep. conservator, Nov., 1883; conservator, Oct., 1891; transferred to Burma, Jan., 1900; offg. inspr.-genl. of forests, Feb., 1903.

EARLE, ARCHDALE, Indian C.S. (commr. of excise and salt, Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. private sec. to the lieut.-govr., Feb., 1886; offg. under sec. to govt., jud., &c., depts., Feb., 1889; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1891; mag. and collr., Jan., 1895; K.-i.-H. medal, Jan., 1901; commr. of excise and salt, May, 1901.

EAST, WILLIAM ALBERT, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 26th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. registrar, dep. registrar and sealer of the high court, and asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of Panchganj, Nov., 1876; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Dharwar, June, 1881; senr. collr., June, 1885; ret'd., May, 1891.

EBDEN, EDWARD JAMES, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Sch., Ipswich, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 6th Dec., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Sept., 1872, served in the forest dept. as acting dep. conservator; on famine duty, Dharwar, 1877, and in Mysore, 1877-78; from July, 1878, served as asst. collr., railway mag., and forest settlt. officer; first asst., Aug., 1881; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1888; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1890; offg. commr., southern divn., Sept., 1895; commr., May, 1897; ret. Feb., 1900.

ECCLES, JOHN, M.A., Survey of India (supt.).—Joined the service as asst. supt. May, 1885; dep. supt., Dec., 1890; supt., May, 1898.

EDGE, SIR JOHN. Kt., K.C., B.A., LL.B., LL.D. (hon.), (member of the Council of India).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1866; Q.C., 1886; went the northern and north-eastern circuit, and was apptd. chief justice, N.W. Prov., Aug., 1886; vice-chancellor, Allahabad univ., 1887-93; apptd. member of the Council of India, March, 1898; member of royal commn. on S. African War, 1902.

EDGERLEY, STEYNING WILLIAM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., pol., judl. and legisl. depts., Bombay).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 20th Nov., 1879, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1881, served in acting appts. in Sind; 2nd asst. collr. in Sind, 1885 and 1886; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct.-Dec., 1888; private sec. to the govr. of Bombay, March, 1889, to July, 1895; C.I.E., May, 1895; jun. collr. and mag., July, 1896; sec. to govt., pol., jud., and legisl. depts., March, 1897; and acting chief sec., July, 1898, and April, 1902; addl. member of legisl. council, Bombay, June, 1902; joint compiler of a manual of village accts. for Sind, 1887, which he also transld. into Sindhi.

EDGINGTON, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst. Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1902.

EDMONSTONE-MONTGOMERIE, CHARLES WATERTON, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Uppingham and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 7th Dec., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; acting commr. of settlts. and agric., May, 1898; 2nd sec. to chief commr., April, 1899, to April, 1900.

EDWARDES, STEPHEN MEREDYTH.—Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. to collr. of land rev., May, 1898; acting under sec. rev. and finl. depts., Aug., 1898.

EDWARDS, ALFRED CLARKE, M.A., Barr. (1876), Bengal Educl. Dept. (principal, Presy. College).—Educ. at Haileybury and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd., 24th April, 1878; arrived, 28th Sept., 1878, and served as profr. in the presidency coll. and principal, Rajshahye coll.; principal, Dacca coll., Nov., 1887; principal, Patna coll., April, 1897; principal, Presidency Coll., Jan., 1900; regr. of Calcutta univ., 1899-1902.

EDWARDS, ARTHUR RICHARD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 29th Nov., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

EDWARDS, THOMAS CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. United Prov.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 29th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., April, 1899; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1899; asst. sec. to govt. of India,

foreign dept., Feb., 1901; offg. pol. agent, Harooti and Tonk, Nov. 1901; re-transfd. to United Prov., Feb., 1903.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM RICE, C.M.G., M.D., Major, Indian Medical Service (resdy. surgeon, Kashmir).—First commr., April, 1886; civil surgeon, Quetta, April, 1892; on Field Service, South Africa, 1900; C.M.G., Nov., 1900; resdy. surgeon, Kashmir, Nov., 1902.

EGERTON, CHARLES PHILIP, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Punjab).—Entered the service, Feb., 1884; in mil. employ till March, 1888; apptd. to the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., March, 1888; dep. commr., July, 1897; dist. judge, Sept., 1897; dep. commr., Jan., 1898.

EGERTON, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Lt. - Col., late I.S.C.—Joined the service, Aug., 1873; was in mil. employ till April, 1879; served in Punjab as asst. commr. from April, 1879; dep. commr., June, 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1901.

EGERTON, SIR ROBERT EYLES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Edinburgh Acad., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. from Haileybury, in 1850; served in the Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer, and was in charge of the Lahore settlt., 1854-58; dep. commr., 1856; addl. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1863; commr., Nagpur, Central Prov., March, 1864; offd. as jud. commr., Central Prov., at various times from 1865 to 1867; finl. commr. of the Punjab, March, 1868; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, 1871, and again in 1872 and 1877; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, April, 1877, to April, 1882, when he retired from the service.

EGERTON, ROBERT WALTER, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. as asst. engr., Sept., 1898, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1890; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., and under-sec. to govt. of Madras, railway branch, Nov., 1890.

EGERTON, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 5th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl., &c., depts., April, 1895; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1897; mag. and collr., May, 1901.

EGGAR, H. C. (solicitor to govt. of India).—Apptd. offg. solicitor to govt. of India, Dec., 1898; confirmed, March, 1900.

EICKE, FRANCIS WALTER, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Joined the dept., 22nd Sept., 1877, as acct.; asst. examr., July, 1881; dep. examr., July, 1887; examr., Nov., 1892.

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C., LL.D.—Born, 16th May, 1849; succeeded his father (viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 1862-63) in the earldom in 1863; educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A., 1877); received the hon. degree of LL.D. from the Univ. of St. Andrews in 1886; was treasurer of Her Majesty's household and first commr. of works in Mr. Gladstone's third administration (1886); sworn of the privy council, Feb., 1886; apptd. lord lieut. of Fife-shire, Aug., 1886; and is also a univ. commr. for Scotland; assumed office as viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 27th Jan., 1894; resigned, Jan., 1899; president of royal commn. on S. African war, 1902.

ELIOT, SIR JOHN, K.C.I.E., M.A., F.R.S.—Joined the service, June, 1869; from 27th Dec., 1869, to Feb., 1876, served in the N.W. Prov. as prof. at Thomason and Muir colls., supt. of the meteorological observatory, Allahabad, and meteorological reporter to govt.; from Feb., 1876 served in Bengal, as prof., presidency coll., and meteorological reporter; meteorological reporter to govt. of India, May, 1880; ditto, and dir.-gen. of Indian observatories, April, 1899; C.I.E., Jan., 1897; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903; ret'd., Jan., 1904.

ELIOTT, FRANCIS HARDINGE, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Oct., 1881; on military duty till July, 1888, when he joined the Burma commn. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1900.

ELLENOR, THOMAS RALPH, India Office (junr. clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, May, 1901; juir. clerk, March, 1903.

ELLERCAMP, HERBERT McDONALD, India Office (second-class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, Oct. 10th, 1870.

ELLES, SIR EDMOND ROCHE, K.C.B., K.C.I.E., R.A. (mil. member of govr.-gen.'s council).—First commn., R.A., Jan., 1867; served with the Lushai expdn., 1871-2 (medal); employed on Turkish boundary commn., 1879; served as dep. asst. qr.-mr.-gen. with the Indian contingent in Egypt, 1882 (medal); dep. asst. qr.-mr.-gen., Bengal, 1883-1885; asst. qr.-mr.-gen., Bengal, 1885-1889 and 1891-1893; served as asst. qr.-mr.-gen. with the Hazara expdn., 1888 (clasp); accompanied special mission to Cabul as 2nd in cmd., 1893; C.B., Dec., 1893; dep. qr.-mr.-gen., Bengal, 1893-1895; commanded Peshawar dist., 1895-1899; commanded Mohmand expdy. force, 1897-8; K.C.B., May, 1898; mil. member of govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1901; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1904.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS HUGH, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from June, 1873, to Nov., 1875, served in Berar as educt. inspr., and acting dir. of public instrn.; principal of the Raja's sch., and tutor and govr. of the Gaekwar of Baroda, Dec., 1875; C.I.E., May, 1883; on duty to England with the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1884; survey and settlt. officer, Baroda, March, 1890; ret'd., June, 1896.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK EDEN, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1858; arrived, 16th March, 1858; served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., April, 1877; district and sessions judge, March, 1881; ret'd., March, 1894.

ELLIOTT, ALGERNON, C.I.E., late Hyderabad Admn.—Educ. at Repton Sch.; joined the service, 16th March, 1869, as acting asst. district supt. of police, Central Prov.; transfd. to Berar as acting asst. commr., June, 1869; confd., April, 1874; dep. commr., July, 1889; civil and sess. judge, Oct., 1895; judicial commr., Feb., 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; ret'd., April, 1903.

ELLIOTT, SIR CHARLES ALFRED, K.C.S.I., LL.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 30th Nov., 1856, and served in the

N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag., and in Oudh as asst. commr.; from Feb., 1863, to Oct., 1866, served in the Central Prov. as settl. officer, Hoshangabad; served subsequently as settl. officer, Farakhabad, and as sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1870-77; commr., Meerut, Oct., 1877; famine commr., Mysore, Sept., 1877; addl. sec. to govt. of India, famine branch, May, 1878; sec. to famine commn., June, 1878; census commr., Oct., 1880; chief commr. of Assam, March, 1881; prest. of the public expenditure committee, Feb., 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; ordinary member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1887; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Dec., 1890, to Dec., 1895; member of London Sch. Board from 1896; author of 'Chronicles of Unao' and of the reports of the famine commn., 1879, and finance commn., 1887.

ELIOTT, CHARLES FREDERIC, late Punjab Forest Dept.—Joined the service, Jan., 1868; asst. conservator, Jan., 1869; dep. consr., July, 1880; conservator, Dec., 1896; ret'd., March, 1902.

ELIOTT, ROBERT ERNEST ALGERNON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent.

ELLIS, ALEXANDER, India Office (2nd class clerk, India store depôt).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 8th Dec., 1902.

ELLIS, ARTHUR STANLEY, India Office (supervisor, store dept.).—Apptd. asst. examr. of stores, India store depôt, 21st Aug., 1893; supervisor, 2nd grade, Sept., 1903.

ELLIS, LOVEYS EDWARD, India Office (second-class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1894.

ELLIS, THOMAS PETER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Oswestry High Sch. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 28th Feb., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ELLIS, WILLIAM MONTAGUE, Major, R.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First commn., 26th July, 1881; entered the dept. as asst. engr., July, 1884; exec. engr., April, 1895.

ELLISON, THOMAS EDWARD, late Bengal C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Coll., Bradford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 19th Oct., 1864; asst. commr., Delhi, Aug., 1865; transf. to Central Prov., Oct., 1868; judge, small cause court, Nagpur, Sept., 1883; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

ELSMIE, GEORGE ROBERT, C.S.I., Barr. (1871), late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, Oct., 1868; dep. commr., May, 1870; from 1871 to 1877 offd. as addl. commr.; civil and sessions judge, March, 1877; commr. and supt., Lahore, 1880-82, and 1885-87; judge, chief court, Punjab, 1882-85; 2nd finl. commr., May, 1887; also member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1888; 1st finl. commr., March, 1889; on special duty at Rawal Pindi durbar, March, 1885; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1893; author of 'Notes on Peshawur Crime,' and a biography of Sir Donald Stewart.

ELSTON, JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 16th Oct., 1871, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Sept., 1872; exec. engr., May, 1883; engr.-in-chief, Gauhati railway survey, March, 1898; ret'd., April, 1901.

ELWIN, EDGAR ALFRED, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 7th Nov., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Jaipur estate, July-Oct., 1890; senr. asst. collr., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1891; sub.-collr. and joint mag., June, 1896; collr. and mag., Dec., 1898.

ELWIN, ERNEST BARNES, Indian C.S. (sub.-collr., and joint mag.).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 5th Jan., 1891, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., Nov., 1896; head asst. collr. and mag., May, 1902; sub.-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1902.

EMANUEL, ALFRED EDWARD LEWIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 19th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag.

EMERSON, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., March, 1898; personal asst. to chief commr., May, 1900.

ENGLISH, ALEXANDER EMANUEL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ENTHOVEN, REGINALD EDWARD, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and inspr. of factories; 2nd asst., June, 1896; 1st asst. and under-sec. to govt., gen. dept., census, July, 1900 to Aug., 1902; author of 'Cotton Fabric of the Bombay Presidency.'

ERACHSHAH MANECKJI NANAVUTTY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Surat High Sch., Bombay Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 19th Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr.

ERAUT, STANLEY GORDON, India Office (2nd class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 19th March, 1902.

ERSKINE, CLAUDE FRANCIS, Survey of India (dep. supt.).—Joined the service, June, 1880; asst. supt., Dec., 1881; dep. supt., Jan., 1896.

ERSKINE, GEORGE ELPHINSTONE, Maj.-Gen., late Kumaun Commn.—Arrived, as cornet, Bombay light cavalry, 9th Dec., 1857; employed under the mil. dept., including service with the Rajputana field force, till Feb., 1863, when he joined the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; asst. settl. officer, May, 1865; personal asst. to chief commr., Oudh, July, 1871, to April, 1878; offg. sec. to chief commr., May to Aug., 1876; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. in forest branch, and

Oudh rev. dept., April, 1878; commr., Jan., 1882; on special duty to inquire into condition of tenantry in Oudh, 1882-83; wrote a report on that subject, and a digest of Oudh rev. circulars; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1895.

ERSKINE, KEITH DAVID, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (supt. of gazetteer, Rajputana).—1st commn., 6th Feb., 1884; pol. asst., May, 1889; pol. agent, May, 1897; in Haraoti and Tonk, Nov., 1900; resdt. in Western Rajputana States, April, 1901; on dept. in connection with revision of Imp. Gaz., Feb., 1903.

ESTCOURT, ERNEST ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 29th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ETESON, ALFRED, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Assam.—Joined the Indian med. dept. as asst. surgn., May, 1854; arrived, 30th June, 1854; held various mil. med. charges (medal for services during the Mutiny); sec. to surgn.-gen., Indian med. dept., March, 1879; dep. surgn.-gen., eastern frontier district, and sanitary commr., Assam, Jan., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1889.

EUAN-SMITH, SIR CHARLES BEAN, K.C.B., C.S.I., D.C.L., Col.—Ensign, Madras infantry, March., 1859; served in Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); sec. to Sir F. Goldsmid's mission to Persia, 1870-72; C.S.I., Nov., 1872; private sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, on special mission to Zanzibar and Muscat, 1872-73; on special duty in India office, 1873-74; pol. agent and consul-gen., Zanzibar, May, 1875; on special duty, foreign dept., on occasion of Prince of Wales's visit to Calcutta, 1875; first asst. rest., Haidarabad, March, 1876; pol. agent, and consul-gen., Muscat, July, 1879; during the Afghan war, 1879-80, was on special pol. duty on the staff of Lieut.-Gen. Sir D. Stewart, and subsequently with Gen. Hill's divn., and with the Kabul-Kandahar force under Gen. Roberts (medal with two clasps, and bronze star); acted as rest., Mewar, in 1882 and 1884; asst. pol. agent, Banswara, Dec., 1882; from May, 1883, served as addl. pol. agent, Bhartpur and Karauli; apptd. consul-gen. at Zanzibar, 1888; ret'd. from the army, March, 1889; K.C.B., Nov., 1890; envoy extraordinary and minister plenipotentiary at Tangiers, 1891-93; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1893.

EVANS, ARTHUR CLEMENT, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Jan., 1876, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); asst. engr., July, 1876; exec. engr., Nov., 1887.

EVANS, CHARLES THOMAS, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; from Aug., 1860, served in subord. offices; apptd. to the dept., Sept., 1869, as asst. engr., and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., June, 1876; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

EVANS, ERNEST SYDNEY, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 11th March, 1895.

EVANS, FREDERICK BUISSON, Indian C.S. (spcl. asst. settlt. officer, Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd.

after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlt. officer, March, 1901.

EVANS, GEORGE HENRY, Major, Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (supt., civil veterinary dept., Burma).—Joined the (home) service, 27th Aug., 1884; supt. civil veterinary dept., Burma, April, 1892; served in Chin-Lushai expedn., 1889-90; author of a book on Materia Medica in Burmese.

EVANS, HENRY FARRINGTON, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1880; special judge, Jhansi, July, 1882; joint mag., 1st grade, Oct., 1882; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1887; on special duty in Madras and Bombay, 1890-91, and as member of Deccan agriculturists relief commn., 1891-92; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, May-July, 1893; commr. and agent, Benares, Dec., 1893; commr., Agra, Nov., 1895; chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1896; junr. member, rev. board, Nov., 1898; C.S.I., June, 1899; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Dec., 1899; member legisl. council, N.W.P. and O., 9th Nov., 1900; ret'd., July, 1902; author of 'Farukhabad and Agra settlt. reports,' &c.

EVANS, LOFTUS GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Sept., 1886; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, March, 1891; district and sessions judge, April, 1891.

EVANS-GORDON, WILLIAM EDEN, Maj., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 15th July, 1876; from Feb., 1883, served under the foreign dept. with the Central India horse; boundary settlt. officer, Central India, 1883-84; attaché, foreign dept., Oct., 1885; also translr. of foreign European languages, Aug., 1887; asst. sec., Nov., 1887; pol. officer in charge of Sardar Ayub Khan, March, 1888; asst. rest., Kashmir, Nov., 1890; joint commr., Ladakh, March, 1889; pol. officer in attendance on the Gaekwar in Europe, 1894; offg. pol. agent, Jhaliawar, March, 1895; ret'd., May, 1897.

EVERETT, STEPHEN HENRY, India Office, (senr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; writer, India office, May, 1872; junr. examr., India audit office, Dec., 1873; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892; senr. clerk, Mar., 1897.

EVERS, BENJAMIN, M.D., C.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service, April, 1868; arrived, 27th Oct., 1868, served as civil surgn. and supt. of jail at various stations from April, 1876; ret'd., April, 1896.

EWBANK, ALFRED LLOYD VANDYKE, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 16th Jan., 1868; arrived, 28th Feb., 1868, and served as prof'r. Patna coll., and principal, Dacca coll.; prof'r., Patna coll., Oct. 1879; principal, Oct., 1881; ret'd., April, 1897.

EYRE, GERVAS SELWYN, Barr. (1890), Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., 12th June, 1869; served in mil. dept. until July, 1879, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in

Burma; on duty with the Burma field force, 1887; and with the force operating against the Chins, 1888; dep. commr., May, 1888; offg. inspr.-gen. of police, 1893, and from March, 1894, to Oct., 1895; commr., April, 1900; ret'd., Feb., 1902, royal humane society's medal for saving lives of two brother officers.

EZECHIEL, JOSEPH ALOYSIUS, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and sub-divl. officer; joint mag. and dep. coll., Jan., 1899.

FAGAN, CHRISTOPHER GEORGE FORBES, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (pol. agent, Alwar).—Educ. at Harrow: first commission, 63rd Foot, 11th Feb., 1875; joined the Bengal Staff Corps, Feb., 1879; served in the Afghan war of 1879-80 (medal); served in the commissariat dept., June, 1881, to June, 1882; squadron offr. 10th B. Lancers, June, 1882; asst. dist. supt. of police, Punjab, Sept., 1885; dist. supt., March, 1887; asst. rest. at Hyderabad, Nov., 1892; asst. pol. agent, Bussorah, Feb., 1897; asst. pol. agent, Maskat, April, 1898; pol. agent, Kota, Feb., 1900; pol. agent, Alwar, Nov., 1901.

FAGAN, PATRICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; settl. officer, Bikanir, 1892-93; dep. commr., Aug., 1896.

FAGAN, ROBERT SIDNEY FELTRUN, Bombay Forest Dept. (conservator).—Joined the service as junior asst. consr., 16th Oct. 1877; asst. consr., Jan. 1879; dep. consr., Dec. 1885; consr., Nov., 1902.

FAICHNIE, ALEXANDER GEORGE, late Postal Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1858; served for some time as supt. of the Rawal Pindi mil. horse van and bullock train; dep. postmr.-gen., Central Prov. and Berar, Nov., 1879; ret'd., Oct., 1894.

FAIRLEY, FRANCIS NOEL, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Store depôt).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 17th March, 1903.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Arrived, 20th March, 1856; in civil charge of Rawal Pindi and its jail, April, 1869; civil surgn. at Gurdaspur, April, 1874, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; offd. as sanitary commr., surgn.-gen., Punjab, and inspr.-gen. of prisons; also acted for various periods as med. adviser to the Maharaja of Patiala; ret'd., 1886; author of several papers on professional subjects.

FALLE, PHILIP JOHN, Comdr. R.I.M., late Bengal Marine Dept.—Entered the Royal Indian Marine, Jan., 1868; apptd. port officer, Calcutta, April, 1868; ret'd., May, 1901.

FANSHAW, SIR ARTHUR UPTON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (dir.-gen. of Post Office in India).—Educ. at Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, Nov., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and inspr.-gen. of registrn.; asst. sec. to the chief commr., July, 1880; in charge of model farm, Nagpur, 1880-81; from April, 1881, served in the postal dept.; postmr.-gen., Bombay, March, 1882; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Dec., 1888; on special duty under home dept., Jan.,

1889; dir.-gen. of the post office of India, May, 1889; member of the royal commn. on opium 1893-95; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

FANSHAW, HERBERT CHARLES, C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bedford, Tonbridge, and Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 6th Nov., 1873, and was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; offg. settl. officer, Rohtak, 1875-76 and 1877; asst. to the officer on special duty in Kashmir, June to Sept., 1879; junr. sec. to govt. Punjab, Nov., 1888; rev. sec., March, 1890; offd. for various periods as chief sec.; dep. commr., April, 1892; chief sec. to govt. Punjab, Sept., 1895, to Nov., 1892; commr. and supt., Jan., 1899; C.S.I., May, 1900; ret'd., May, 1901; author of the Rohtak settl. report, and 'Punjab Famine Code.'

FARDUNJI KUVARJI TARAPURVALLA, C.I.E., B.A., L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service 28th March, 1876, and served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1901; K.-i-H medal, May, 1900.

FARLEY, FRANK, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll. apptd. to the dept. 11th Oct., 1879, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1880; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

FARMER, HENRY RICHARD, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Loughborough, Bromsgrove, and Uppingham Gr. Schs.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 4th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1887; ret'd., June, 1896.

FARQUHAR, WILLIAM, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 28th May, 1858; arrived, 31st Dec., 1858, and served under the mil. dept. in Madras, Burma, and the Straits Settlements; also held civil med. charge, Tounghoo, in 1859; med. officer, Utakamand, Nov., 1882; dep. surgn.-gen., Belgaum and W. districts, July, 1885; ret'd., July, 1890.

FARRAN, GEORGE HOUGHTON, M.A., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Served from 17th May, 1866, as head asst. in the prothonotary's office, high court, Bombay; dep. registrar, commr. for taking affidavits, and judge's clerk, Nov., 1873; asst. commr. for taking accts. and asst. taxing master, July, 1876; master and registrar in equity, commr. for taking accts., and taxing officer, Sept., 1893; ret'd., Jan. 1899.

FARRANT, JAMES THOMAS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1877; and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., March, 1878; exec. engr., Dec., 1892.

FAULDER, CECIL JOSEPH SALKELD, Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived 9th Dec., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1887; mag. and collr., March, 1892; commr., Chittagong divn., Dec., 1902.

FAULKNER, ERNEST TREVELYAN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to Bengal; transf. to state rlys., Nov., 1886; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

FAUNTHORPE, JOHN CHAMPION, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 26th Oct., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1897; under sec. to govt., Jan. to April, 1899; dep. commr., Nov., 1902.

FAWCETT, CHARLES GORDON HILL, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 9th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. and asst. judge, Feb., 1897; under-sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1898; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, March, 1899, and April, 1901; 1st asst., Nov., 1901.

FAWCETT, EDWARD PINDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Dean Close Sch., Cheltenham, Ley's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1897, arrived 7th Dec., 1898; and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

FAYRER, SIR JOSEPH, Bart., K.C.S.I., LL.D., M.D., F.R.S., F.R.C.P., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen.—Acting asst. surgn., R.N., Aug., 1847; asst. surgn., royal artillery, 1849; joined the East India Company's service, April, 1850, and served under the mil. dept., also holding civil surgery of Cherra Punji, 1850; served in the Burma campaign, and ofld. as civil surgn., Rangoon, 1853; apptd. residency surgn., Lucknow, by Lord Dalhousie, for services during war in Burma; hon. asst. rest. at Lucknow, Sept., 1854; civil surgn., Lucknow, March, 1856; brevet rank of surgn. for services in defence of Lucknow residency during the siege, Sept., 1858; profir. of surgery and surgn. of med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1860; med. charge of Mysore princes, June, 1860; fellow of univ. of Calcutta, March, 1861, and served for two years as prest. of the med. faculty; prest. of the Asiatic Society of Bengal, 1867; offg. surgn. to viceroy, 1869; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Edinburgh in his tour through India, 1870; hon. physician to the Queen, July, 1871; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to India, 1875-76, in med. charge of his party; ret'd., 1874; prest. of med. board, India office, 1874-95; also member of army sanitary commn., and of senate of army med. sch. at Netley; bart., Jan., 1896; author of 'Clinical Surgery in India'; 'Clinical and Pathological Observations in India'; 'Thanatophidia of India', and other works; retired from India Office Medical Board, Jan., 1895.

FELL, GODFREY BUTLER HUNTER, Indian C.S. (private sec. to lieut.-govr., Burma).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., April, 1897; private sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1897; acting asst. sec., March, 1899; confid., Oct., 1899; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1900; private sec. to lieut.-govr., April, 1903.

FENNER, HENRY ALGERNON SHRAPNEL, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 20th June, 1861; exec. engr., Sept., 1863; suptg. engr., March, 1887; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt. of the Punjab, Jan.-Feb., 1892; ret'd., May, 1893.

FENTON, LAYARD LIVINGSTONE, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Bombay Pcl. Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1870, and after serving under the mil. dept. was transf. to the rev. survey dept. as asst. supt., Nov., 1873; asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, March, 1880; supt. of surveys under the rajasthanik court, Dec., 1890; prest. rajasthanik court, April, 1896; 1st asst. Kathiawar, April, 1899; ret'd. Jan., 1902.

FENTON, MICHAEL WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 18th Nov., 1883, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., under sec. to govt., Oct., 1887, to Nov., 1891; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., July, 1892; rev. sec. to govt., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1899; author of 'Punjab Municipal Manual.'

FENTON, THOMAS CHARLES, India Office (staff clerk, registry and record dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd May, 1873; attached to the store dept. till Aug., 1879, and subsequently to the registry dept.; called to the Bar (Middle Temple), 17th Nov., 1898; staff clerk, Oct., 1901.

FERARD, HENRY CECIL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Eton, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 17th Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., April, 1893; dep. commr., July, 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; mag. and collr., July, 1898.

FERARD, JOHN EDWARD, India Office (senior clerk, military dept.).—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; upper divn. clerk, local govt. board, 2nd Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India office, 7th April, 1893; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Feb., 1894; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, July, 1898, to Feb., 1901; senior clerk, pub. works dept., March, 1901; transf. to mil. dept., Oct., 1903.

FERGUSSON, Rt. HON. SIR JAMES, Bart., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., M.P.—Born in 1832; educ. at Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; entered the grenadier guards, and served in the Crimean campaign, 1854-55 (medal and three clasps); ret'd. from the army in 1855; M.P. for Ayrshire, 1854-57, and 1859-68, and for N.E. Manchester, from 1895; under sec. of state for India, June, 1866; under sec. for the home dept., July, 1867; govr. of S.
