HEMINGWAY, FREDERICK RICKETTS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HEMINGWAY, HENRY EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.

HEMINGWAY, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Oundle, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 14th Dec., 1896, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.

HENDERSON, CLAUDE GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 24th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

HENDERSON, EDWARD PIERCY, Barr. (1871), late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 10th Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; govt. advocate, Feb., 1878; rettd., Sept., 1897.

HENDERSON, GEORGE, M.D., Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1859; civil asst. surgn., Shahpur, May, 1862; supt. of Lahore central jail, April, 1868; offg. supt. of botanical gardens in Bengal, Dec., 1872; civil surgn., Gujranwala, Dec., 1875, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; rettd., Nov., 1889.

HENDERSON, GILBERT STUART, Barr. (1876) (judge, High Court, Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, High Court, Calcutta, 13th June, 1902.

HENDERSON, HARRY JOSEPH HUME, late Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service, May, 1872, as asst. supt. of police; supt., Nov., 1878; dist. supt., Dec., 1893; ret'd., May, 1903.

HENDERSON, JAMES PERCY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; appt'd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1891.

HENDERSON, JOHN JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (special engr.).—Appt'd. from S. Punjab and Delhi Rly. Co., as special asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1886, and posted to N.W. Rly.

HENDERSON, PHILIP DURHAM, C.S.I., Maj.-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras cavalry, Feb., 1857; attaché, foreign dept., May, 1871; under sec. to the govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1872; on special duty in Kashmir, 1874; pol. officer on staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Sept., 1875; C.S.I., March, 1876; gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, March, 1878; rest. in Mysore, and chief commr., Coorg, April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Feb., 1895.

HENDERSON, WILLIAM GEORGE HUME, F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Indian M.S. (civil surgn., Poona).—Joined the service, 21st Nov., 1876; in mil. employ till April, 1878, since when has held several civil surgeoncies; was on field service in Upper Burma, 1886-88; presy. surgn., April, 1901; civil surgn., Poona, March, 1902.

HENDERSON, WILLIAM SCOTT, India Office, (2nd class clerk, India Store Depôt).—Appt'd. clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1900.

HENDLEY, THOMAS HOLBEIN, C.I.E., Col., Indian Med. Service, late Bengal Med. Dept.—First commn., Oct., 1869; appt'd. inspr.-gen. of civil hosp's., Calcutta, April, 1898; ret'd., April, 1903.

HENNESSY, SEPTIMUS HOPE, late Jud. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service as acting extra asst. commr., 13th July, 1864, and was confd., 8th July, 1867; judge, small cause court, Sept., 1889; acted also as cantonmt. mag. from Nov., 1888; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

HENNIKER, FREDERICK CHANDOS, Indian C.S. (dir. of land records and agric., Assam).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appt'd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1897; dir. of land records and agriculture, Dec., 1898.

HENRIQUES, QUENTIN QUIXANO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; appt'd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HENRY, EDWARD RICHARD, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (commr. of metrop. police, London).—Educ. at St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, and Univ. Coll., London; appt'd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, June, 1882; on famine duty, Tirhut, 1873; private sec. to lieut.-govr. in 1879 and 1880; mag. and collr., May, 1890; offg. sec. to board of rev., 1890-91; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1892; C.S.I., May, 1898; commr., Mar., 1900; ret'd., March, 1901; asst. commr. of metrop. police, London, 1901; commr. of ditto, March, 1903.

HENSLLEY, JOHN WILLIAM, India Telegraph Dept. (supt.).—Appt'd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879;

supt., April, 1888; served with Chin-Lushai expdn., 1883-03 (medal and clasp).

HENSLLOWE, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1866, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, and in the Central Prov.; transfid. to railway establ., Jan., 1880; engr.-in-chief and manager, Cawnpore-Farukhabad and Muttra-Hathras railways, Dec., 1880; manager, Cawnpore-Achnera state railway, 1884; transfid. to Burma state railway, Dec., 1886; ret'd., 1890.

HENSLLOWE, FRANCIS JOHN DELORAIN, Capt., Indian Army.—First commn., Aug., 1888; joined the staff corps, Oct., 1889; appt'd. comdt., Zhob levy corps, May to Aug., 1900.

HENVEY, FREDERICK, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby Sch.; appt'd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., under sec. to govt., offg. sec. to board of rev., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on special duty in 1871 for purpose of compiling a narrative of the famine of 1869-70; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., July, 1874; offg. rest., Nepal, 1877-78; on special duty, Kashmir, April, 1878; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, Jan., 1883; rest., E. Rajputana states, Dec., 1885; agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

HERBERT, CHARLES, Lt.-Col., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt. Gwalior).—First commn., June, 1874; served under the mil. dept. to 1882, including Afghan war of 1879-80; extra A.D.C. to viceroy, 1878 and 1882; offd. as asst. to the govr.-gen., Rajputana, June, 1882; mag. of Abu, Nov., 1882; on depn. to Mewar states to settle boundary disputes, 1883; offg. cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, 1884 and 1885-86; asst. commr., Ajmir, April, 1889; asst. to the agent, Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmir-Merwara, March, 1891; offg. pol. agent in E. states of Rajputana, 1891-92; 1st asst. to agent, Central India, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Kotah, March, 1894; ditto, Eastern States of Rajputana, 1900; resdt., Gwalior, Feb., 1901.

HERBERT, DAVID WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; appt'd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1880; exec. engr. Dec., 1893; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1901; sany. engr., Poona, March, 1902.

HERBERT, DOUGLAS, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Tunbridge Sch.; 1st commn., 25th Nov., 1885; joined the Assam commn., March, 1891, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1899.

HERBERT, HERBERT, F.R.C.S., Major, I.M.S. (prof., Grant Med. Coll., Bombay).—1st commn., 31st March, 1887; on mil. duty till Dec., 1892; civil surgn., Kaira, April, 1893; ophthalmic surgn., J. J. Hospital, and prof., Grant Med. Coll., June, 1897.

HERBERT, PATRICK HERBERT COGHLAN, India Office (senr. clerk, Jud. and Public Dept.).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; junr. clerk, accts. branch, 11th March, 1872; jud. and public dept., 1877; rest. clerk, 1885-88; senr. clerk, May, 1891; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state (Mr. G. W. E. Russell, M.P.), 1892-94; editor of India Office List, 1887-91; compiled jointly with Mr. Newmarch the India office cypher code.

HERRON, HERBERT GEORGE WHITBY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 27th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Burma as asst. commr., Nov., 1888, and offg. under sec.; returned to Bengal, Nov., 1890; joint mag. and collr., Nov., 1896; ret'd., Jan., 1904.

HERSCHEL, JOHN, F.R.S., Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1855; first commn., Dec., 1856; arrived at Calcutta, Jan., 1859; served in the great trigonol. survey from Nov., 1859, in the grades of asst. surveyor and dep. supt.; ret'd., March, 1886.

HERVEY, HAMILTON LAW, Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Giggleswick Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 27th March, 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., May, 1894; 1st asst., April, 1897; judge and sessions judge, July, 1897.

HEWAT, RICHARD ALEXANDER, late India Office.—East India House writer, old estabt., 18th Aug., 1858; junr. clerk, mil. dept., Aug., 1860; senr. clerk, Jan., 1875; asst. sec., judl. and public dept., Feb., 1894; ret'd., March, 1896.

HEWETSON, JOHN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Repton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in Burma, from March, 1886, as asst. commr., judge, civil court, Mandalay, and district officer; returned to Madras, Oct., 1888; senr. asst., Ganjam, July, 1889; head asst., Aug., 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1894; district and sessions judge, May, 1897; collr. and mag., S. Canara, Aug., 1898; dist. and sess. judge, Tinnevelly, Oct., 1899.

HEWETT, JOHN PRESCOTT, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (chief commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; in charge of provl. gazetteer, Aug., 1883; acted as asst. acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1884; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1884; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1886; confd., Aug., 1887; offd. as private sec. to the viceroy in 1888 and 1892, and as sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1890; offg. census commr. for India, July, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1891; dep. commr., March, 1893; sec. of royal commn. on opium, Oct., 1893; mag. and collr., April, 1894; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1895; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; member of plague commn., Nov., 1898; offg. chief commr., Central Provs., Sept., 1902; confd., Nov., 1903; author of contributions to provl. gazetteer.

HEWETT, WILLIAM SELWOOD, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Jud. Dept. (cantonmt. mag., Rawalpindi).—First commn., 30th Dec., 1871; arrived, 13th March, 1872, and was employed on mil. duty, including service in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); cantonmt. mag., March, 1887.

HEWISH, JOHN, India Office (dep. acctnt.-gen.).—Junr. clerk, 1st Feb., 1869; senr. clerk, Sept., 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen. and assessor of income tax, May, 1900.

HEWITT, JAMES FRANCIS KATHERINUS, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1858; arrived, 12th Feb., 1859, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; dep. commr., wards' estate in Behar, Feb., 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., Sept., 1874; mag. and collr., Oct., 1878; commr., Chota Nagpur, March, 1879; ret'd., July, 1885; author of 'The Ruling Races of Pre-Historic Times in India,' &c.

HEXT, REAR-ADMIRAL SIR JOHN, A.D.C., K.C.I.E., R.N. (Retd.)—Apptd. Director of the Royal Indian Marine, 26th Feb., 1883; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., Feb., 1898.

HEYCOCK, WILLIAM BISSILL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Oakham Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HEYWOOD, JOHN MAGEE, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1854; 1st commn., Bengal engrs., June, 1856; served in the Bengal public works dept. from April, 1858, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., May, 1881; offg. chief engr., and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., 1883-84; chief engr. and joint sec., irrign. branch, Punjab, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1889.

HEZLETT, JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Coleraine Academy, Queen's Coll., Galway, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HICKIE, WILLIAM CORNELIUS, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Apptd. to the dept. as acctnt., 14th Sept., 1878; asst. examr., Oct., 1882; dep. examr., May, 1886; examr., Aug., 1894.

HIGGINS, ALFRED WILLIAM BUCKLE, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Baileybury; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 6th Dec., 1876, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; sec. to the draft forest bill committee, 1882; dep. conservator of forests, Cuddapah, Dec., 1882; also forest settl. officer, July, 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1890; principal asst. to collr., Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1892; member, rev. board, and dir. of land records and agric., Dec., 1898; addl. member of legisl. council, Madras, Aug., 1901; member, rev. board, and commr. of rev. settlt., April, 1902; ret'd., May, 1903.

HIGGINS, ANDREW FRANK, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Oct., 1873, and posted to Hyderabad; exec. engr., May, 1881; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; suptg. engr., Feb., 1901.

HIGGINS, JOHN JAMES, late Police Dept., Central Prov.—Apptd. acting adjutant, mil. police, Narsinghpur, for services rendered during the Mutiny under Sir Henry Havelock, Dec., 1858; from March, 1861, served in the police dept. of the Central Prov., and was promoted to district supt., 1st class, June, 1879; ret'd., March, 1894.

HIGHAM, SIR THOMAS, K.C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., July, 1867; arrived, Oct. 1867, and served in the irrign. dept., Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; asst. sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Nov., 1882; supt. of works, Derajat, Sept.,

1883; suptg. engr., Aug., 1886; offd. as chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., Punjab, July-Oct., 1891; chief engr. and sec., irrig. branch, Oct., 1894; on special famine relief duty, May to July, 1897; insp.-gen. of irrigation and joint sec. to govt. of India, Nov., 1897; author of 'Hydraulic Tables'; C.I.E., May, 1896; K.C.I.E., June, 1902; ret'd., April, 1903.

HIGHT, ARTHUR EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Nov., 1878; exec. engr., March, 1890; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

HIGNELL, SIDNEY ROBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HILDEBRAND, ARTHUR HEDDING, C.I.E., late Burma Commn.—From Feb., 1868, served in the Burma police dept. as asst. supt.; from March, 1872, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and was sent on special mission to Chiengmai, 1875, and in charge of expedn. to W. Karenni, 1876; dep. commr., Jan., 1885; supt., S. Shan states, May, 1888; C.I.E., May, 1888; supt. and pol. officer, S. Shan states, Aug., 1891; ret'd., March, 1902.

HILDEBRAND, GODFREY, Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Oct., 1864; arrived, Jan., 1868; joined the public works dept., March, 1869, and served in Bengal and the N.W. Prov.; transf. to mil. works, May, 1872; sec. to defence committee, 1881; suptg. engr., March, 1886; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, 1890-91; asst. insp.-gen. of fortifications, War Office, July, 1894; dep. inspr.-gen., of fortifications, Nov., 1897; ret'd., June, 1902.

HILL, ARTHUR, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1880; exec. engr., June, 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

HILL, ARTHUR PHILIP, India Telegraph Dept. (superintendent).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1880; supt., March, 1897; has received India medal, Burma, and two clasps.

HILL, CHARLES HENRY, Barr. (1872) (judge, Calcutta high court).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1889, as public prosecutor to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh; judge, Calcutta high court, Jan., 1892.

HILL, CLAUDE HAMILTON ARCHER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. sec., foreign dept., govt. of India).—Educ. at St. Mark's Sch., Windsor, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec., pol., &c., depts., and sec. to legisl. council, Nov., 1892; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Aug., 1895; 1st asst., Nov., 1897; acting pol. agent, Hyderabad, Nov., 1897; private sec. to govt. of Bombay, Feb., 1900; dep. sec., foreign dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1903; C.I.E., Jan., 1904.

HILL, FREDERICK GEORGE, India Office (2nd class clerk, Funds Dept.).—Educ. at City of London Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 6th July, 1882.

HILL, FREDERICK JUSTLEY ARNOLD, Salt Dept., Bombay (asst. collr.).—Educ. at King's Sch., Sherborne; joined the service, 20th June, 1881, as superny. asst. collr.; asst. collr. of salt rev., June, 1883.

HILL, HENRY, late India Office.—Educ. at the Edinburgh Acad. and Edinburgh Univ.; India office writer, old establ., 1859; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 1860; asst. private sec. and précis writer to the sec. of state, 1867-74; sent. clerk, Jan., 1872; rev., statistics and commerce dept., 1874; asst. sec. in that dept., Sept., 1877; asst. in public works dept., and asst. govt. dir. of guaranteed Indian railway cos., June, 1892; reverted to rev. and statistics dept. as asst. sec., July, 1898; ret'd., April, 1900.

HILL, JOHN, Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Lieut., royal engrs., June, 1862; arrived, 10th Dec., 1865; apptd. to the trigonl. survey as asst. surveyor, Dec., 1866, and employed in Kumaon and Garhwal, Bombay, &c.; dep. supt., July, 1882; attached to Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; in charge of tidal and levelling operations, Poona, Nov., 1890; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1895; ret'd., March, 1896.

HILL, ROWLAND, Stationery Dept., Madras (supt. govt. press, Madras).—Dept. supt., govt. press, Oct., 1874; supt., Sept., 1886.

HILL, SAMUEL CHARLES, B.A., B.Sc., Educ. Dept., Bengal (in charge of records of govt. of India).—Apptd. Profr., Dacca Coll., June, 1881; principal, Krishnagar Coll., Mar., 1888; profr., Hooghly Coll., Nov., 1891; profr., Presy. Coll., Mar., 1897; insp. of schools, Behar circle, April, 1897; ditto, Presy. circle, Feb., 1899; in charge of records of Govt. of India, and asst. sec., Home dept., Nov., 1900.

HILL, THOMAS COUTTS, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 1st Aug., 1857; supt., British Burma, Feb., 1879; employed in connection with occupation of Upper Burma, 1886; chiefsupt., Sept., 1892; ret'd., March, 1894.

HILL, WILLIAM, Maj.-Gen., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras infantry, Aug., 1853; served in the Mysore commn. from July, 1859; dep. supt., Nov., 1862; dep. commr., April, 1873; acted for various periods as commr. of divn.; acting commr. of Coorg, 1881; from March, 1885, served as civil and sessions judge, Bangalore; returned to Europe, 1891.

HILTON, JOHN EDWARD, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 8th Oct., 1868, and posted to the Punjab; transf. to mil. works dept., May, 1872; exec. engr., Aug., 1877; transf. to Punjab provl. establ., Nov., 1880; under sec. to govt., Sept., 1892; suptg. engr., May, 1893; ret'd., July, 1899.

HIRTZEL, FREDERIC ARTHUR, M.A., India Office (private sec. to sec. of state).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford (Craven scholar); fellow of Brasenose, 1895; apptd. junr. clerk, 11th June, 1894; rest. clerk, Mar. 1897; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, July, 1898; ditto to permanent under sec. of state, Mar., 1901; senior clerk, mil. dept., July, 1902; private sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1903.

HITCHCOCK, JOHN (inspr. of machinery, India Store Depot).—Apptd. asst. examr. of stores, July, 1882; examr., Nov., 1896; supervisor, 2nd grade, Dec., 1898; inspr. of machinery, Sept., 1903.

HITTU RAM, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1859, and held non-gazetted appts. till Feb., 1877, when he was apptd. native asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan; on special

duty with Afghan boundary commn., 1884; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1890; on special duty under Kalat state, May, 1893; author of a Baluchi manual, and a history of Dera Ghazi Khan.

HLA OUNG, MAUNG, Accts. Dept. (off. comptr., India Treasuries).—Entered the service, 1872, and served in the finance dept.; asst. comptr., Nov., 1880; asst. acct.-gen. for Burma, April, 1893; asst. comp.-gen., Calcutta, Nov., 1898; dep. acct.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1899; offg. dep. auditor.-gen., April, 1902; offg. comptr., India Treasuries, Sept., 1902; has published a revision of 'Gordon's Burma manual.'

HOARE, HERBERT JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at private schs. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1895; settl. officer, Nov., 1896; dep. commr., Dec., 1901.

HOBART, ROBERT THOMPSON, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov., as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Dec., 1873; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Oct., 1882; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1889.

HOBART-HAMPDEN, AWDRY GEORGE, India Forest Dept. (conservator).—Joined the service as asst. consr. and Jan., 1882; dep. consr., Feb., 1891; consr., Dec., 1902; director, forest school, Dehra Dun, April, 1903.

HOBDAV, JAMES RAMSAY, Col., Indian Army, Survey of India Dept. (dep. surveyor-genl.).—First commn., 13th Jan., 1869; proby. asst. supt., survey dept., Sept., 1874; confd., July, 1875; served in the Afghan war, 1879, and with Upper Burma field force, 1886; dep. supt., Jan., 1887; supt., Dec., 1895; dep. surveyor-genl., Sept., 1898; accompanied special expedn. in Upper Burma along the Chinese frontier, 1890-91.

HOBHOUSE (BARON), RIGHT HON. ARTHUR HOBHOUSE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., K.C.—Born 10th Nov., 1819; educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, where he gained a first-class in classics in 1840; called to the bar, 1845, and practised in the Rolls court; Q.C. in 1865; apptd. a charity commr. in 1866; endowed schools commr., 1869; legal member of council of the govr.-gen. of India, 1872-77; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1877; arbitrator under the Epping Forest Act, 1878; privy councillor, and member of the jud. committee, 1881; created a baron in 1885; one of the quorum of the House of Lords for jud. purposes; author of 'The Dead Hand,' a collection of addresses connected with endowments and the settlements of land, 1880; contested Westminster unsuccessfully in the general election of 1880.

HOBHOUSE, SIR CHARLES PARRY, Bart., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 22nd July, 1844, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in the finl. dept., govt. of India, as asst. to sub-treasurer, and asst. to acctnt.-gen.; collr. of Bardwan, 1857-59; mag. and collr., 1850; on special duty in 1860 to adjudicate on suits arising out of the indigo disturbances, Nuddea and Jessor; supt. and rememberer of legal affairs, and govt. advocate, 1862; also member of Bengal legisl. council, 1863-64; civil and sessions judge, 1864-66; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1866; chairman of the Orissa

famine fund, 1867; judge of the high court, 1868-71; ret'd., 1872.

HODGES, ROBERT NATHANIEL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th Oct., 1873, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transf. to state railways, Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Sept., 1880; employed as engr.-in-chief on several railway surveys, including Saugor-Katni scheme; suptg. engr., 1898.

HODGSON, GODFREY BECKETT, Major, Indian Army, Survey of India (supt.).—First commn., 1st May, 1878; served in Afghan war, 1880; apptd. proby. asst. supt., 13th Sept., 1883; confd., July, 1884; dep. supt., May, 1889; supt., June, 1897.

HODSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (dir. of railway construction).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., April, 1883; supt., way and works, N.W. railway, Nov., 1891; engr.-in-chief, Mushkaf-Bolan railway, July, 1894; services lent to Ceylon Govt., June, 1896; suptg. engr., Aug., 1896; dir. of railway construction and dep. sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Jan., 1898.

HODSON, RICHARD GEORGE, late Educn. Dept., Burma.—Joined the dept. as dep. inspr. of schs., Mysore, April, 1861; inspr., April, 1867; acting dir. of public instrn., 1879-80; transf. to Burma as acting principal, high sch., Rangoon, April, 1880; from Oct., 1881, served as inspr. of schs. in Burma, and offd. as dir. of public instrn.; ret'd., Feb., 1893; has transld. Munshi Srinivasaya's 'Dialogues in Kanarese.'

HOERNLE, AUGUSTUS FREDERICK RUDOLF, C.I.E., M.A., Ph.D., late Educn. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. principal, Calcutta Madrassa, Feb., 1884; member of board of examrs., Nov., 1896; C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., Mar., 1899; has written a grammar of the Gandian languages; dictionary of the Bihari language, and other philological works.

HOEY, WILLIAM, M.A., D.Lit., late Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at the Wesleyan Connexional Sch., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 18th Oct., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag., and joint mag.; employed on archaeo-logical explorations in Gondia in 1884; dep. commr., Feb., 1890; mag. and collr., Aug., 1890; offg. commr., Oct., 1897; author of 'Trades and Manufactures in N. India,' 'A History of Asaf-ud-Daulah, Nawab Wazir of Oudh,' 'Buddha; His Life, His Doctrine, and His Order,' 'Memoirs of Delhi and Fyzabad,' &c., &c.; ret'd. July, 1900.

HOGAN, JOHN LEMON POICTEVIN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Nov., 1873, and posted to mil. works; transf. to Mysore, Sept., 1877; to Central Prov., Sept., 1878; and to state railways, Jan., 1880; services lent to Bombay, Baroda and Central India railway co., Jan., 1885; exec. engr., Feb., 1885; suptg. engr., May, 1899; junr. consg. engr. to govt. of India, for rlys., Calcutta, May to Dec., 1901.

HOGG, ADAM GEORGE FORBES, C.B., Lieut.-Gen., Indian Army, late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1854; served with the Persian expedn., 1857 (medal and

clasp), in the Mutiny, the China war, 1860, the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal), and the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and clasp); apptd. pol. rest. and collr. of stamp rev., with charge of the vice-admiralty court, Aden, May, 1885; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

HOGG, SIR FREDERIC RUSSELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 12th Feb., 1857, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., Punjab and Sind, July, 1863; postmr.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1868; representative of the govt. of India at the postal congress, Paris, 1878; dir.-gen. of post office of India, March, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; K.C.I.E., May, 1888; ret'd., April, 1889.

HOGG, STAPLETON COTTON, late Finl. Dept., India Office.—Temporary clerk, 25th July, 1861; junr. clerk, accts. dept., 1862; transfd. to finl. dept., 1863; senr. clerk, Dec., 1875; asst. sec., Sept., 1895; ret'd., Oct., 1901.

HOGG, SIR STUART S., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1853; arrived, Jan., 1853; served in the Punjab as asst. commr. till 1859, and afterwards in Oudh as dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Bardwan, 1861; chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, 1866; commr. of police, Calcutta, and member of Bengal legisl. council, 1874; ret'd., 1878.

HOGG, THEODORE WILLIAM, Col., Indian Army, late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., 4th Dec., 1857; arrived, 28th Jan., 1858; and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. from Aug., 1867; small cause court judge, March, 1883; dep. commr., Aug., 1889; on U.S. list, July, 1898.

HOLDERNESS, THOMAS WILLIAM, C.S.I., B.A., India Office (sec., rev. and statistics dept.)—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 17th Feb., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. sec. to govt., and junr. sec., board of rev.; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Sept., 1881; joint mag., N.W. Prov., April, 1883; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1886; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1888; sec. to govt., finl. dept., March, 1892, at the same time acting as sec. in the jud. dept.; member of legisl. council, Aug., 1892; mag. and collr., Aug., 1893; sec. to govt., judl. dept., July, 1894; famine duty, home dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1897, and sec. to govt., rev. and agric. dept., July, 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; retired from Indian C.S., May, 1901; apptd. sec., rev. and statistics dept., India Office, April, 1901.

HOLDICH, SIR THOMAS HUNGERFORD, K.C.M.G., K.C.I.E., C.B., Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—Lieut., royal engrs., Dec., 1862; arrived, 3rd March, 1865, and served in the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (medal and clasp); joined the topogr. survey, July, 1866; on duty with the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); joined the Afghanistan field force in 1879, and was in charge of survey operations in N. Afghanistan during second period of the campaign, and asst. field engr. during defence of Sherpur (medal and clasp); in charge of Kohat topogr. survey, Feb., 1881; dep. supt., April, 1883; in charge of Baluchistan topogr. survey, Oct., 1883; attached to the Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; survey officer

with the Zhob field force in 1890; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; C.B. and C.I.E., May, 1894; on special duty with Khaibar-Asmar delimitation commn., 1894-95; in charge, Pamir detachment, June, 1895; temporary appt. to Ceylon, Sept., 1896; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; chief survey officer with Tirah expedy. force, Aug., 1897; ret'd., Feb., 1900; member of Chile-Argentina Boundary Tribunal, 1902; K.C.M.G., Dec., 1902; author of articles on the geography of Afghanistan and the Indian frontier, and on mil. instrn., topography, &c.

HOLIDAY, FREDERICK CHARLES (auditor, India Audit Office)—Temporary clerk, 17th June, 1861; junr. asst., June, 1863; senr. examr., Dec., 1868; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892; asst. auditor, Jan., 1895; auditor, Feb., 1903; member of post office committee on conveyance of mails to the East, May, 1903.

HOLLAND, GERALD EDWARD, C.I.E., D.S.O., Commander Royal Indian Marine (port officer, Rangoon)—Joined the Indian Marine, July, 1880; promoted commander, April, 1893; marine transport officer to the South African Force, Sept., 1899; port officer, Rangoon, May, 1901; C.I.E., July, 1901.

HOLLAND, ROBERT ERSKINE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras)—Educ. at Winchester and Oriel Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 10th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transfd. to Bombay, July, 1900; 1st asst., March, 1901; retransfd. to Madras, Nov., 1902.

HOLLAND, THOMAS HENRY, F.G.S., Geol. survey of India (director)—Joined the service 10th July, 1890, as asst. supt. and curator, geol. museum and laboratory; dep.-supt., July, 1894; dir., Feb., 1903; has contributed to the survey publications.

HOLME, ALAN THOMAS, Indian C.S. (settl. offr., United Prov.)—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 15th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. offr., Nov., 1898; settl. offr., Nov., 1901.

HOLME, CHARLES HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1891; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

HOLME, HENRY EDWARD, Barr. (1892), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.)—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 27th Sept., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1894; joint mag., June, 1896; dep. commr., Nov., 1902.

HOLME, HUGH BASIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma)—Educ. at Rugby Sch., Durham Coll. of Science, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 9th Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

HOLMES, ARTHUR CLEMENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.)—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 20th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

HOLMES, ROBERT ANDREW KING, M.A., M.D. Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ.; jail supt., Meerut, April, 1876; transf. in similar capacity to Lucknow, Feb., 1885; in 1892 acted as inspr.-gen. of prisons; retd., Mar., 1899.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Windermere Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; in charge of Amir Yakub Khan, March, 1880; joint mag., April, 1881; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., March, 1883; asst. commr., Oudh, Dec., 1883; joint mag., June, 1884; dep. commr., Aug., 1887; mag. and collr., Dec., 1888; acted as inspr.-gen. of police in 1893; retd., Sept., 1894.

HOLMES, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and in Bombay as off. asst. acctnt.-gen.; head asst., Coimbatore, Dec., 1884; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge S. Kanara, Feb., 1893; collr. district mag. and agent to govt., Vizagapatam, Dec., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., N. Arcot, Dec., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Kistna, Feb., 1899; retd., Aug., 1902.

HOLMS, JOHN MITCHELL, Indian C.S. (second sec. to govt., United Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 26th Nov., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1889; junr. sec. to board of rev., May, 1889, and Feb., 1891; asst. commr., Oudh, March, 1892; joint mag., Sept., 1892; acted as registrar, high court, in 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr. and joint sec. to rev. board, Aug., 1896; sec. to rev. board, Nov. 1898; inspr.-gen. of registrn. and commr. of excise, Mar., 1899; mag. and collr., Nov., 1901; second sec. to govt., April, 1903.

HOLMWOOD, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 16th Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1890; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1894.

HOLROYD, WILLIAM RICE MORLAND, Col., Indian Army, late Educl. Dept., Punjab.—First commn. as ensign, Dec., 1853; served from 11th Aug., 1858, as inspr. of schools, and was apptd. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, Feb., 1868; under sec. to govt., educl. dept., Oct., 1884; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1890.

HOME, ALEXANDER LEFEVRE, late Indian Forest Dept.—From 1st March, 1864, served in the dept. in the Punjab, Central Prov. and Bengal, and was apptd. dep. conservator, Oct., 1871; asst. compr.-gen. of forests, April, 1877; conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1883; Upper Burma, Oct., 1892; Assam, Dec., 1894; N.W. Prov., April, 1899; retd., Jan., 1901.

HOME, FREDERICK JERVIS, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public works dept., N.W. Prov., irrign. branch, from Feb., 1861, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1883; offg. chief engr. irrign. branch, and joint sec. to govt., N.W.

Prov., 1885; on special duty in the Bheartpur state, Oct., 1886; chief engr. and joint sec., Punjab, Dec., 1889; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Nov., 1890; C.S.I., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1894; retd., May, 1895.

HOME, WALTER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1877, and posted as asst. engr. to State Railways; exec. engr., Dec., 1891; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900.

HOOKER, WILLIAM HENSLOW, India Office (1st grade supervisor, Store Depôt).—Supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depôt, Aug., 1877; 1st grade, July, 1888.

HOOPER, CLEMENT WILLIAM ROBERT, Cmdr., R.I.M. (dep. constr. of the port of Madras).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1878; cmdr., Sept., 1891; dep. constr. of the port of Madras, June, 1902.

HOOPER, EDWARD DAW MASHITER (conservator, forest dept., Madras).—Entered the dept. as asst. constr., Dec., 1874; dep. constr., Sept., 1882; constr., March, 1900; is the author of reports on the forests of British Honduras and several West Indian islands.

HOOPER, JOHN, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 7th Dec., 1871, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; settlt. officer, Basti, Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., April, 1890; sec., board of rev., Aug., 1890; addl. commr., Fyzabad, Oct., 1896; commr., July, 1899; author of Basti settlt. report; C.S.I., Jan., 1904.

HOOPER, SIR WILLIAM ROE, K.C.S.I., Surgn.-Gen.—Asst. surgn., Bengalmed. establ., Feb., 1859; arrived, Aug., 1859, and served in Bengal, holding various mil. med. charges; from July, 1861, served in the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn., jail supt., Benares, and civil surgn. at Benares and Lucknow; retd., Jan., 1895; prest. of the India office med. board, Jan., 1895; C.S.I., June, 1897; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; retd. from India office, Jan., 1904.

HOPE, CHARLES WILLIAM WEBLEY, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Mannamead Sch., Plymouth, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1860; asst. commr., Oudh, June, 1891; settlt. officer, April, 1893; joint mag., April, 1896; dep. commr., June, 1896.

HOPE, SIR THEODORE CRACRAFT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Barr. (1866), late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 5th June, 1853, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and educl. inspr.; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, May, 1860; prest. of committee on finances of educl. dept., March, 1861; joint commr. to receive N. Kanara from the Madras govt., Feb., 1862; dep. commr. of customs, salt, and opium, Oct., 1862; collr. mag., and Jan., 1867; from July, 1875, served as addl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, also acting as sec. to govt. of India in dept. of rev., agric. and commerce, and was apptd. addl. sec. for famine, Dec., 1876; reappointed as addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council in 1877 and 1878; commr., N. divn., and agent to govr., Surat, Jan., 1878; on special pol. duty in England, 1878; provisional member of council, Bombay, Oct., 1880; sec. to govt. of India, fin.

and commerce dept., Aug., 1881; ordinary member of govr.-gen.'s council in charge of public works dept., July, 1882; C.I.E., July, 1882; K.C.S.I., May, 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

HOPKINS, EGBERT SILAS, India Office (staff clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry dept., 14th Nov., 1883; transf'd. to finl. dept., Sept., 1891; staff clerk, Jan., 1904.

HOPKINS, HENRY MAYNE REID, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 24th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1892; asst. supt. and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, Feb.-Dec., 1893; asst. commr., Feb., 1896; dep. commr., July, 1898; mag. and collr., July, 1900.

HOPKINSON, WILLIAM, Col., Bengal Infantry.—First commn., 1859; from 28th Nov., 1867, served in Bengal as asst. commr. and sub-registrar of assurances; cantonmt. mag., March, 1874; small cause court judge, Nov., 1875; dep. commr., April, 1886; again cantonmt. mag., April, 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

HORDERN, PETER, B.A., late Educ. Dept., Burma.—Apptd. to the dept., 19th Nov., 1861; arrived, March, 1862; asst. prof'r., presidency coll., Calcutta, March, 1862; dir. of public instrn., British Burma, Nov., 1867; fellow of Calcutta univ., April, 1876; ret'd., 1889; author of a 'Handbook of Rules and Practice of the Educn. Dept., Burma.'

HORMASJI DADABHAI, Khan Bahadur, late Bombay Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 26th April, 1889; judge, Bombay small cause court, July, 1891; ret'd., Nov., 1901.

HORMASJI MERWANJI HAKIM, Lieut.-Col., Ind. Med. Service (district med. offr., Madras).—First commn., 2nd Oct., 1880; military duty till 1892; dist. surgn., Cuddalore, March, 1892; civil surgn., Tellicherry, Sept., 1893; dist. med. and sany. offr. at various stations from Feb., 1895; Salem, Jan., 1902.

HORN, DAVID BAYNE, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.)—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; asst. sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Feb., 1878; exec. engr., March, 1882; under sec., public works dept., 1885-86; suptg. engr., Feb., 1896; member, legisl. council, Bengal, July, 1902; chief engr. and sec., public works dept., Bengal, Aug., 1902.

HORNE, WILLIAM OGILVIE, Indian C.S. (collr., &c., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting dep. registrar, high court; head asst., March, 1890; special asst., Ganjam, Feb., 1893; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., Nov., 1893; head asst., S. Kanara, March, 1894; sub.-collr. and joint mag., April, 1890; collr., district mag. and agent to govr., Vizagapatam, Mar., 1898.

HORSFALL, JEREMIAH GARNETT, C.I.E., B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras, and was apptd. district and sessions judge, April, 1878; collr. and mag., Jan., 1879; ret'd., April, 1890; C.I.E., May, 1890.

HORSFALL, THOMAS MENDELSSOHN, Barr. (1885), late Indian C.S., Madras.—Educ. at the

Wilhelms - Gymnasium, Berlin; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Madura, Dec., 1884; also forest settl. officer, Nov., 1885; registrar of high court (appellate side), Sept., 1888; principal asst., Ganjam, Dec., 1890; collr. and mag. Dec., 1891; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1892; ret'd., Oct., 1900.

HORSFORD, NORMAN MACLEOD THOMAS, Col., Indian Army—Arrived, as ensign, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the Mutiny campaign (medal and clasp); from Aug., 1861, served in the police dept., N.W. Prov., as asst. inspr.-gen., and district supt.; and from Dec., 1867, in Oudh as asst. commr., junr. sec., and offg. dir. of public instrn.; supt. of encumbered estates, Rae Bareli, 1874-76; offg. supt. of excise and stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., April, 1876; cantonmt. mag., Lucknow, March, 1878; city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, April, 1883; district judge, April, 1886; dep. commr., March, 1887; chief commr., Andaman and Nicobars, April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Sept., 1895.

HORSLEY, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and was in charge of the forests in Khandesh, 1872-74; on famine duty, 1874 and 1877; forest settl. officer, Nov., 1878; second asst. collr., Sept., 1883; judge and sessions judge, May, 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1896.

HORSPool, WILLIAM PADDISON (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Educ. at the Newmark Coll. Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 19th Aug., 1878.

HÖRST, HENRY, late Survey Dept., Govt. of India.—Librarian and accountant, Thomason coll., Jan., 1853; sub-assst., great trigon. survey, Sept., 1854; in charge of picket which repulsed night attack on Murree sanitarium, 1857 (medal); transf'd. to topogr. survey, April, 1868; supt., Sept., 1882; ret'd., 1889.

HORST, WALTER PHILIPS VON DER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 17th Oct., 1872, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; offg. asst. sec., public works dept., 1881 and 1882-84; exec. engr., Jan., 1883; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

HOSE, JOHN WALTER, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Dulwich and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1891; under sec. to govt., April, 1893; registrar, high court, March, 1894; dep. commr., March, 1898; mag. and collr., June, 1899.

HOSKYN, CHARLES REGINALD, Major, R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 9th Jan., 1873; apptd., 29th Sept., 1876, and employed on the Punjab N. and Indus valley railways; transf'd. to superior accts. establ., and served as dep. examr. and examr.; dep. acctn.-gen. for inspn. duty, March, 1894; examr., guaranteed railway accts., Bombay, Oct., 1894; ret'd., 22nd Feb., 1901.

HOSSEIN, A. (see Ameer Hossein).

HOTSON, JOHN ERNEST BUTTERY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at

Edinburgh Acad. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 24th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. HOUGH, ALFRED LACEY (addl. sess. judge, Burma).—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1870, as sub-assst. conservator of forests; apptd. to the commn. as extra asst. commr., Oct., 1874; dep. commr., Aug., 1891; addl. sessions judge, Nov., 1902.

HOUGHTON, BERNARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (addl. sessions judge, Burma).—Educ. at All Saints, Bloxham, Wellingborough Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 23rd Jan., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transfed. to Burma, as asst. commr., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., March, 1891; addl. sess. judge, April, 1901; author of an 'Essay on the Language of the S. Chins,' and other linguistic works.

HOUSDEN, CHARLES EDWARD, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. 23rd Oct., 1876, and posted as apprentice engr. to Rajputana; asst. engr., Oct., 1877; transfed. to Upper Burma, Jan., 1887; exec. engr., Sept., 1887; asst. sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1890; under sec. to govt., Upper Burma, Dec., 1895; transfed. for service at Port Blair, Aug., 1898; asst. to chief engr. and under-sec. to govt., pub. works dept., July to Sept., 1899.

HOUSDEN, WILLIAM PREST, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept. 21st May, 1881, and posted to N.W. Provs. and Oudh; asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., June, 1894.

HOUSE, HENRY FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Nov., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. supt., Dehra Dun, and asst. commr.; settlt. officer, Nov., 1893; mag. and collr., Jan., 1897; acting sec., rev. board, Mar., 1900; sec., rev. board, Dec., 1900 to June, 1902; has edited the N.W. Prov. rent act.

HOUSLEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, India Office, (senior clerk, Acclnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 19th Feb., 1872; junr. clerk, June, 1882; senior clerk, April, 1902.

HOWARD, EDWARD STAFFORD, C.B..—Educ. at Harrow, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1875; M.P. for E. Cumberland, 1876-85, and for the Thornbury divn. of Gloucestershire, 1885-86; under sec. of state for India, April-Aug., 1886; apptd. a commr. of woods and forests, Oct., 1893; C.B., May, 1900; member of board of visitors, R.I.E. Coll., Jan., 1903.

HOWARD, HENRY FRASER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HOWARD, WILLIAM STUART, M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept. —Joined the service, 10th June, 1859; arrived, 20th Nov., 1859; exec. engr., Dec., 1865; chief engr., Aug., 1888; sec. to govt. of Bombay, public works dept., June, 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1894.

HOWELL, ARTHUR PEARSE, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1857;

arrived, 27th Jan., 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; from April, 1864, to March, 1878, served in the secretariat of the govt. of India, in the finl. home, and rev. depts.; commr., Jabalpur, Central Prov., April, 1877; sec., famine commn., March, 1878; commr., Narbada, Sept., 1878; acting jud. commr., Central Prov., 1879-80, and again in 1885; commr., Hyderabad assigned districts, June to Dec., 1882; member of educ. commn., 1882-83; offg. rest., Hyderabad, March, 1888; ret'd., Aug., 1889; author of notes on educn. in India, and on jails and jail discipline, and of a paper on educ. legislation.

HOWELL, AUBERON ARTHUR, Captain, Indian Army (dep. commr., Assam).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1885; asst. commr., Assam, Oct., 1888; dep. commr., Feb., 1898.

HOWELL, EVELYN BERKELEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 8th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov., as assist. commr.

HOWELL, GERARD CHARLES LISLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Lancing and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 16th Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.

HOWELL, MORTIMER SLOPER, C.I.E., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. inspr. of public instrn., April, 1867; asst. supt. and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, March, 1868; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1871; mag. and collr., Nov., 1879; from March, 1880, served as district and sessions judge in various districts; addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, Dec., 1891; jud. commr., November, 1892; ret'd., April, 1896; author of an Arabic grammar.

HOWETT, FRANK, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 1st Dec., 1902; served in South African War, 1901-02 (medal and three clasps).

HOWLETT, RICHARD ROSS, B.A. India Office (junior clerk, Store Dept.).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge (scholar); junr. clerk, 16th April, 1895.

HUBBACK, JOHN AUSTEN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 6th Dec., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HUDA, N. (see Nural Huda).

HUDSON, CHARLES TILSON, Major, Indian M.S.—Entered the service, 30th March, 1889; dep. assay master, Bombay Mint, Sept., 1900.

HUDSON, CHARLES WILLIAM MAITLAND, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Doveton Protestant Coll., Madras, Madras Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 1st Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., May, 1895; 1st asst., July, 1897.

HUDSON, WALTER FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at

Dulwich Coll., Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

HUDSON, WILLIAM HEBARD, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 17th Nov., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine duty in Behar, 1873-75; from May, 1880, served in the jud. dept., N.W. Prov., as civil and sessions judge; ret'd., Jan., 1890.

HUGGINS, JOHN REGINALD, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 20th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HUGHES, ARTHUR JOHN, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th July, 1862, and arrived, 7th Dec., 1862; exec. engr., Dec., 1869; asst. sec. to govt. of Bengal, irrign. branch, May, 1872; on famine works, Salem district, Sept., 1877; served under the foreign dept. in Kashmir, 1884-85; suptg. engr., Nov., 1888; suptg. engr. for waterworks, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1888-93; offg. chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., April, 1895; ret'd., 1896; author of a report on the feasibility of improved water communication between the Ganges and Calcutta.

HUGHES, DAVID ERSKINE, M.D., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., late Bombay Med. Service.—Joined the service, March, 1866; arrived, 9th Nov., 1866; served under the mil. dept., and also acted as civil surgn.; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Belgaum, from May, 1882; civil surgn., Poona, Sept., 1890; reverted to mil. dept., Sept., 1892; and apptd. prin. med. officer, Bombay and Aden dists.; ret'd., Sept., 1897.

HUGHES, EDWARD WILLIAM MACKENZIE, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., April, 1871, as asst. engr., Punjab irrign.; locomotive supt., Dhond-Manmad state railway, May, 1879; Indus valley railway, Feb., 1881; dep. locomotive supt., N.W. railway, Jan., 1886; on special duty in Mexico in 1887-88; locomotive supt., E. Coast railway, Jan., 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

HUGHES, GEORGE, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1875, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; district judge, Aug., 1890; asst. commr., 1st grade, Dec., 1890; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

HUGHES, JOHN WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Jan., 1900.

HUGHES, THEODORE WILLIAM HUGHES, A.R.S.M., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India.—Apptd. asst., and joined, 11th Nov., 1862; 1st grade officer, April, 1883; suptd. Rewa coal explorations from March, 1883; designation changed to dep. supt.; supt. April, 1887; acted as dir. in 1892; ret'd., Oct., 1894; author of contributions to the survey publs.

HUGHES, WALTER CHARLETON, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (suptg.

enr. and chairman, Bombay Port Trust).—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1868, and served in various grades of the dept.; asst. sec. to govt., April, 1882; under sec., March, 1884; sec., Nov., 1888; chairman, Bombay port trust, June, 1892; C.I.E., May, 1900; addl. member of legisl. council, Bombay, July, 1897, Jan., 1899, and Jan., 1902.

HUGHES, WILLIAM, B.A., M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as proby. civil engr.; asst. engr., Sept., 1870; exec. engr., Sept., 1877; suptg. engr., Feb., 1894; chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., Oct., 1896; ret'd., Oct., 1902.

HUGHES, WILLIAM GWYNNE, Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn. as ensign, June, 1853; arrived, 11th Sept., 1853, and was employed on mil. duty and in the police dept., Burma; transfid. to the Burma commn., June, 1864, and served as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag. of Rangoon, town mag. of Akyab, supt. of the hill tracts, Arakan, and dep. commr.; ret'd., June, 1889; author of 'The Hill Tracts of Arakan.'

HUGHES-BULLER, RALPH BULLER, Indian C.S. (special duty in Baluchistan).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 4th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settl. officer; pol. asst., May, 1899; asst. to gov.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, Aug., 1900; special duty in connection with census and gazetteer, Oct., 1901; author of a report on the census of Baluchistan, 1901.

HUGHESDON, JOSEPH CHARLES, Barr. (1876), late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Sutton Valence Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., small cause court judge, and sub-collr. and joint mag.; on famine duty, June, 1877; district and sessions judge, Tinneveli, March, 1880; ret'd., Aug., 1888.

HULLAH, ARTHUR, late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 20th Feb., 1869, and employed on various lines; supt., Aug., 1885; in charge, Rajputana divn., April, 1892; personal asst. to dir.-gen., Feb., 1893; ret'd., Sept., 1896.

HULLAH, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.).—Educ. at Oundle and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 16th Dec., 1899, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.

HUME, ALLAN OCTAVIAN, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1849; arrived, 7th March, 1849; served in N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Etawah, March, 1858; commr. of customs, N.W. Prov., Punjab, and Central Prov., July, 1867; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., 1870-71; sec. to govt. of India in dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, June, 1871; member of board of rev., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1879; ret'd., 1882; author of several works on ornithology.

HUMFRESS, GEORGE, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Thomson Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883; employed on N.W. railway from Jan., 1886; dep. engr.-in-chief, Northern section, Feb., 1898; suptg. engr., April, 1899.

HUMPHREYS, CHARLES EDWARD, India Office (second-class clerk, India Audit Office).—Appdtd. 2nd class clerk, India audit office, 20th May, 1902.

HUMPHREYS, ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (senr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 29th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; under sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1901; senr. sec. to finl. commr., Nov., 1901.

HUMPHRIES, EDGAR DE MONTFORT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Grammar Sch., Lichfield, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 9th Dec. 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

HUMPHRYS, ISAAC HUTCHINSON, India Office (senior clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (exhibr. and prizeman); book-keeper, 5th July, 1872; junr. clerk, Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, Sept., 1894.

HUNT, SAMUEL BRADSHAW, Surgn.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Asst. surgn., 31st March, 1865; arrived, 10th Dec., 1865, and served under the mil. dept., and also as civil surgn. in Madras; surgn., 4th district, and prof. of med. jurisprudence, med. coll., Feb., 1893; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1893, and apptd. princ. med. officer, Bangalore and Southern districts; ret'd., March, 1898.

HUNTER, DUNCAN CLEMENTS HORACE, late Punjab Police Dept.—Joined the service, 17th March, 1874, as offg. asst. dist. supt.; confd., Oct., 1875; dist. supt., March, 1885; ret'd., March, 1903.

HUNTER, JOHN MUIR, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commr., royal artillery, 12th April, 1864; arrived, 14th Dec. 1865; boundary settl. officer, Baroda and Mahi Kantha states, Oct., 1869; from June, 1872, served as asst. pol. agent in Mahi Kantha, Kolhapur, and Kathiawar; from Feb., 1883, served as supt. of surveys under the rajasthanik court, Kathiawar, and was employed in settling claims in dispute between native states, from Dec., 1884; prest., rajasthanik court, May, 1891; asst. agent, Baroda, Oct., 1891; first asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1893, to Jan., 1894, and Nov., 1894, to March, 1895; prest., rajasthanik court, January, 1894; pol. agent, Kolhapur, April, 1896; pol. agent, Kathiawar, April, 1897; C.S.I., June, 1899; ret'd., April, 1902.

HURRY, ARNOLD EARDLEY, Barr. (1899), Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 25th Oct., 1882, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from March, 1886, served in Burma as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; retransfd., Dec., 1890; junr. sec. to govt., March, 1895; dep. commr., June, 1896; offg. divl. judge, March, 1897; confd., Oct., 1898.

HUSAIN, A. (see Akbar Husain).

HUSSAIN, A. (see Aulad Hussain).

HUSSANALLY, A. A. (see Ali Ahmed Hussanally).

HUSSEY, VICTOR EDWIN GROVE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 28th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Provs. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

HUTCHESON, GEORGE, M.D., C.M., Col. Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 1st Oct., 1866; served on gen. and mil. duty, and in 1870-71 as chemical analyst; on special duty, cholera enquiry, 1872-73; sanitary commr. and supt.-gen. of vaccn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1889; on depn. to mil. duty, April-Oct., 1895; admn. med. officer, Central Provs., July, 1896; inspr.-gen. of civil hospls., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1899; ret'd., Oct., 1902; author of 'Cholera, its Causes, and Mode of Dissemination,' and 'Cholera as a Preventible Disease.'

HUTCHINS, SIR PHILIP PERCEVAL, K.C.S.I., Barr. (1875), late Madras C.S. (member of the Council of India).—Apptd. to the Madras civil service from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 27th Feb., 1858; served as asst. collr. and mag., dep. sec. to govt., sec. to board of examrs., and as judge of the court of small causes, Chittur; private sec. to govr. of Madras, Nov., 1863; registrar, high court, March, 1865; district and sessions judge, Madura, April, 1873; called to the bar (Inner Temple), June, 1875; member of rent law committee, Sept., 1882; acting sec., rev. dept., Dec., 1882; on special duty in connection with reorganization of jud. establishs., March, 1883; board of examrs., May, 1883; judge of the high court, Madras, Nov., 1883; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1884; member of govr.'s council, Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; member of the council of the govr.-gen., Nov., 1888; K.C.S.I., May, 1891; ret'd., Nov., 1893; sec., jud. and public dept., India office, Dec., 1893, to July, 1898; member of Council of India, Aug., 1898.

HUTCHINSON, FREDERICK THOMAS, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 25th March, 1871, as asst. comptr.; dep. comptr. May, 1874; examr., Sept., 1881; examr., public works accts., Bombay, May, 1890; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

HUTCHINSON, HENRY NORTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (registrar, court of judl. commr. of Oudh).—Educ. at Lancing Coll. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; registrar, court of judl. commr. of Oudh, July 1903.

HUTCHINSON, JAMES BIRD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 26th May, 1864; from March, 1868, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Dec., 1884; served under govt. of India, Aug. to Oct., 1887; commr. and supt., Lahore, March, 1894; has publ. a 'Guide to Dalhousie,' and a gen. index of regulations and acts in force in the Punjab.

HUTCHINSON, SYDNEY HUTTON COOPER, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Telegraph Dept. (dir., construction branch).—Apptd., 30th Nov., 1871, as asst. supt.; supt., Bombay, Oct., 1887; chief supt., Feb., 1901; K.-i.-H. medal, Nov. 1901; dir., construction branch, March, 1903.

HUTTON, CHARLES HERBERT, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the

Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1893.

HUTTON, EDWARD, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service as supt. of mails, Jabalpur, 10th Feb., 1858; supt., travelling post office, Nov., 1877; inspr., field force, Peshawar column, Feb., 1879; presidency postmr. at Calcutta, April, 1881; acted as postmr.-gen. at Lahore in 1890, at Bombay in 1891, and at Calcutta from April, 1892; retd., April, 1893.

HYAT KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Hyat Khan).

HYDARI, M. A. N. A. (see Muhammad Akbar Nazar Ali Hydari).

HYDE, HENRY, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 27th June, 1867; surgn., Oct., 1871; zillah surgn. and jail supt., April, 1872; civil surgn., Nov., 1884; retd., May, 1895.

HYDE, HENRY THOMAS, Barr. (1876).—Apptd. administrator-gen., Bengal, 1st Jan., 1902.

HYDE-CATES, GEORGE EDWARD, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (pol. supt., Palanpur).—First commn., 6th Dec., 1876; joined the Staff corps, Jan., 1880; adjt., Kolhapur inf., Jan., 1882; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, July, 1893; pol. agent, Cutch, Mar., 1898; pol. agent, Somali Coast, June, 1900; ditto, Savantvadi, March, 1901; pol. supt., Palanpur, Nov. 1901.

HYNES, GEORGE JOHN, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Entered the dept., 4th May, 1868; asst. dir.-gen. of the post office of India, Oct., 1880; dep. postmr.-gen., Burma, June, 1887; postmr.-gen., Punjab, Jan., 1891; dep. dir.-gen. of post office of India, July, 1891; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, March-Aug., 1894; retd. Jan., 1896.

IBBETSON, SIR DENZIL CHARLES JELF, K.C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (member of gov.-gen.'s council).—Educ. at Coll. Sch., Adelaide, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 8th Dec., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dep. supt. of census, Punjab, Aug., 1881; compiler, 'Punjab Gazetteer,' March, 1883; offg. dir. of public instrn., March, 1884; dep. commr., April, 1885; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Nov., 1885; offd. as 2nd final commr. in the Punjab in 1890; in charge of Gujranwala settlt., May, 1891; member of Deccan agriculturists relief commn., 1891-92; on special duty connected with the cantonmt. act, 1893; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., July, 1894; promoted to commr., retaining his offg. appt., April, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., Dec., 1896; chief commr., Central Provs., July, 1898; temp. member of gov.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1899; confd., March, 1902; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; has publ. 'Census Report of the Punjab (1881)'; 'Outlines of Punjab Ethnography,' the Karnal settlt. report, and contributions to the provl. gazetteers.

IBRAHIM SHAIK DAUD AHMADI, L.C.E., F.R.I.B.A., Bombay P.W. Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service 12th March, 1883, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

IEVERS, OLIVER GOLDSMITH, Major, Indian Army, Hyderabad Commn. (cantonmt. mag.,

Secunderabad).—First commn., 22nd Oct., 1881; in mil. employ till Nov., 1894, when apptd. asst. commr., Hyderabad; ditto, and cantonment mag., Secunderabad, April, 1897.

ILBERT, SIR COURTENAY PEREGRINE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. (clerk of House of Commons).—Educ. at Marlborough and Oxford; scholar, and afterwards fellow of Balliol Coll.; Hertford scholar, 1861; Ireland scholar, 1864; Craven scholar, 1864; Eldon law scholar, 1867; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 7th June, 1869; counsel to educn. dept., 1878-82; law member of the council of the gov.-gen. of India, 1st May, 1882, and was prest. of the council, 3rd Feb. to 8th March, 1886; resigned, 13th Nov., 1886; vice-chancellor of Calcutta univ., 1885-86; asst. parly. counsel to the treasury, 1886; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895; parly. counsel to the Treasury, Jan., 1899; clerk of House of Commons, Feb., 1902.

IMPEY, LAWRENCE, Major, Indian Army, Foreign dept., govt. of India (pol. agent, Bhopal).—First commn., 10th March, 1883; pol. assist., Aug., 1889; asst. to gov.-gen'l's. agent in Rajputana, April, 1890; asst. commr., Merwara, March, 1895; pol. agent, Bhopal, May, 1895; again asst. in Rajputana, June, 1895; pol. agent, Alwar, July, 1898, to Feb., 1899, and Oct., 1899; pol. agent in Bhopal, March, 1902.

IMPEY, WILLIAM HENRY LOCKINGTON, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 7th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Naini Tal, Nov., 1886; under sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1888; settlt. officer, Oct., 1889; joint mag., April, 1892; sec. to govt., finl. dept., Aug., 1893; also member of legisl. council, Dec., 1893 and Dec. 1901; mag. and collr., and acting sec. to govt., jud. dept., March, 1895; offg. commr., June, 1899; offg. chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1901; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; commr., July, 1902; chief sec. to govt., Oct., 1902; part author of Jhansi settlt. report.

INAM ALI, Maulvi, B.A., Khan Bahadur (dist. judge, Punjab).—Apptd. asst. profr. govt. coll., Lahore, 31st Oct., 1887; probr. under native civil service rules, 4th Feb., 1888; asst. commr., April, 1891; dist. judge, Aug., 1895; has publ. in Urdu a transn. and an epitome of Hamilton's lectures on psychology.

INCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

INGLIS, ERNEST, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (divl. and sess. judge, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Arrived 27th March, 1877; in mil. employ till 1886; asst. commr., Punjab, Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; divl. judge, Dec., 1898; divl. and sess. judge, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901.

INGLIS, JOHN, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., joined the service, 1st Oct., 1882, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1894.

INGLIS, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Nov., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and

dep. collr., May, 1890; junr sec. to board of rev., July, 1892; mag. and collr., June, 1896.

INGLIS, WILLIAM ARBUTHNOT, India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1883; offg. under sec., public works dept., Bengal, 1891-92; suptg. engr., July, 1896; chief engr., Dec., 1902.

INGRAM, GERALD CONSTANTINE WINNINGTON, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 13th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1898.

INNES, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (spcl. asst. settlmt. offr., Madras).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlmt. offr., March, 1901.

INNES, JAMES JOHN McLEOD, V.C., Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn. in Bengal engrs., 1848; served under the civil engr., Punjab, from 1852; asst. to chief engr., Oudh, Jan., 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps, V.C.); exec. engr., Cawnpore, Dec., 1857; garrison engr., presidency, April, 1858; compt. and examr. of accts., Lahore, Feb., 1861; offg. acctnt.-gen., public works dept., 1865-66, and 1867-68; suptg. engr., Punjab, Oct., 1869; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., public works dept., June, 1870; chief engr., Sept., 1881, and dep. inspr.-gen., mil. works; inspr.-gen., 1882; ret'd., March, 1886.

INNES, JOHN ALBERT, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Entered the service, April, 1869; extra asst. commr., Oct., 1871; dist. supt. of police, Jan., 1884; ret'd., Oct., 1901.

INNES, LEWIS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Charterhouse and Haileybury, and apptd. in 1844; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-judge, and civil and sessions judge; judge of the high court, Madras, Feb., 1865, to Sept., 1883, and offd. on several occasions as chief justice; ret'd., 1883; fellow of the univ. of Madras, 1865-83; vice-chancellor, 1874-80.

IRELAND, SAMUEL GARDINER DE COURCY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

IRVINE, GEORGE DUNCAN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras from May, 1865, as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr. and joint mag., and acting collr. of sea customs at Madras; district and sessions judge, July, 1882 ret'd., June, 1891.

IRVINE, ROBERT HAMILTON GRANT, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service as dep. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1861; apptd. asst. supt. of police, April, 1863; district supt., July, 1869; ret'd., Sept., 1891.

IRVINE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Anne's Sch., Brixton, and King's Coll., London; apptd.

after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Dec., 1881; author of 'Canal Rates versus Land Revenue,' 'The Rent Digest; or the Law Procedure relating to Landlord and Tenant, Bengal,' 'Bangash Nawabs of Farukhabad,' and Ghazipur settlt. report; ret'd., 1888.

IRVING, LEWIS ALLEN, Lieut.-Col., late Royal Army Med. Corps.—Med. officer to govr. of Bombay (Rt. Hon. Lord Reay), 1885 to 1890; ret'd., April, 1898.

IRVING, MILES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived 16th Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

IRWIN, ALFRED MACDONALD BULTEEL, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 24th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab till June, 1877, and subsequently in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; offd. as commr. in 1894, and from June, 1895; confid., May, 1898; member of legis. council, April, 1903.

IRWIN, GEORGE ROBERT, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 16th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India; foreign dept., Dec., 1886; first asst. sec. for Berar to rest., Haidarabad, March, 1892; pol. agent, Jhalawar, April, 1893; resdt., Jaipur, April, 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1901; gen. supdt., thagi and dakaiti dept., July, 1901; ret'd., Nov., 1903.

IRWIN, HENRY C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., Central Prov., 1st Feb., 1868; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; supt. of works, Simla imperial circle, Oct., 1881; C.I.E., June, 1888; acting public works sec. to agents for Rajputana and Central India, Nov., 1888; transfid. to Madras public works dept. as suptg. engr., and apptd. consulting architect to govt., Sept., 1889; ret'd. Jan., 1896.

IRWIN, HENRY CROSSLY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Black heath Proprietary Sch., St. Edmund's Hall, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 6th Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., joint mag. and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Nov., 1889; acted as sec. to the board of rev. in 1893; offd. as dist. and sess. judge, April, 1895; author of 'The Garden of India; or Chapters on Oudh History and Affairs'; ret'd., July, 1896.

ISHAK KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Ishak Khan).

ISMAY, STANLEY, C.S.I., Barr. (1883), Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 14th Nov., 1871; served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and was on special duty in the Kalahandi state, 1882; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, May, 1884; offg. inspr.-gen. of police and jails, 1885 and 1890; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, Dec., 1889; dep. commr., Jan., 1890; addl. sessions judge, Jan., 1892; offg. jud. commr., Feb., 1895; divl. judge, June, 1895; judl. commr.

Dec., 1897; C.S.I., Nov., 1901; author of 'Rules for the Superintendence and Management of Jails in the Central Prov.' (1885).

IVENS, JOHN HENRY ANDERSON, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the public works dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1877; exec. engr., Aug., 1891; suptg. engr., Feb., 1900.

IVENS, THOMAS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 26th July, 1867, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1874; offg. under sec., Oct., 1886, and again, May, 1889; suptg. engr., Oct., 1892; ret'd. 1898.

IZAT, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1863, as asst. engr., Haidarabad; exec. engr., March, 1868; transf'd. to railway branch, 1870, and served on Nizam's, Holkar's, Dhond and Manmad, Kathiawar, and Bombay E. Deccan lines; suptg. engr., April, 1880; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Jan., 1883; services lent to Bengal and N.W. railway co. as agent and chief engr., Feb., 1883; ret'd., 1889; C.I.E., Dec., 1898.

JACK, JAMES CHARLES, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JACK, ROBERT ERNEST, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JACKSON, ARCHIBALD (staff clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 24th Dec., 1892; staff clerk, May, 1901.

JACKSON, ARTHUR MASON TIPPETTS, M.A., Barr. (1888), Indian C.S. (junr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 17th Dec., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. and inspr. of factories, Bombay, Sept., 1892; 2nd asst., July, 1895; under sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Feb., 1897; 1st asst., May, 1898; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1898, to Feb., 1900; junr. collr., April, 1902.

JACKSON, EDWIN JAMES, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1864; asst. supt., Nov., 1868; dep. supt., Oct., 1885; supt., cadastral survey, Burma, March, 1895; ret'd., Nov., 1899.

JACKSON, FRANK ERNEST, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at the City of London Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and sub-divl. officer; asst. commr., Assam, Feb., 1896.

JACKSON, FREEMAN HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, 1861; arrived, 10th Jan., 1862; from Jan., 1867, acted in charge of office of asst. rest., Amreli, and was apptd. to settle boundaries in E. Kathiawar, Jan., 1868; acting asst. rest., Dwarika, and commandant of the Gaekwar's battalion, March, 1869; asst. rest., Baroda, in charge Okhamandal, July, 1874; on duty in Baroda, Jan., 1875; extra asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., June, 1875; from May, 1876,
