served as asst. agent to the govr.-gen., Baroda, and from Feb., 1879, as supt. of the Gaekwar's contingent, Kathiawar, asst. in charge of Amreli Mahals, and supt. for the improvement of the breed of horses in Kathiawar; offd. for various periods as rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent, Baroda; pol. supt. of Palanpur, Aug., 1895; returned to Europe, Dec., 1897.

JACKSON, GILBERT HOLLINSHEAD BLOMFIELD, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., Merton Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JACKSON, HUGH MILBOURNE, Major, R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 19th June, 1877; entered the dept. as asst. supt., June, 1883; dep. supt., April, 1889; served in the Burma expedn., 1886, the first S. Shan expedn., 1886-87, with the N. Karen column, 1888-89, and in S. Africa, 1900-02.

JACOB, EDWARD FOUNTAINE, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (traffic supt., N.W. railway).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; district traffic supt., Feb., 1880; attached to public works secretariat, govt. of India, April, 1888; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, May, 1890; traffic supt., May, 1892; C.I.E., May, 1898.

JACOB, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (judl. commr. and judge of Sadar Court, in Sind).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Highgate Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Nov., 1879, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1883, served as asst. judge and sessions judge, and as asst. agent for sardars in the Deccan; joint judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; acting registrar, high court, appellate side, Feb., 1888; 1st asst. judge, Nov., 1891; judge and sessions judge, March, 1894; judl. commr. and judge of Sadar Court in Sind, April, 1902; offg. judge, high court, Bombay, July, 1903.

JACOB, HAROLD FENTON, Capt., Indian Army, For. dept., govt. of India (asst. to resdt., Baroda).—First commn., 14th Sept., 1887; in mil. employ till Nov., 1892, when he was apptd. offg. asst. pol. supt., Palanpur; confd., Aug., 1895; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, April, 1897; special asst. to pol. supt., Palanpur, Dec., 1899; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Oct., 1901; asst. to resdt. Baroda, April, 1903.

JACOB, HERBERT BRUCE, Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Dec., 1857; served from 26th June, 1869, in the educ. dept., Central Prov., and was apptd. inspr. of schools, April, 1871; acting inspr.-gen. of educn., 1882-84; cantonmt. mag., Karachi, 1889-92; ret'd. to Europe, Dec., 1895.

JACOB, LIONEL MONTAGUE, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Nov., 1894; supg. engr., Jan., 1899.

JACOB, SIR SAMUEL SWINTON, K.C.I.E., A.I.C.E., Col., Indian Army, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 17th Nov., 1862, and posted to Bombay; services lent to Jaipur state, July, 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; public

works sec. to agent in Rajputana, 1886, 1887, and 1889; C.I.E., May, 1890; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893; services specially retained, under Jaipur state, Dec., 1896; sec. to govt. of Punjab, public works dept., Nov., 1897; returned to Europe, 1898; K.-i-H. medal, Nov., 1901; K.C.I.E., June, 1902.

JACOB, SYDNEY LONG, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th January, 1869, and served on irrig. works in Bombay and the Punjab as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served through the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); suptg. engr., Oct., 1890; chief engr., Feb., 1898; C.I.E., May, 1900; retd., July, 1900.

JAMBULINGAM MUDALIYAR, C., C.I.E., Rao Bahadur, B.A., M.L.—Apptd. offg. judge, Madras City civil court, 13th Sept., 1902; confd., 30th Sept., 1902; C.I.E., June, 1902.

JAMES, CHARLES HENRY, Major, I.M.S. (medical adviser to Patiala state).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1891; in mil. employ till July, 1893, when he was apptd. offg. dep. sany. inspr., Punjab; confd., March, 1898; held various civil surgeonies from Feb., 1899, to July, 1902, when he was apptd. profir. at Lahore Medical Coll.; med. adviser to Patiala State; Feb., 1903; author of 'Notes on Vaccination.'

JAMES, CHARLES STREATFIELD, M.I.E.E., Indian Telegraph Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879; supt., Jan., 1899.

JAMES, SIR HENRY EVAN MURCHISON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Cathedral Sch., Carlisle, and Durham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 14th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and as branch inspr.-gen. of registrn., and educt. inspr. in Sind; under sec. to govt. in the rev., fin., and gen. depts., Nov., 1875; postmr.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1877; postmr.-gen., Bengal, March, 1882; acted as dir.-gen. of the post office in India, 1883-85; dep. dir.-gen., Nov., 1885; senr. collr. and collr. and mag., Ahmadabad, April, 1888; commr., June, 1890; commr. in Sind, Nov., 1892; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Feb.-March, 1895, Dec., 1896-March, 1897, and Jan.-March, 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; offg. member of council, July, 1898, and April, 1899; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1901; author of 'The Long White Mountain; or, Travels in Manchuria'; retd., Dec., 1900.

JAMES, HENRY ROSHER, M.A., Bengal Educn. Dept. (profir., Patna Coll.).—Apptd. profir., Patna Coll., 9th Feb., 1890; was profir. presy. Coll., Calcutta, from Jan., 1900, to July, 1901.

JAMES, HENRY WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 23rd May, 1881, and posted to Burma; asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; asst. to chief engr., and under sec. to govt., Burma, Oct., 1899, to Nov., 1900.

JAMESON, ARTHUR SAMPSON, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1886, and served in the locomotive dept., N.W. railway; transfed. to E. Bengal state railway, Aug., 1888; district locomotive supt., July, 1889; locomotive supt., Oct., 1890; retd., 1901.

JAMESON, ROBERT, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Sanitary Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the Bengal med. service, Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866, and was employed on civil and mil. duty; in 1868 was in med. charge at Calcutta of troops and coolies, en route to Abyssinia; served in Abyssinia with 12th Bengal cavalry; served in Bombay and Punjab, 1868 and 1869, and in the N.W. Prov. from 1870 on civil and jail duty dep. sanitary commr., Nov., 1886; retd., May, 1896.

JACQUET, ROBERT GLOVER, India Office (senr. clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Junn. clerk, 11th May, 1877; senr. clerk, April, 1897; assessor of income tax, May, 1902.

JARDINE, SIR JOHN, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 30th Oct., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; acting under sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1869; from May, 1871, served in Kathiawar as pol. asst.; also jud. asst., June, 1873; acting registrar of the high court, Bombay, Oct., 1873; sec. to the Baroda commn. for the trial of the Gaekwar, 1875; sec. to the delegates of the govt. of India in the negotiations for a commercial treaty with Portugal, 1877, also acting as dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept.; acting sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Nov., 1877; from Feb., 1878, served in British Burma as jud. commr.; prest. of the educt. syndicate, Sept., 1881; acting chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, Oct., 1885; judge of the high court, Bombay, Nov., 1885; acted as chief justice in 1895; retd., Feb., 1897; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; author of 'Notes on Buddhist Law'; editor of Moung Tet Pyo's Customary Law of the Chin Tribe, also of a reprint of Dr. Tandy's transln. of Father Sangermano's description of the Burman Empire, and a compilation of printed judgments of the jud. commr. of British Burma; wrote a preface to Dr. Forchhammer's 'King Wagaru's Dhamma-Sattham.'

JARDINE, WILLIAM ELLIS, Indian C.S. (first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 20th Feb., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. to rest., Hyderabad, Nov., 1893; head asst. and mag., Bellary, March, 1896; pol. asst., Hyderabad, Jan., 1897; ditto, Indore, May, 1898; and asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India; first asst. to the resdt., Hyderabad, May, 1900; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1903.

JARRETT, HENRY SULLIVAN, C.I.E., Col., Indian Army, late Legisl. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Dec., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal); also in the Mahsud Waziri expdn., 1860 (medal and clasp), and the 2nd Yusufzai expdn., 1863 (clasp); sec. to board of examrs., Fort William, March, 1871; in addn., asst. sec., legisl. dept., govt. of India, from Aug., 1875; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

JATAR, S. B. (see Shriram Bhikaji Jatar).

JEFFERY, THADDEUS JAMES PASSMORE, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrig. works in Bombay and the Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1879; suptg. engr., Oct., 1894; ret., Feb., 1900.

JEFFREYS, WILLIAM, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 8th June, 1854; arrived, 24th Jan., 1856, and served in the irrign. branch, Punjab, and in the N.W. Prov.; on duty in the public works secretariat, govt. of India, 1864-65; transfed. to Bengal, and depd. to investigate a project of irrign. and navigation for the river Gandak, Oct., 1867; offg. asst. to chief engr., and asst. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1869; suptg. engr., April, 1872; acting chief engr. and joint sec., irrign. branch, March, 1887; ret'd., Nov., 1887.

JEFFRIES, FRANCIS JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Queen Mary's Sch., Walsall, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JEHANGIR DOSABHAI FRAMJI, Barr. (1887), Customs Dept., Bombay (special collr. under L. A. Act, Bombay).—Joined the service April, 1879; asst. collr. of customs, Feb., 1881; collr. of customs and salt rev., Sind, Feb., 1900; special collr. under land acquisition act for city of Bombay, Aug., 1900.

JEHANGIR KAIKHASRO NAVROJI KABRAJI, Statutory C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1888; asst. collr. and mag., March, 1888; confd., 24th Feb., 1890; 2nd asst., May, 1895; 1st asst., July, 1897.

JEHANGIR, P. (see Pestanji Jehangir).

JELF, ARTHUR ROYLANCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (personal asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Eton Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1869, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1903.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of customs, &c., Bombay).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 15th Jan., 1880, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and from June, 1881, in Sind; 3rd asst. collr. in Sind, June, 1885; manager of encumbered estates in Sind, and educ. inspr., Sept., 1885; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1889; 1st asst., Feb., 1892; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindi translr. to govt., March, 1892; junr. collr. and collr. of salt rev., Oct., 1896; senr. collr. and ditto, April, 1900; commr. of customs, salt, opium, &c., July, 1903.

JENKINS, SIR LAWRENCE HUGH, Knt., K.C.I.E., Barr. (1883) (chief justice, Bombay high court).—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, April, 1866; chief justice, high court, Bombay, April, 1899; Knt., Aug., 1899; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

JENKINS, RICHARD ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JENKINS, ROBERT GEDOIN, Col., Indian Army, late Madras Police Dept.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1856; arrived, 8th Feb., 1857; served in the Madras police from July, 1863, and was promoted to supt., 1872; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

JENKINS, THOMAS MORRIS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—Ensign, 2nd March, 1866; served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr. from 28th April, 1874; acted as port officer and collr. of customs, Bassein, in July, 1881; settl. officer, 1884-85; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; ret'd., March, 1899.

JENKINSON, SIR EDWARD GEORGE, K.C.B., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow and Haileybury; apptd. in 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as joint mag. and dep. collr. of Benares, and afterwards of Farukhabad; served through the Mutiny (medal); also as settl. officer, mag. and collr., commr. of rev. in Jhansi, and commr. in Oudh; ret'd., 1880; was private sec. to Earl Spencer when lord-lieut. of Ireland, and asst. under sec. for police and crime, 1882-85; C.B. in 1883, and K.C.B. in 1888.

JENNINGS, JAMES GEORGE, M.A., Educ. Dept., United Prov. (prof. of English Literature, Muir Central Coll.).—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. prof. of English Literature and Logic, Queen's Coll., Benares, 16th Nov., 1892; ditto, Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, Sept., 1895.

JENNINGS, ROBERT HENRY, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., R.E., Foreign Dept. Govt. of India (resdt., W. Rajputana States).—First commn., 12th Sept., 1872; arrived, 1st Dec., 1875, and was employed as asst. engr. and in various acting appts. in the Punjab and Baluchistan; served through Afghan war, 1879-80, and the operations against the Maris, 1880; on boundary settl. duty in Central India, 1883-84; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Simla, July, 1885; district staff officer, Meerut, Sept., 1888; dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Meerut, April, 1890; exec. engr., mil. works dept., June, 1890; acting asst. pol. agent and consul, Bassora, Feb.-Aug., 1892; pol. officer with Sardar Ayub Khan, Feb., 1893; offg. pol. agent, Alwar, April, 1896; confd., Oct., 1896; pol. agent, Malwa, May, 1897; resdt. at Indore, Oct., 1899; resdt., W. Rajputana States, Feb., 1903; C.S.I., Jan., 1904; author of an account of a journey through W. Baluchistan, Persia, &c., and of contributions to Persia and Baluchistan gazetteers.

JENNINGS, WILLIAM ERNEST, Major, Indian M.S. (civil surgn., Sholapur).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1887; in mil. employ till Nov., 1895; health offr. Port of Bombay, Nov., 1895; civil surg., Shikarpur, Sept., 1899; ditto, Sholapur, July, 1900; special medl. offr. for plague operations, Bombay city, Oct., 1900; ditto, Poona, March to Oct., 1902.

JERVOISE, ALAN ARTHUR CLARKE, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 23rd March, 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., pol. agent and collr. of stamp rev., and acting dep. commr. of customs and opium; junr. collr. and mag., Sept., 1877; senr. collr. and mag., March, 1879; ret'd., April, 1887.

JEWETT, THOMAS HOPE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. 22nd May, 1872, and posted to mil. works; on famine duty in Mysore, 1877-78; transfed. to Assam, Sept., 1880; exec. engr., April, 1886.

JNANENDRA NATH GUPTA (see Gupta, J. N.).

JNANENDRA NATH ROY (see Roy, J. N.).

JOGESH CHANDRA MITTER, B.L., late Bengal Judicial Dept.—Dep. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1868; munsif, April, 1870; subordinate judge, July, 1882; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1900, to July, 1902.

JOGINDRA NATH PAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 12th Dec., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

JOHN, HENRY CELESTIN ROBERT, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1884, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

JOHNS, WILLIAM ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Nov., 1891; dep. consg. engr. to govt. of India, May, 1901; ditto, Bombay, June, 1902; employed with Seistan boundary commn. from Dec., 1902.

JOHNSON, SIR ALLEN BAYARD, K.C.B., Gen., Indian Army, late Mil. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Winchester; joined the service (infantry), July, 1846; apptd. to 4th Sikhs as volunteer for service in Burma (medal and clasp for Pegu); in civil employ in Punjab, 1854; served in the Mutiny in 1858 (medal and clasp); asst. adjutant-gen., 1861; mil. dept., Calcutta, 1862; asst. sec. to govt. of India in the mil. dept., 1875; dep. mil. sec., 1876; sec. in the mil. dept., India office, from Jan., 1877; temporarily employed as mil. sec. to the govt. of India, 1879–81; C.B., 1881; ret'd. from India office and was made K.C.B., May, 1889.

JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER THOMAS HEATON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Sedbergh and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JOHNSON, The Most Rev. EDWARD RALPH, D.D., LL.D.—Educ. at Rugby, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; ordained, 1851; vicar of Northenden, Cheshire, 1866–76; archdeacon of Chester, 1871–76; apptd. bishop of Calcutta and metropolitan of India, 14th Dec., 1876, resigned May, 1898.

JOHNSON, EVANS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Nov., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, Rumpu disturbances, 1879–80; sub-collr. and joint mag., S. Arcot, Jan., 1883; collr. and mag., May, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1886; collr. and mag. and agent, Ganjam, May, 1890; ret'd., March, 1893.

JOHNSON, JOHN, India Office (senior clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; junr. clerk, June, 1882; senior clerk, March, 1903.

JOHNSON, The Ven. CHARLES FRANCIS HARDING, M.A., late Ecclesi. Dept., Bombay.—educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; junr. chaplain, 13th Feb., 1869; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1879; archdeacon of Bombay, Oct., 1888; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

JOHNSON, DAVID LANDALE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Boston Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge;

apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Feb., 1897.

JOHNSTON, FREDERICK WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (political agent, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Sept., 1901; pol. agent, Wana, N.W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1901.

JOHNSTON, HENRY JOSEPH, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to the Punjab (irrign.); exec. engr., Jan., 1894.

JOHNSTON, JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Royal Univ. of Ireland and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 19th Nov., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Assam as asst. commr., Jan., 1903.

JOHNSTON, JAMES LEONARD, Barr. (1897), Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 24th Feb., 1897, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSTON, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1873, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; ret'd., April, 1903.

JOHNSTON, JOHN LEECH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1884; jud. commr. and judge of sadar court in Sind, May, 1895; ret'd., May, 1898.

JOHNSTON, THOMAS KENNETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Boston Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSTONE, DONALD CAMPBELL, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, Nov., 1883; district judge, Dec., 1891; divl. judge, Aug., 1895.

JOHNSTONE, FREDERICK JOHN, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Served the Central Prov., from July, 1861, and was apptd. asst. to the chief engr., Aug., 1873; offg. comptr. of public works accts., 1875; transf'd. temporarily to Punjab, Oct., 1879; suptg. engr., Nov., 1880; posted to British Burma, Jan., 1881; transf'd. to Assam as sec. in the public works dept., Jan., 1884; chief engr., June, 1888; chief engr. and joint sec. to the govt. of Bengal, June, 1891; sec., March, 1892; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1893; ret'd., Jan., 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1896.

JOHNSTONE, JAMES WILLIAM DOUGLAS, For. Dept., Govt. of India (inspr.-gen. of educn., Gwalior State).—Apptd. asst. prof., govt. coll., Lahore, 2nd July, 1877; head-master, Mayo Coll., Ajmere, July, 1881; princpl., resdy. coll., Indore, Feb., 1885; tutor to Maharaja Sindia,

Feb., 1890; inspr.-gen. of educ., Gwalior State, Dec., 1894.

JOHNSTONE, JAMES WILLIAM HOPE, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 4th Feb., 1853; from March, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and dep. commr.; offd. as commr. in 1880 and 1883; civil and sessions judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1881; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; ret'd., Feb., 1886.

JOLL, HENRY, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Nov., 1890; ret'd., March, 1892.

JOLLIFFE, EDWIN WILLIAM, India Office (second class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 2nd April, 1885.

JONES, GEORGE EDWARD, M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept. (loco. supt.).—Apptd. to loco. dept., state rlys., May, 1879; asst. loco. supt., Feb., 1883; dist. loco. supt., May, 1901; loco. supt., April, 1903.

JONES, HENRY SYDNEY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to State rlys.; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; lent to Bombay, Baroda, and C.I. Rly. Co. from Jan., 1885.

JONES, JOHN ALFRED, M.I.C.E. (sanitary engr., Madras).—Joined the service as exec. engr., public works dept., Madras municipal commn., Jan., 1877; acted as prest. of the commn. in 1886 and 1888; apptd. sanitary engr. to govt. of Madras, April, 1890; author of 'Switches and Crossings,' and of a manual for district and municipal boards.

JONES, JOHN LLOYD THOMAS, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (offg. assay master, Calcutta mint).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1887; on mly. duty till 1898; supt. of Matheran, May, 1898; dep. assay master, Bombay mint, Jan., 1900; offg. assay master, Calcutta mint, April, 1903.

JONES, OSWALD ROUTH (commr. of police, Madras Town).—Joined the service, 3rd Sept., 1870, under the mil. dept.; served subsequently in Natal, Mauritius, and in England; in April, 1879, was apptd. to Madras as asst. supt. of police; asst. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1886; supt., Aug., 1889; chief presidency mag., March, 1891; commr. of police, Madras Town, Jan., 1900.

JONES, RICHARD GODFREY, Lieut.-Col. Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 13th June, 1874; served with 67th Foot till January, 1878; entered the Staff Corps, January, 1878; inspg. officer, Mysore Imperial Troops, July, 1890; mil. sec. to the rest., Hyderabad, March, 1895; mil. asst. to Mysore govt., 1897.

JONES, RICHARD LLEWELYN, M.A., Madras Educ. Service (profir. Presy. Coll., Madras).—Apptd. prof. of physics, Presy. Coll., Oct., 1889; ditto, and meteorol. reporter to Madras govt., April, 1899.

JONES, THOMAS, Barr. (1871), late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, 7th April, 1866; clerk and offg. 5th judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1873; registrar and chief ministerial officer of the court, June, 1882; 4th judge, Oct., 1884; and 3rd judge, Oct., 1889; ret'd., 1892.

JONES, WILLIAM BRITTAIN, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 15th Sept., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab

as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; from April, 1865, served in the Central Prov. as dep. commr., sec. to the chief commr., and acting commr. in various divns.; on special duty for drawing up land rev. and tenancy bills for Central Prov., Sept., 1874; commr., Hyderabad assigned districts, April, 1877; rest., Hyderabad, June, 1882; chief commr., Central Prov., May, 1883; ret'd., Jan., 1885.

JONES, WILLIAM CHARLES NIGEL, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; in charge, Jowaki field telegraphs, 1877-78 (medal); also employed in connection with Kuram valley field force; supt., Aug., 1887; personal asst. to dir.-gen., 1889-90; ret'd., March, 1901.

JONES, WILLIAM RICHARD SUMPTION, late India Public Works Dept.—Dep. supt., Rurki workshops, Nov., 1867; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1873, as exec. engr., N.W. Prov.; transf. to Rajputana state railways, April, 1879, as dep. locomotive supt.; carriage and wagon supt., Ajmir, January, 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1893.

JOPLING, LINDSAY MILLAIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.

JOPP, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 19th Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; joint judge and sessions judge, Ahmadabad, April, 1892; registrar, high court, Bombay, May, 1893; acting special judge under Deccan agriculturists relief act, Nov., 1893; judge and sessions judge, Ahmednagar, January, 1897; ret'd., Aug., 1901.

JOPP, JOHN, C.B., Col., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., 20th Oct., 1856; served at capture of fort of Sheik Othman, near Aden, in 1858, in the Abyssinian war of 1868 (medal), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); dep. quartermaster-gen., Bombay, May, 1888; pol. rest., &c., Aden, May, 1890; C.B., May, 1894; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1895.

JOSCELYNE, DOUGLAS, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 11th Aug., 1870, and posted to Rajputana; employed in Madras on famine relief, 1878-79; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf. to Burma as asst. sec. in the public works secretariat, July, 1888; under sec., Nov., 1889, to March, 1890; suptg. engr., August, 1895; chief engr., and sec. to govt., Bengal, July, 1899; ret'd., Dec., 1902.

JOSEPH, EUSTACE ALEXANDER ACWORTH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 24th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

JOSEPH, GEORGE JOHN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 23rd May, 1872, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883.

JOSLEN, FRANK, Major, Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (supt. civil veterinary dept., Bombay).—Joined the (home) service, 9th May, 1884; supt., civil veterinary dept., South Punjab, Sept., 1896;

ditto, Rajputana, Nov., 1899; ditto, Bombay, May, 1901.

JOTINDRA NATH ROY (see Roy, J. N.).

JOUBERT, CHARLES HENRY, M.B., Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, United Prov.).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was in mil. employ until 1874; served as civil surg., rest. surg., presidency hosp., offg. supt. of emigration, offg. health officer for Calcutta, and offg. sanitary commr. for Bengal; profr., med. coll., June, 1890; fellow of Calcutta univ., Jan., 1893; princ. med. officer, Lahore dist., March, 1900; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Prov., Oct., 1902.

JOWERS, HAROLD, late Indian C.S., Central Provs.—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

JOYCE, ALFRED, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr., and was employed in the N.W. Prov.; transf'd. to Madras, April, 1883; exec. engr., Jan., 1886; ret'd., March, 1902.

JOYNER, ROBERT BATSON, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; acted as sanitary engr. in 1892; suptg. engr., May, 1894; C.I.E., January, 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1898.

JUDGE, ALFRED STEWART, Customs Dept., Bengal (supt., preventive service and salt dept., Calcutta).—Joined the service, 15th Jan., 1880, as proby. asst. supt. of police; confd., May, 1882; dist. supt. of police, Sept., 1891; collr. of income tax, Calcutta, Feb., 1895; supt., preventive service and salt dept., Calcutta, June, 1896.

JUKES, JOHN EDWIN CLAPHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

JWALA PRASAD, Kunwar, B.A., late Statutory C.S., Bengal.—Apptd. a proby. under the native civil service rules, Dec., 1884; confd., as asst. mag. and collr., Feb., 1887; asst. commr., Nov., 1891; joint mag., Feb., 1896; addl. judge, Dec., 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1898; ret'd., May, 1901; has transl'd. Sir Alfred Lyall's 'Asiatic Studies' into Hindustani.

JWALA PRASAD, Pandit, M.A., Statutory C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Apptd. a proby. under native civil service rules, July, 1886; confd., 12th March, 1889, as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., July, 1900; K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1903.

JYOTSANATH GHOSAL, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Metropolitan Coll., Calcutta; Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895; and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

KABRAJI, J. K. N. (see Jehangir Kaikhasro Navroji Kabraji).

KALI KUMAR BOSE, B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (subordinate judge).—Apptd. munsif, July, 1873; subordinate judge, Nov., 1894.

KALI PADA GUPTA (see Gupta, K. P.).

KALI PRASANNA MUKERJI, B.A., B.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1866, as munsif; subord. judge, Feb., 1879; ret'd., Feb., 1898.

KANHAYA LAL, Rai Bahadur, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 15th Nov., 1879, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1880; exec. engr., Oct., 1893.

KARUNA DAS BOSE, M.A., B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (subordinate judge).—Apptd. munsif, Jan., 1871; subordinate judge, Sept., 1889.

KARUNAMOY BANERJI, B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (subordinate judge).—Apptd. munsif, Aug., 1871; subordinate judge, March, 1892.

KASHINATH KESHO THAKUR, Rao Bahadur, M.A., LL.D. (divl. and sess. judge, Central Prov.).—Joined the service, 3rd Jan., 1887, as extra asst. commr.; dep. commr. and divl. and sess. judge, Oct., 1901.

KASHINATH RAMCHANDRA GODBOLE, B.A., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Entered the dept. as asst. engr., Feb., 1873; exec. engr., Oct., 1885.

KAUL, H. K. (see Hari Kishen Kaul).

KAVASJEE JAMASJEE BADSHAH, B.A., Statutory C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 12th Nov., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1887; dep. dir.-gen. of post office, July, 1896; comptr., post office, April, 1897; postmaster-genl., Bengal, April, 1902, to April, 1903.

KAYE, JAMES LEVETT, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (settl. commr., Jammu and Kashmir).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; asst. resdt., Kashmir, July, 1892; settl. commr., Jammu and Kashmir, March, 1895.

KAYE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileynbury, 1858; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; commr. of Agra, July, 1885, and of Rohilkhand, Nov., 1889; on special duty in connection with the police re-organisation commn., 1890-61; junr. member of board of rev., Nov., 1891; ret'd., April, 1893.

KEALY, EDWARD HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Merwara).—Educ. at Felsted, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. asst. commr., Merwara, March, 1902.

KEANE, MICHAEL, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blackrock Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll. London; appointed after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

KEATINGE, GERALD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay, as asst. collr. and mag.; acting dist. supt. of police, Kanara, Sept.-Dec., 1897, and May-June, 1898; 2nd asst., April, 1902.

KEATINGE, RICHARD HARTE, V.C., C.S.I., Gen., Indian Army.—First commn., Bombay artillery, June, 1842; from 1847 was employed in civil duties on the N.W. frontier, and was apptd. asst. supt., Nimar, Dec., 1851; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp and V.C.), including the pursuit of Tantia Topi, 1858-59; pol. agent, Nimar, 1860; pol. agent, Gwalior, and subsequently in Kathiawar, 1862; served in Kathiawar against the rebel Waghirs in 1865; agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1868-74; also ex-officio chief commr. of Ajmir, 1871-74; chief commr. of Assam, Feb., 1874, to June, 1878; placed on the unemployed supy. list, Jan., 1884.

KEDAR NATH MOZUMDAR, B.A., B.L.—Served in the subord. jud. Dept., Bengal, from 16th Dec., 1865, and as subord. judge, Jan., 1879 to 1900.

KEDAR NATH ROY, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—From 4th April, 1878, served as munsi, and on 27th July, 1888, was apptd. to the statutory civil service as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr.; Oct., 1893; joint mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; mag. and coll., July, 1899; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1900; author of three legal works.

KEEFER, WILLIAM NAPIER, Surgn.-Major, late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 1st Oct., 1869; served in the Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp), the Duffla expdn., 1874-75, the Jowaki campaign, 1877-78 (clasp), the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal and clasp), the Zaimusht expdn., 1879, and the Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal and clasp, bronze star, and 4th class Osmanieh); senr. med. officer, Port Blair, April 1884; retd., Dec., 1886.

KEEGAN, DENNIS FRANCIS, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the Bengal med. service in 1866; acted as residency surgn., Indore, 1877-78; served in the Afghan war, 1879-82 (medal and clasp, bronze star); apptd. residency surgn., Indore, and civil med. officer, Central India, June, 1881; retd., April, 1894; author of several articles and papers on med. subjects.

KEELAN, EUGENE JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Oct., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in Oudh; transf. to state railways, Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1880; retd., 1896.

KEEN, WILLIAM JOHN, Capt., Indian Army (pol. agent, Tochi, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—First commn., 19th Nov., 1892; in military employ till Sept. 1898, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; commdt., border military police, Peshawar, N.W. Frontier Prov., Oct., 1901; pol. agent, Tochi, March, 1902.

KEENE, CHARLES JAMES, C.I.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. traffic supt.).—Joined the service, 29th Dec., 1874, as asst. traffic supt., Punjab N. state railway; served also on the N. Bengal state railway, the N.W. and other railways; district traffic supt., 1890; dep. traffic supt., March, 1901; C.I.E., June, 1903.

KEENE, HENRY G., C.I.E., M.A. (Hon.), late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Oxford; apptd. from Haileybury, 1847; arrived in India, 3rd Oct., 1847; served in the N.W. Prov. as

asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1856; mag. and collr., April, 1860; civil and sessions judge, April, 1867; offd. as commr. of Agra in 1872 and 1875, and of Allahabad in 1874; fellow of the Calcutta univ.; author of 'Indian Administration,' 1867; 'Fall of the Mughal Empire,' 1876; 'History of Hindustan,' 1885; 'Life of Madhava Rao Sindhia,' 1892; 'History of India for the use of Students,' 1893; 'A Servant of John Company,' 1897, &c.; retd., 1883.

KEENE, HENRY GEORGE HARRIS, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acnt.-gen., Burma).—Joined the dept., 14th March, 1874, as asst. acntn.-gen., asst. compr.-gen., and asst. compr., Burma; compr., Hyderabad, March-July, 1886, and from Oct., 1893; dep. acntn.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1902.

KEENE, ROBERT THEAKSTONE, India Public Works Dept. (special engr.).—Apptd. from S. Punjab and Delhi rly. co. as spcl. asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1886, and posted to N.W. rly.; special engr., Nov., 1899.

KEESS, WALTER, M.A., Madras Educl. Dept. (princl. Saidapet Agric. Coll.).—Apptd. principal, Coll. of Agric., Saidapet, Jan., 1897.

KEITH, ROBERT CHARLES STEUART, B.A.—Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch., Edin. Univ., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

KEITH, STANLEY, India Office. (staff clerk, finl. dept.).—Educ. at Lancing Coll.; writer, India Office, 6th June, 1873; clerk, 2nd class, April, 1879; staff clerk, July, 1896; joint editor of the India List, 1885-95, and of the India Office List, 1895-96.

KEITH, WILLIAM JOHN, M.A., India C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sec. to finl. commr., June, 1900.

KELLAKKA COVILAGOM MANAVEDAN RAJA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at the Madras Univ.; apptd. prob. for the civil service, 4th June, 1880; confd., Aug., 1882; asst. collr. and mag., July, 1882; head asst., Aug., 1888; sub. collr. and joint mag., Feb. 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1897.

KELLAWAY, HERBERT FRANK, India Office (2nd class clerk, store dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 27th Dec., 1901.

KELLEY, WILLIAM, India Office (staff clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879; staff clerk; April, 1902.

KELLY, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and asst. supt. of survey; small cause court judge, Jan., 1876; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1879; retd., Jan., 1888.

KELSALL, JOHN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; from March, 1865, to Jan., 1867, served as proby. asst. supt. of police, and subsequently as asst. collr. and mag., and principal asst.; district and sessions judge,

Aug., 1880; compiled the 'Bellary District Manual'; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

KEMBALL, SIR ARNOLD BURROWES, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Addiscombe; 2nd lieut., Bombay artillery, Dec., 1837; served in the Afghan war, 1838-39, and was present at the siege and capture of Ghazni (medal); apptd. asst. to the rest. at Bushire, Feb., 1842; pol. rest., Persian Gulf, May, 1852; consul gen., Bagdad, and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, 1855 to 1873; served in the Persian expdn. of 1857 (medal and clasp, C.B.); served with the Turco-Persian boundary commn., 1873; during the Russo-Turkish war, 1876-77, was British mil. attaché at the headquarters of the Turkish army.

KEMBALL, CHARLES ARNOLD, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (pol. resdt., Bushire).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1881; pol. asst., Aug., 1892; pol. agent, Oct., 1899; acting pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, April, 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

KEMBALL, CHARLES GURDON, Barr. (1864), late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1850; arrived, 25th Nov., 1850; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge and sessions judge, jud. asst. commr. (in Sind), and dep. mag.; collr. and mag., Karachi, Nov., 1861; served as judge and sessions judge, Surat, from Jan., 1866; judge of the high court, Bombay, Aug., 1871; acted as member of council, Bombay, in 1880 and 1881; ret'd., 1885.

KEMBALL, HENRY VERO ROOKE, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., Dec., 1895.

KEMBLE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., Oct., 1873; inspr.-gen. of jails, July, 1876; offg. opium agent, Benares, May, 1882; opium agent, Behar, May, 1884; offg. commr., Patna, May-Nov., 1890; acting member of board of rev., June, 1892; ret'd., April, 1893.

KENDALL, CHARLES HENRY BAYLEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blackheath and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 4th Dec., 1902, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr.

KENDALL, EDWARD AUSTIN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Bedford Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; judge, small cause court, July, 1896; joint mag., July, 1898; ditto, and asst. commr., Feb., 1901.

KENNAWAY, FREDERICK WALTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Dulwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 27th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., under sec. to govt., April, 1902; junr. sec. to finl. commr., May, 1902.

KENNEDY, HARTLEY, C.S.I., late Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the dept., 30th Oct., 1871; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1874; supt., March, 1876; inspr.-genl., Dec., 1896; commr., Jan., 1899; C.S.I., June, 1902; ret'd. Jan., 1904.

KENNEDY, JAMES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 14th Nov., 1863, and served in the

N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1884; ret'd., April, 1890.

KENNEDY, JOSEPH, late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam (1884-86) as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

KENNEDY, MICHAEL, Bombay Police Dept. (supt. of police).—Entered the service, 8th March, 1877, and served as asst. supt. of police; supt., Jan., 1885; 1st grade, April, 1901.

KENNEDY, ROBERT GREIG, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; transf. to Baluchistan, Oct., 1884; returned to the Punjab, Aug., 1890; suptg. engr., Feb., 1898.

KENNEDY, ROBERT MUIRHEAD, Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 16th Nov., 1877, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag., Bandra; 1st asst., May, 1891; in 1891 acted as pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, and as administrator, Rajpipla state; talukdari settlt. officer, Nov., 1891; on depn. to adjudicate on Cutch Morvi, &c., disputes, June, 1894-June, 1896; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1896; senr. collr., June, 1897; acting commr. of customs, June, 1898; actg. sec. to govr., pol. and jud. depts., February, 1899; collr. and mag., May, 1899; collr. of land rev., Oct., 1899; commr., April, 1902.

KENNEDY, THOMAS JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at the Liverpool Institute, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 20th Nov., 1880, and served in the Punjab and Central Prov. (1885-87) as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; district judge, April, 1892; dep. commr., December, 1894; divl. judge, August, 1896.

KENNEDY, WILLOUGHBY PITCAIRN, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Pol. Dept., Bombay (agent to the Governor in Kathiawar).—First commn., 8th Feb., 1870; from March, 1875, served as asst. to pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Aden, Zanzibar, Baluchistan, and Kathiawar; asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Aug.-Oct., 1880; special pol. agent, Cambay, 1890-94; second asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1893; administrator, Rajpipla state, Oct., 1894; administrator, Navanagar state, July, 1895; pol. agent, Kathiawar, March, 1901.

KENNEY, HERBERT, ARTHUR ROBERT, Col., late Madras Cavalry.—Cornet, 20th Sept., 1859; arrived, 31st Oct., 1859; mil. sec. to the govr. of Madras, May, 1881, to April, 1884; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Dec., 1881; sec. to the govt. of Madras, mil. dept., Dec., 1885; ret'd., April, 1892.

KENSINGTON, ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 12th Feb., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and offg. under sec.; settlt. officer, April, 1884; on special duty in Ambala settlt., Dec., 1889; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and com-

merce dept., April, 1890; retransfd. to Punjab as dep. commr., Dec., 1894; divl. judge, March, 1896; transfd. to N.W.P. and Oudh as acctnt.-gen., class III, June, 1896; returned to Punjab as divl. judge, Nov., 1897.

KENYON, EUSTACE ALBAN. India Telegraph Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1880, and posted to Calcutta; supt., May, 1896.

KER, JAMES CAMPBELL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Irvine Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

KER, THOMAS, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 16th May, 1874, and employed on state railways in Rajputana; exec. engr., May, 1883; retd., May, 1894; K.-i-H. medal, Nov., 1901.

KERR, JOHN HENRY, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dept. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1900.

KERR, THOMAS COULTER, Capt., Store Dept., India Office (surveyor of shipping).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll.; served during China war, 1860-61, and Abyssinian war, 1868-69, in command of hired troopships; surveyor of shipping, India office, 1st April, 1881.

KERSHASP, PIROZESHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bombay University and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 20th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

KERSHAW, LOUIS JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Belfast Methodist Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 31st Oct., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1892; dep. commr., April, 1898.

KETTLEWELL, ARTHUR BRADLEY, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 31st Oct., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. offr., Wano, Oct., 1898, to Jan., 1899; dep. commr., Nov., 1902.

KEYS, ARTHUR INNES, India Office (clerk, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, London office, Indo-European telegraph dept., 7th Feb., 1880; transfd. to registry and record dept., India office, Jan., 1889; clerk specially attached, Aug., 1891; rejoined Indo-European telegraph dept., June, 1894.

KEYSER, ALFRED, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch., and Royal Acad., Gosport; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 29th Oct., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Surat, Dec., 1884; senr. collr., &c., April, 1887; acting commr., S. divn., March, 1890; retd., March, 1892.

KHANDALAVALA, N. D. (see Navroji Dorabji Khandalavala).

KHANDERAO CHIMANRAO VEDARKAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., LL.B., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 25th April,

1863, as Gujarati and Marathi translr. and interpreter, high court; dep. registrar and sealer, Feb., 1867; services lent to the Maharaia Holkar, Oct., 1890; judge, Bombay small cause court, Dec., 1890, continuing to be in foreign service; retd., Jan., 1896.

KHANDUBHAI GULABHAI DESAI, L.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th April, 1870, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884 to 1902; is a fellow of the Bombay univ.

KHAREGHAT, M. P. (see Mancherji Pestanji Khareghat).

KHARSEDJI MANEKJI KHARSEDJI, Khan Bahadur, B.A., Barr. (1869) (judge, small cause court, Bombay).—Served from 29th May, 1874, as judge of the court of small causes, Ahmadnagar, and subsequently at Ahmadabad; judge, Bombay small cause court, June, 1888.

KHARSEDJI RATANJI BOMANJI, Statutory C.S. (junr. collr. and pol. agent, Bombay).—Probr. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1882; confd., 8th Jan., 1885; 1st asst., Jan., 1895; forest settlt. offr., April, 1896; junr. collr., and collr. and mag., April, 1900; pol. agent, Jath and Daphlapur state, Jan., 1903.

KHUSHWAKT RAE, RAE, Barr. (1887), United Prov. admn. (dep. collr.).—Apptd. proby. asst. mag. and collr., under the native civil service rules, 8th April, 1890; dep. collr., April, 1894.

KIERNANDER, CHARLES ROBERT CAMPBELL, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept. 5th Aug., 1864, and served as asst. and dep. acctnt.-gen., inspr. of local offices of acct., and dep. comptr.-gen.; asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., 1884-85; comptr. of Indian treasuries, April, 1885; dep. auditor-gen., Oct., 1886; dep. comptr.-gen., April, 1891; retd., March, 1892.

KILBY, GEORGE CHARLES, Barr. (1871), late Bengal Jud. Service.—Dep. supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, 28th Feb., 1876; retd., Sept., 1896.

KILBY, REGINALD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

KILBY, SANFORD JAMES, late Bengal Preventive Service.—Asst. collr. of customs, Chittagong, 30th June, 1865; dep. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1866; from Aug., 1868, served in the police dept.; on famine relief duty, 1873 and 1876; from Dec., 1876, served in the customs preventive service, and also in Madras as dep. commr., abkari and salt dept.; retd., Nov., 1892.

KILGOUR, FREDERICK, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—First commn., 11th July, 1857; proby. asst. supt. of police, Nov., 1865; asst. supt., Aug., 1873; supt., June, 1874; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., July, 1895.

KINCAID, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, Indian C.S. (judl. asst., Kathiawar).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1891, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1897; judl. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, June, 1900.

KINCAID, WILLIAM, Major-Gen., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Madras army, March, 1849; apptd. to adjust boundaries' disputes, Bhopal agency, March, 1864; asst. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, and small cause court judge and cantonmt. mag., Nowgong, 1866; pol. agent, Bhopal, Aug., 1876; pol. agent, Bhopawar, and commandant, Malwa Bhil corps, Oct., 1879; pol. agent, Bhopal, June, 1881; returned to Europe, 1887.

KING, CARLETON MOSS, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Brighton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 15th Nov., 1902, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

KING, CHARLES MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Dec., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1901.

KING, SIR GEORGE, K.C.I.E., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1865; house surgn., med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, 1866; supt. of royal botanical gardens, proftr. of botany in Calcutta med. coll., and supt. of cinchona plantation in British Sikhimm, from July, 1871; also quinelogist, Dec., 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; in addn., dir. of the botanical survey of India, May, 1891; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1898; retd., Feb., 1898; author of 'Manual of Cinchona Cultivation in India,' and other botanical works; and editor of 'Annals of the Royal Botanical Gardens, Calcutta.'

KING, JOSHUA, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 26th Oct., 1860, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec. to govt.; talukdari settlt. officer, 1874-76 and 1876-77; on special duty, Baroda, 1877-79; junr. collr. and mag., Ahmadnagar, May, 1879; senr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Satara, May, 1884; retd., 1886.

KING, LUCAS BLACKER BURNET, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 11th Oct., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served in Bengal in various grades and as supt. of survey, and district and sessions judge; retd., August, 1885.

KING, LUCAS WHITE, C.S.I., B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Ennis Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 27th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transfed. to foreign dept. as offg. asst. commr., Ajmir, June, 1885; pol. agent, Oct., 1885; asst. to rest., Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, July, 1887; on special duty with Maharaja of Mysore, Dec., 1887; first asst. to agent for Central India, Feb.-July, 1888; retransfed. to Punjab as asst. commr., Sept., 1890; also pol. officer, Shirani field force, Oct., 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1893; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., 1894-95; pol. officer with Tirah expedy. force, Oct., 1897-Jan., 1898 (medal and three clasps); C.S.I., May, 1898; author of a history of the Barakzai coinage of Afghanistan.

KING, MAURICE, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil

Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 20th Jan., 1868, and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., April, 1876; transfed. to Burma as suptg. engr., May, 1893; re-transfed. to N.W. Prov., Nov., 1896; retd., March, 1898.

KING, WALTER GAWEN, C.I.E., M.B., C.M., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (sanitary commr., Madras).—Arrived, 31st Oct., 1874, and served under the mil. dept. in Madras and Burma; apptd. civil surgn., June, 1877; also supt. of jail, March, 1878; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1892; acting supt., Madras lunatic asylum, April, 1893; sanitary commr., June, 1895; C.I.E., June, 1899.

KING, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Bengal; transfed. to mil. works, April, 1872; exec. engr., Sept., 1879; retransfed. to Bengal, 1879; employed in Baluchistan, 1885-88, and acted as joint sec. to govr.-gen.'s agent; retd., Sept., 1894.

KING-HARMAN, MONTAGUE JOCELYN, Col., Indian Army, late Mil. Secretariat, Govt. of India.—First commn., 2nd Oct., 1860; served in Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal), and Lushai expdn., 1871-72; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, 1877; dep. sec., mil. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1892, till Aug., 1897; placed on unemployed supy. list, Mar., 1900.

KINGSFORD, DOUGLAS HOLLINSHEAD, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1902.

KINGSMILL, JAMES, late Central Press, Bombay.—Gen. supt. of the central press, and editor of the 'Bombay Govt. Gazette,' March, 1872-1891.

KINNEY, ALEXANDER.—Apptd. dep. administrator-gen. of Bengal, 1st April, 1903.

KINSMAN, FREDERICK, B.Sc., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; supt., Sept., 1886; personal asst. to dir.-gen., Feb.-May, 1888; in charge, Upper Burma divn., May, 1893; chief supt., March, 1897; retd., March, 1900.

KIPLING, JOHN LOCKWOOD, C.I.E., late Educn. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the govt. service as architectural sculptor, Bombay, May, 1865; from April, 1875, served in the Punjab as principal of Mayo sch. of industrial art, and curator, Lahore museum; retd., May, 1893.

KIRAN CHANDRA, DE (see De K. C.).

KIRK, HENRY ALEXANDER, late India Telegraph Dept. (dir.-in-chief, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept., India Office).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; supt., March, 1876; served during the Afghan war, 1879-80 (mentioned in desp., medal and clasp); in charge telegraph workshops, Calcutta, Oct., 1885; chief supt., Sept., 1896; director, May, 1900; retd. April, 1902; apptd. dir.-in-chief, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept., India Office, April, 1902.

KIRKPATRICK, HENRY CLARE, Barr. (1883) (reporter, Bombay high court).—Joined the service 8th Jan., 1878, and served as reporter to the high court.

KIRKWOOD, TOWNSEND MOLLOY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint

mag. and dep. collr., and district and sessions judge; also in the public works dept., 1869-71, and was on famine duty, Bhagalpur, 1874; ret'd., 1883.

KIRTON, WILLIAM FERDINAND, Indian C.S. (asst. sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Trinidad, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1898; joint mag., Dec., 1901; asst. sess. judge, Jan., 1903.

KISCH, HERMANN MICHAEL, M.A., Barr. (1883), Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 24th Oct., 1873; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1873; on famine duty in 1874 and 1877; asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, Feb., 1875; offg. under sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., May, 1878; under sec., jud. and pol. depts., Jan., 1880; offg. sec. in the finl. dept., June, 1881; offg. under sec., govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Dec., 1883; postmr.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1885; offd. as dep. sec., fin. and commerce dept., in 1889, and as dir. gen. of post office of India in 1890-91 and 1893-95; on depn. to Vienna postal congress, 1891; dep. dir.-gen. of post office, Feb., 1897; on depn. to Washington postal congress, 1897; postmaster-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1900; commr., April, 1902; author of statistical accts. of Chittagong and other districts.

KISSORY MOHUN CHATTERJEE, B.A., Barr. (1873), late Bengal judl. dept.—Offd. as judge of Calcutta small cause court in 1888 and 1889, and was confd. in that post, 3rd Oct., 1889; ret'd., April, 1902; author of a legal treatise on the transfer of immovable property.

KITCHIN, ARTHUR JAMES WARBURTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 4th Jan., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

KITTS, EUSTACE JOHN, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs. and Oudh).—Educ. at Portland Gr. Sch., Mill Hill Gr. Sch., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 29th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and in Berar as asst. commr. and small cause court judge; on famine duty in Madras, 1877-78; asst. commr., Ajmir, 1883; from April, 1884, served in the N.W. Prov. as joint mag., asst. commr., and small cause court judge; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; ret'd., Dec., 1901; author of 'Berar Census Report' (1882), and 'Castes and Tribes found in India' (1885).

KNAPP, ARTHUR ROWLAND, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Madras).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 20th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1899.

KNIGHT, RAYMONT, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Coll., Stony Stratford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Jan., 1885, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and forest settlt. officer; 1st asst., May, 1896; judge and sessions judge, Feb., 1897.

KNOWLYS, HENRY BOLD, late Berar Commn.—Served as a writer in the Ceylon civil service, Feb., 1859, to Aug., 1866; from Sept., 1866, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as asst. commr.; small cause court judge, Sept., 1873; dep. commr., May, 1886; ret'd., July, 1891.

KNOX, GEORGE, late Punjab Administration.—Asst. engr., May, 1854; from June, 1856, served in the Punjab commn. as personal asst. to commr., dep. commr., and offg. commr.; ret'd., 1890.

KNOX, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian C.S. (judge, high court, N.W. Prov.).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; from March, 1877, served in the jud. dept. as small cause court judge, and district and sessions judge; member of the provl. legisl. council, 1886 and 1889; legal remembrancer, Sept., 1887; judge of the high court, N.W. Prov., Dec., 1890; author of a 'Digest of the Criminal Law of the Bengal Presidency,' a 'Digest of Civil Procedure,' 'The Legal Remembrancer,' 1879-82, and 'Treatise upon the Indian Stamp Law.'

KNOX, HORACE CHALONER, M.I.C.E., late Indian Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1885; ret'd., 1898.

KNOX, HUBERT THOMAS, Barr. (1868), late Madras C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 4th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., joint sessions judge for trying Rumpa cases, and asst. commr. (Nilgiris); sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1887; collr. and mag., Nov., 1888; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

KNOX, KENNETH NEVILLE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 18th Nov., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

KNYVETT, ALEXANDER VANSITTART (dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Bengal).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1867, as asst. supt. of police; district supt., Dec., 1876; personal asst. to the inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1892; dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1899.

KNYVETT, CHARLES FREDERICK (dep. inspr.-gen. of police, United Prov.).—Joined the service, 25th June, 1869, as acting asst. district supt.; confd., Oct., 1873; district supt., Dec., 1878; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Jan., 1895.

KNYVETT, WILLIAM LEYCESTER NATHANIEL, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., Bengal.—First commn., 10th June, 1854; served in the dept. from June, 1863, as district supt.; dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1885; offg. inspr.-gen., July, 1888; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

KONSTAM, EDWIN MAX, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1896; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1898; ret'd., Sept. 1902.

KOUGH, CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Kingstown, Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam.

of 1865; arrived, 16th Dec., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., head asst., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; forest settlt. officer, March, 1884; collr. and mag., Nov., 1887; ret'd., July, 1894.

KRISHNA CHANDRA BANDOPADHYA (see Bandopadhy, K. C.).

KRISHNA GOVINDA GUPTA, Barr. (1873), Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Maimansingh Zilla Sch., Pogose Sch., Dacca, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 24th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1874; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1884; mag. and collr., Oct., 1889; junr. sec. to the board of rev., May, 1890; commr. of excise, Bengal, March, 1893; mag. and collr., March, 1895; commr., May, 1901.

KRISHNA LAL DATTA (asst. acct.-gen., Bengal).—Joined the service, March, 1881; chief supt., compt. and audr. gen.'s office, May, 1891; asst. compt. gen., Calcutta, April, 1896; asst. acct.-gen., and inspr. of local fund accts., Madras, May, 1900, ditto, and examr. of ditto, Bengal, March, 1903.

KRISHNAJI BALWANT WAGLE (see Wagle, K. B.).

KUCHLER, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A. (prof., Presy. Coll., Calcutta).—Apptd. prof., presy. coll., Calcutta, March, 1835; has held professorships at Patna Coll., and at the Sibpur Engrg. Coll.

KYNOCH, GEORGE COLLINGWOOD, late Burma customs.—Joined the service, June, 1877, and served as asst. sec. to chief commr.; chief collr. of customs, April, 1896; ret'd., April, 1900.

LACE, JOHN HENRY, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Bengal).—Joined the service, 25th Dec., 1881, as asst. consr. of forests; dep. consr., July, 1890; asst. inspr.-gen. and supt. of working plans, Oct., 1900; consr., March, 1902.

LAINE, ABRAHAM JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

LAING, JAMES ANDERSON, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 1st April, 1870; arrived, 26th Dec., 1870, and served as civil surgn. and supt. of jail, and acting rest. surgn. at the gen. hosp., Madras; dep. sanitary commr. and inspr. of vaccn., Feb., 1878; commr., June, 1888; ret'd., Sept., 1895.

LAKSHMAN GOPAL DESHMUKH, B.A., Statutory C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under native civil service rules, 16th April, 1881, and asst. collr. and mag.; confd., 19th June, 1885; 1st asst., Jan., 1895.

LAL, B. M. (see Brij Mohan Lal).

LAL, K. (see Kanhaya Lal).

LAL, R. (see Rajan Lal).

LAL, S. (see Sri Lal).

LALA, M. (see Mulraj Lala).

LALKALA, C. J. (see Cowasji Jamshedji Lalkaka).

LAMB, RICHARD AMPHLETT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Highgate Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Oct., 1879, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, Khandesh, March, 1885; served in Burma as dep. commr.,

1887-90 (medal with clasp); 1st asst. collr. and mag., Aug., 1892; joint collr. and collr. and mag., Sept., 1896; acting chairman, Poona plague committee, October, 1897; senr. collr., April, 1899; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

LAMBERT, GEORGE BANCROFT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

LAMBERT, GEORGE BUCHANAN, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; entered the service, Oct., 1887, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1891.

LAMBERT, SIR JOHN, K.C.I.E., late Police Dept., Bengal.—Served from 23rd Nov., 1863, as asst. and district supt.; visitor of the presidency jail, and of the lunatic asylums, July, 1872; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1874; served under the govt. of India, foreign dept., from March, 1882, to March, 1884, as offg. gen. supt. of the operations for the suppression of thagi and dakaity; acted as commr., Calcutta, and prest. of the boiler commn., 1885-86; commr. of police, Calcutta, April, 1889; member of provl. legisl. council, June, 1892; K.C.I.E., June, 1893; ret'd., May, 1897.

LAMBOURN, GEORGE ERNEST, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Oxford High School and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 17th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Dec., 1902.

LAMINGTON (BARON), RIGHT HON. CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E. (govr. of Bombay).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; asst. private sec. to Marquess of Salisbury when prime minister, 1885-86; M.P. for St. Pancras, 1886-90; govr. of Queensland, 1895-1901; G.C.M.G., 1900; govr. of Bombay and G.C.I.E., 12th Dec., 1903.

LANCASHIRE, JOSEPH KENNETH, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Currie Sch., Folkestone, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

LANE, CLAYTON TURNER, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Berar Commn.—Ensign, Bengal army, 11th Dec., 1858; from March, 1863, served in the Punjab as asst. supt. and district supt. of police; transf. to Berar police, Sept., 1865; inspr.-gen. of police, &c., Hyderabad assigned districts, April, 1877; C.I.E., May, 1895; rank of dep. commr., June, 1895; placed on unemployed supy. list, Feb., 1899.

LANE, JAMES HORSBURGH, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 1st Feb., 1857; supt., 1st grade, Bombay, Aug., 1870; dir. of traffic, Feb., 1879; dep. dir.-gen., April, 1890; offg. dir.-gen., 1892; ret'd., June, 1894; compiler of 'Word Code for Foreign Telegrams.'

LANE, JOHN MACDONALD, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 22nd Sept., 1862; supt., Bengal, Oct., 1869; served subsequently in the Malabar Coast, Nagpur, British Burma, &c., divns.; ret'd., April, 1891.

LANE, WILMOT, Barr. (1868), late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Hailey-

bury, 1854; arrived, 13th Jan., 1855, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr., and in Oudh as offg. dep. commr., Lucknow; mag. and collr., Jan., 1872; civil and sessions judge, Aug., 1877; commr., Sept., 1880; offd. as member, board of rev., N.W. Prov., 1883; ret'd., April, 1889.

LANG, ARTHUR MOFFATT, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1852; arrived, July, 1854, and served in the Punjab; exec. engr., March, 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); asst. to chief engr., Oudh, April, 1858; asst. sec., public works dept., Oudh, July, 1865; principal, Thomason coll., 1871-77; suptg. engr., dep. inspr.-gen., mil. works, and sec., defence committee, Feb., 1879; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, public works dept., Feb., 1881; chief engr., and sec. to chief commr., British Burma, public works dept., Jan., 1883; joint sec., buildings and roads and railway branches, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1885; ret'd., Dec., 1888.

LANG, BASIL.—Offd. as advocate-gen., Bombay High Court, May-Nov., 1891, and from April, 1892; confd., 23rd Oct., 1893; resgd., Dec., 1901.

LANG, JOHN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec. to govt.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1896; political agent, Dec., 1900; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; mag. and collr., June, 1902.

LANG, RICHARD TICKELL MONTGOMERY, Col., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 20th Nov., 1859; from May, 1862, served in the Punjab as asst. and dep. commr.; offg. commr., 1881-82; ret'd., March, 1895.

LANG, ROBERT DACRES MENZIES, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1894; ret'd. Dec., 1903.

LANGLEY, ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 10th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

LANGLEY, EDWARD HARTLEY RAYNSFORD, B.A. (Dub.), M.R.C.S. (Lon.), Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Bombay med. service, 23rd July, 1858, and was employed under the mil. dept., and as acting civil surgn. in various stations; served in China in 1860-62; presidency surgn. and marine surgn., Bombay, Oct., 1876; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

LANSLOWNE (MARQUESS OF), RIGHT HON. HENRY CHARLES KEITH FITZMAURICE, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., D.C.L. (sec. of state for foreign affairs).—Born, 1845; educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; succeeded to the marquisate in 1866; a lord of the treasury, 1868-72; under sec. of state for war, 1872-74; under sec. of state for India, April to July, 1880; govr.-gen. of Canada, May, 1883; apptd. govr.-gen. of India, and assumed office, 10th Dec., 1888; returned to England, 27th Jan., 1894; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1888; K.G., 1894; sec. of state for war in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895, to Nov., 1900; sec. of state for foreign affairs, Nov., 1900.

LARGE, PHILIP TOWNSEND SOMERVILLE, B.C.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Bombay; transf. to state railways, Punjab, Jan., 1870; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; employed on Bengal-Nagpur railway, Oct., 1884; services lent to Gwalior state, Oct., 1892; suptg. engr., Feb., 1893; ret'd., April, 1897.

LARKINS, ARTHUR BROOKS, India Telegraph Dept. (dep. dir.-gen.).—Apptd. asst. supt., 30th Oct., 1869, and employed on various lines, including Tavoy-Siam line; supt., March, 1886; dir., construction branch, April, 1895; dep. dir.-gen., Aug., 1901.

LARMINIE, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as proby. asst. engr.; exec. engr., Sept., 1877; suptg. engr., Aug., 1894; ret'd., Feb., 1901.

LARYMORE, ALEXANDER DOUGLAS, late Jail Dept., Bengal.—Served in the Behar cavalry as trooper, 1857-58; joined the Bengal police as asst. supt., 31st July, 1862; district supt., June, 1872; on famine relief duty, 1874; jail supt., Jan., 1877; has offd. as inspr.-gen. of jails; ret'd., Nov., 1898.

LAST, WALTER ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Westminster, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. settl. officer, Sept., 1894; settl. officer, Oct., 1896; joint mag., Dec., 1896.

LATHAM, FRANCIS LAW, M.A., Barr. (1864), late Jud. Service, Bombay.—Offd. as remembrancer of legal affairs, 1872-73, and as advocate-gen., and judge of the high court, Bombay, for various periods between 1880 and 1884; advocate-gen., Bombay, March, 1884, to Oct., 1893.

LATIF, A. Q. A. (see Abdullatif Qamr-ud-din Amir-ud-din Latif).

LA TOUCHE, SIR JAMES JOHN DIGGES, K.C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (Lieut.-Governor, United Prov.).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer, till Dec., 1871, when he was transf. as settl. officer to Ajmir; offg. dep. commr., Ajmir; returned to the N.W. Prov., 1875, and served as district supt. of police, settl. officer, &c.; from Aug., 1886, served in Upper Burma as commr.; rejoined the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as mag. and collr., April, 1890; member of provl. legis. council, Dec., 1891; commr. and agent, Benares, April, 1893; chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; member, board of rev., Dec., 1896; addl. member of viceroy's legis. council, Oct., 1897, and again Jan., 1899; offg. lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., May to Nov., 1898; confd., Dec., 1901; K.C.S.I., Nov., 1901; author of 'Gazetteer of Ajmir-Mairwara.'

LA TOUCHE, JAMES NORMAN DIGGES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to Cent. Provs.; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Calcutta, April, 1895; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1898.

LA TOUCHE, THOMAS HENRY DIGGES, B.A., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India (supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., geol. survey, Nov., 1881; dep. supt., April, 1887; supt., July, 1894; has written several publications of the geol. survey.

LA TOUCHE, WILLIAM PAGET, Lieut.-Gen., Indian Army, late Salt Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay army, Sept., 1854; served in the Persian war, 1856, and the Mutiny campaign; apptd. asst. mag., Dec., 1859, and asst. district supt. of police, Jan., 1865; acting pol. supt., Palanpur, 1867; from Dec., 1867, served as asst. and district supt. of police; and from Feb., 1883, as dep. collr. of salt rev.; returned to Europe, 1887.

LAUDERDALE (EARL OF), Rt. HON. FREDERICK HENRY MAITLAND, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C.—First commn., 4th Hussars, Feb., 1861; served in Central India in civil employ from 1869 as boundary officer, and pol. asst.; 2nd asst. agent, Indore, 1877; 1st asst., 1878; addl. pol. agent and supt., Charkhari, 1880; ret'd., Jan., 1886.

LAUGHLIN, ROBERT CAMPBELL, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., Oct., 1866; held charge of various divns., chiefly in Madras and Bengal, and of the telegraph stores, Calcutta; ret'd., May, 1891.

LAUGHTON, DANIEL WILSON, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Berar Commn.—First commn., Jan., 1854; arrived, April, 1854; from Jan., 1865, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as asst. commr., small cause court judge, and asst. commt. mag.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn., 1873; dep. commr., Feb., 1885; ret'd., May, 1886.

LAURIE, HENRY MONTAGUE, B.A., Barr. (1887), Indian C.S. (chief sec. to chief commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1887, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; acted as commr. of excise in 1892; dep. commr., June, 1895; 2nd sec. to chief commr., April, 1900; chief sec. to ditto, March, 1903.

LAURIE, LEWIS KOSUTH, Barr. (1876), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 27th Oct., 1873, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and in various acting appts.; under sec., Oct., 1885; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, Jan., 1890; dep. commr., Dec., 1890; commr. of excise, Jan., 1891; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, April, 1892; sec. to chief commr., April, 1895; commr., Sept., 1895; author of a digest of rulings of jud. commrs., Central Prov., in civil cases (1881), and some manuals relating to the Central Prov.; ret'd., Jan., 1900.

LAURIE, MAXWELL, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., April, 1901.

LAW, SIR EDWARD FITZGERALD, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., Govt. of India (finl. member of govr.-gen.'s council).—Entered the Royal Artillery as lieutenant, 1868; served in the Suakin campaign of 1885; entered the diplomatic service in 1887 as commercial attaché at St. Petersburg;

minister resident at Athens, 1898; K.C.M.G., May, 1898; member of the council of the Ottoman debt, at Constantinople, Jan., 1899; financial member of the govr.-gen.'s council, March, 1900; C.S.I., Jan., 1903.
