WOOD, RICHARD BOARDMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; temp. transf. to Bombay on famine duty, July, 1900.

WOOD, SAMUEL GEORGE, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the accounts estabt. of the public works dept. as accountant, Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Provinces; dep. examr., Jan., 1882; examr., May, 1887; ret'd., June, 1902.

WOOD, WALTER GUNNELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1894; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1897.

WOODBURN, ARCHIBALD FRANCIS, Barr. (1882), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Ayr Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. collr., June, 1885; 1st asst., Sept., 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Hyderabad (Sind), Feb., 1893; collr. and mag. and dist. registrar, March, 1897; acting commr., Jan., 1898; acting chief sec., plague dept., April, 1899; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

WOODCOCK, ELBOROUGH MARTIN, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived as ensign, 9th March, 1856; served with the Persian expedy. force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp); apptd. to Oudh mil. police, 27th Nov., 1858; from March, 1860, served in the dept. as district supt.; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1892.

WOODMAN, HENRY CHARLES, B.A., India C.S. (under sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. depts., Bengal).—Educ. at Durham Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 18th Jan., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. depts., Aug., 1901.

WOODROFFE, JAMES TISDALL, Barr. (1860).—Apptd. advocate-gen., Calcutta, Dec., 1899; member, legisl. council, Bengal, Jan., 1900, and Jan., 1902.

WOODROFFE, JOHN GEORGE (Barr. 1889).—Apptd. standing counsel, Calcutta High Court, 1st April, 1903.

WOODS, ALBERT EDWARD, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., New Cross, and Sandhurst; entered the army, 22nd Oct., 1881; joined the Assam commn., 12th Aug., 1889, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1893.

WOODWARD, HILLERSDEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and Birmingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 28th Oct., 1871, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, Sholapur, Nov., 1883; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1885; 1st asst., July, 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892; senr. collr., July, 1896; ret'd., July, 1898.

WOODWARD, WILLOUGHBY, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 26th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag.

and district registrar, Panch Mahals, and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Aug., 1881; senr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Satara, March, 1886; retd., March, 1890.

WOOTTON, JOHN JOSEPH, India Office (staff clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 20th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, May, 1901.

WORDSWORTH, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Educl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the educt. service, May, 1861; arrived, 10th July, 1861, and served as head-master of the Elphinstone instn., Bombay, acting prof. in the Elphinstone coll., and asst. inspr. of schs.; from May, 1862, served as principal of the Deccan coll., Poona; principal and prof., Elphinstone coll., Nov., 1874; also sheriff of Bombay, Dec., 1885; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retd., 1890.

WORGAN, JOHN BERNEY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 16th Dec., 1856; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; from May, 1876, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, 1st grade, July, 1879; retd., Dec., 1891.

WRAY, JOHN WILLOUGHBY, Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Staff Corps, late Bombay Pol. Dept.—First commn., 12th Nov., 1870; entered pol. employ as asst. pol. agent, Cutch, May 1877; acting asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1885; pol. agent, Kolhapur, and southern Mahrattra country, April, 1897; pol. agent, Cutch, June, 1900; retd., March, 1902.

WRIGHT, FREDERICK CHARLES BUCKLEY, India Office (senr. clerk, store dept.).—Junr. clerk, 4th July, 1867; senr. clerk, June, 1891.

WRIGHT, HENRY NELSON, Indian C.S. (registrar, high court, N.W. Prov.).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1895; under sec. to govt., April, 1897 to Jan., 1899; registrar, high court, Feb., 1901; author of 'Notes on the Coins of the Sultans of Delhi.'

WRIGHT, JAMES MCCALLUM, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Fordyce Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov. 1890; asst. commr., April-Oct., 1894; dep. commr., Sept., 1896; mag. and collr., April, 1898.

WRIGHT, JAMES WALKER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. in July, 1859; served in the Punjab as asst. and exec. engr.; transf. to the Punjab provl. list, Nov., 1879; suptg. engr., July, 1886; offg. chief engr., and sec. to chief commr. of Burma, April, 1890; offg. chief engr., buildings and roads branch, and joint sec. to govt. of Punjab, Sept., 1890; sec. to chief commr. of Assam, June, 1891; retd., Jan., 1893.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. proby. asst. engr., Oudh, 23rd Sept., 1864; confd., 1st April, 1865; exec. engr., March, 1871; lent to Cawnpore municipality, Sept., 1884; services lent to Rampur state, Jan., 1890; suptg. engr., May, 1891; retd. 1899.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Educl. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Corpus

Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd., 7th Aug., 1867, and served as prof., Benares and Muir colls.; principal, Bareilly coll., Dec., 1873; prof., Muir coll., Jan., 1877; principal, Benares coll., Oct., 1888; retd. July, 1897; has publ. a handbook of the educ. dept., an account of the Muir coll., and various educt. works.

WROUGHTON, ROBERT CHARLES (inspr.-gen. of forests).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1871; served as asst., and dep. conservator of forests; conservator, Dec., 1895; offg. inspr.-gen. of forests to govt. of India, April, 1902; confd., Nov., 1902.

WYBROW, GEORGE DIGBY, A.M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served in Madras as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1892; retd., July, 1895.

WYER, FREDERICK, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Dec., 1861; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, May, 1884; offd. as commr. in 1883 and 1885; retd., May, 1887.

WYER, THOMAS ROWLAND, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. of Glasgow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; asst. commr., Nov., 1884; joint mag., March, 1888; settlt. officer in addn., March, 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891; mag. and collr., May, 1894; retd., Nov., 1900.

WYLIE, HENRY, C.S.I., Major-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Oct., 1861; arrived, 15th Nov., 1861; and took part in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1863, the 2nd Yusufzai expdn. (medal and clasp), the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (clasp), the Abyssinian expdn., 1868 (medal), and the Hazara campaign, 1868 (clasp); on special duty in Baluchistan, with Major R. Sandeman, 1875-77; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, 1877; served in the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); C.S.I., Feb., 1881; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, 1882; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; Jhalawar, April, 1885; Bhopal, March, 1887; rest., Nepal, Oct., 1893; offg. agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Feb., 1898; retd., April, 1900.

WYLLIE, SIR WILLIAM HUTT CURZON, K.C.I.E., M.V.O., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state for India).—Ensign 106th Foot, Oct., 1866; arrived, Feb., 1867; served in Oudh, 1870-79, as asst. commr.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1875, and as junr. sec. to govt., 1876-77; from Jan., 1879, served under the foreign dept., as cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, asst. commr. in Merwara and Ajmir, and as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; served in Madras, 1880-81, as mil. sec. to the govr., and acted as private sec.; C.I.E., May, 1881; pol. agent, in charge of ex-Gaekwar of Baroda, Mulhar Rao, 1881; asst. rest., Hyderabad, 1882; asst. commr., Ajmir, Nov., 1883; 1st asst. agent, Rajputana, 1884; addl. pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1885; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Kotah, Jan., 1889; offg. commr., Ajmir, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, Jhalawar, in 1891 and 1892; offg. rest., states of

Rajputana, May, 1893; rest., Mewar, March, 1894; rest., West Rajputana states, June, 1898; ret'd. from I.S.C., July, 1901; pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state, July, 1901; M.V.O., Aug., 1902; K.C.I.E., Sept., 1902.

WYNCH, LIONEL MALING, B.A., Indian C.S. (private sec. to govr. of Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to land rev. commrs., Feb., 1894; sec. to commrs. of salt, Abbark, and sep. rev., Oct., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1897; collr. and mag., Dec., 1899; private sec. to govr. of Madras, March, 1901.

WYNDHAM, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 13th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1894; dep. commr., Sept., 1899; mag. and collr., March, 1900; dep. commr., 1st grade, Dec., 1901.

WYNNE, SYDENHAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 21st Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. to the collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Oct., 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; collr. and mag., Jan., 1890; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1898; ret'd. Aug., 1898.

YASIN KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Yasin Khan).

YATE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.M.G., Col., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan).—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settl. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhob, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; confd. 1897; rest., Mewar, June, 1898; offg. resdnt., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1899; agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1900; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' 'Khurasan and Seistan,' &c.

YATES, LOUIS EDMUND HASSELLS., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. loco. supt.).—Apptd. asst. loco. supt., 22nd Feb., 1880; dist. loco. supt., E. Bengal state rly., Aug., 1887; ditto, N.W. rly., Oct., 1899; dep. loco. supt., N.W. rly., July, 1901.

YEATS, SIDNEY KILNER LEVETT, India Public Works Dept. (examr.).—Apptd. asst. exam., 28th May, 1878; dep. examr., May, 1886; examr., Aug., 1893.

YELD, HORACE PARR, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1874; was employed in the mil. dept. up to Aug., 1884, when he was apptd. prob. in the assay dept., Bombay mint; transf. to Calcutta mint, Sept., 1884; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1886, and Calcutta mint, Jan., 1888; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

YEWDALE, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Leys Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 10th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

YOUNG, FRANK POPHAM, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (settl. commr., Patiala State).—Entered the service, Aug., 1884; in mil. employ till Dec., 1888; apptd. to the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., Dec., 1888; dep. commr., April, 1898; C.I.E., June, 1899; settl. commr. Patiala State, Jan., 1900; pol. agent, Phulkian States in addition, Jan. to Nov., 1901.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, India Office (2nd class clerk, finl. dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 22nd May, 1888; transf. to finl. dept., 14th May, 1901.

YOUNG, GEORGE GORDON, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 23rd Oct., 1856; from June, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; dep. commr., Jan., 1873; commr. and supt., Jan., 1881; offg. 2nd finl. commr., 1888 and 1890; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

YOUNG, HARRY GAVIN, Madras Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-gen.).—Entered the service, April, 1874, and served as inspr., asst. supt., and supt. of police; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1895.

YOUNG, JAMES, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., July, 1879; ret'd., 1899.

YOUNG, JAMES DAVID (dist. supt. of police, United Prov.).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1879, and acted as asst. dist. supt.; confd., March, 1881; dist. supt., April, 1885.

YOUNG, MATTHEW, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., May, 1900.

YOUNG, WILLIAM EDLIN, Sind commn. (collr. of customs, Sind).—Entered the service 17th Aug., 1872, and served as extra asst. commr. of customs, Bombay; asst. collr., Feb., 1881; collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind, Aug., 1900.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM MACKWORTH, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settl. officer, and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., Oct., 1873; settl. sec. to finl. commr., March, 1875; dep. commr., Jan., 1878; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Feb., 1878; sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1880; commr. and supt., May, 1887; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1888; commr., Jalandhar divn., April, 1888; 2nd finl. commr. of the

Punjab, April, 1889; 1st finl. commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June-Nov., 1893; prest. of the Indian hemp drugs commn., Oct., 1893; rest., Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Sept., 1895; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, March, 1897; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., March, 1902.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ARTHUR DELAVAL, Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clifton Coll.; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1875; arrived, 9th Dec., 1877, and served as asst. collr. and mag., talukdari settlt. officer, and asst. pol. agent; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1890; and as administrator, Rajpipla state, in 1888 and 1890-91; transfld. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Nov., 1893; commr., April, 1897; and acting pol. agent, April, 1897; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; retransfd. to Bombay, March, 1902; commr., April, 1902; addl. member of legisl. council, July, 1903.

YOUNGHUSBAND, FRANCIS EDWARD, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (with Tibet Frontier commn.).—First commn., 10th May, 1882; apptd. to the foreign dept., July, 1889; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, April, 1892; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1892; asst. to govr.-genl.'s agent, Rajputana, Nov., 1897; pol. agent in Haraoti and

Tonk, Sept., 1898; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; offg. resdt., Indore, Feb., 1902; on depn. with Tibet Frontier commn. from June, 1903.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ROMER EDWARD, B.A., Barr. (1879), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 14th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., March, 1886; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888, to April, 1890; offg. junr.-sec., May, 1893; dep. commr., Sept., 1894; chief pol. offr., Tochi Field Force, July, 1897; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1898, to Feb., 1900.

YUSUF, MUHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Patna Coll. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. mag. and collr., Jan., 1896; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1899.

YUSUF ALI, A. (see Abdullah Ibn Yusuf Ali).

ZORAB, JOHN MANOOK, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the mil. dept., and as civil surgn., Balasor, March, 1887, Cuttack, March, 1891, Bahaglpur, April 1896, Cuttack, June, 1897; ret'd., Dec., 1901.

ADDENDA.

À ABABRELTON, ROBERT RELTON DE RELTON, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 15th Sept., 1903.

KENNEDY, STEWART, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 10th Feb., 1904.

RICHARDS, HENRY ERLE (member of govr.-genl.'s council).—Educ. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 4th May, 1887; nominated to succeed Mr. Raleigh as legal member of govr.-genl.'s council, 1904.

CASUALTIES.RETIREMENTS.
Name. Service or Appointment. Date.
Abdul Hamid, Khan Bahadur ... Provincial Civil Service, United Provinces 17 Jan., 1903.
Achutan Nayar, U. ... Provincial Civil Service, Madras ... 17 Nov., "
Akbar Husain, Saiyid, Khan Bahadur. Provincial Civil Service, United Provinces 24 Dec., "
Alexander, E. B. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 19 Sept., "
Alexander, E. J. ... Public Works Department, Railways ... 1 April, "
Allison, Lt.-Col. H., I.M.S. ... Surgeon and Professor, Medical College, Madras 23 Dec., "
Anant Ram ... Provincial Civil Service, United Provinces 1 Sept., "
Anson, Lt.-Col. G. W., I.A. ... Commissioner, United Provinces ... 30 Nov., 1903.*
Bailey, R. J. ... Public Works Department, Burma ... 7 Dec., 1903.
Barfoot, G. ... Messenger, India Store Depôt ... 23 March, "
Barry, Lt.-Col. D. F., I.M.S. ... Civil Surgeon, United Provinces ... 10 Dec., "
Barwick, Commander F. M. ... Royal Indian Marine ... 14 Sept., "
Bashyam Aiyanger, Sir V., C.I.E. ... Judge of the High Court, Madras ... 10 Jan., 1904.
Batten, H. G. ... Deputy Commissioner, Burma ... 28 April, 1903.
Bean, J. D. C. W. ... Indian Police, Punjab ... 15 May, "
Bipradas Chatterji ... Provincial Civil Service, Bengal ... 19 July, "
Bose, Pramatha Nath, B.Sc. ... Geological Survey of India ... 1 Dec., "
Brind, M. J. ... Indian Telegraph Department ... 3 March, "
Broome, L. N. ... Indian Police, Punjab ... 22 Nov., "
Calthrop, H. ... Indian Forest Service, Burma ... 4 Dec., "
Candy, E. T., C.S.I. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay... ... 12 July, "
Carson, Lt.-Col. W. P., I.M.S. ... Civil Surgeon, Bombay... ... 28 Jan., 1904.
Carstairs, R. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 16 Oct., 1903.
Cartland, F. ... Registrar, Military Department, Government of India 1 Nov., "
Charles, T. G. ... Indian Police, Bengal ... 1 April, "
Chattopadhyay, G. C., Rai Sahib... Public Works Department, Railways ... 5 Oct., "
Chunder Naraina Singh, Rai Bahadur. Collector of Stamp Revenue, Calcutta ... 12 June, "
Clarke, Col. H., R.E. ... Public Works Department, Accounts Branch ... 17 Sept., 1903*
Clarkson, Lt.-Col. J. W., I.M.S. ... Sanitary Commissioner, Bombay ... 31 March, "
Clôete, H. N. C. ... Public Works Department, Bengal ... 17 Oct., "
Clowes, T. H. ... Public Works Department, Bengal ... 23 Sept., "
Conley, A. ... Public Works Department, Accounts Branch ... 30 Nov., "
Connor, E. J. ... Survey of India ... 18 Aug., "
Crawford, J. A. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay... ... 16 Aug., "
Dantra, Lt.-Col. S. H., I.M.S. ... Civil Surgeon, Burma ... 10 July, "
Daulat Ram, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur... Postal Department, Government of India ... 31 Jan., "
Davis, A. A. F. ... Public Works Department, Bengal ... 28 Oct., "
Deacon, W. J. ... Messenger, India Office ... 9 July, "
Desmazures, L. C. ... Public Works Department, Railways ... 1 July, "
Downes, Commander P. ... Royal Indian Marine ... 26 March, "
Drewet, T. ... Senior Inspector of Steam Boilers, Bombay ... 21 Jan., "
Dudgeon, P. J. ... Public Works Department, Railways ... 21 Dec., "
Dumergue, J. W. F. ... Indian Civil Service, Madras ... 1 April, "
Durham, Rev. R. H., D.D. ... Madras Ecclesiastical Department ... 22 June, "
Duthie, J. F. ... Director, Botanical Department, Northern India 1 Jan., "
Eliot, Sir J., K.C.I.E., F.R.S. ... Meteorological Reporter and Director-General of Indian Observatories 1 Jan., 1904.
Elliott, A., C.I.E. ... Judicial Commissioner, Berar... ... 1 April, 1903.
Ewing, R. C. D. ... Survey of India ... 1 Dec., "
Fanshawe, R. A. L. ... Indian Police, Burma ... 2 March, "
Finlay, J. F., C.S.I. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 10 May, "
Finn, F. .... Deputy Superintendent, Indian Museum (resigned) 2 Dec., "

* Date of transfer to Unemployed List.

RETIREMENTS—continued.
Name. Service or Appointment. Date.
Finucane, M., C.S.I. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 14 Oct., 1903.
Fox, A. J. ... Public Works Department, Accounts Branch ... 27 Oct., 1902.
Frith, Lieut. W. W. C. ... Royal Indian Marine (resigned) ... 5 Aug., 1903.
Fryer, Sir F. W. R., K.C.S.I. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 4 April, "
Gadsden, Chief Engr. F. O. ... Royal Indian Marine ... 19 Oct., 1903.
Girish Chandra Bose ... Provincial Civil Service, United Provinces ... 16 Jan., "
Godfrey, F. E. ... Public Works Department, Accounts Branch ... 2 April, "
Goldsmid, F. L. ... Indian Police, Bombay ... 29 Oct., "
Goodridge, Capt. W. S., C.I.E., R.N. ... Director, Royal Indian Marine ... 5 March, 1904.
Gouldsbury, C. E. ... Indian Police, Bengal ... 20 Jan., 1903.
Gowhar Ally ... Provincial Civil Service, Bengal ... 25 Dec., "
Grant, A. ... Public Works Department, Hyderabad ... 28 Sept., "
Grierson, G. A., C.I.E. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 16 Sept., "
Groves, G. Barton ... Postal Department, Government of India ... 3 Nov., "
Groves, H. ... Public Works Department, Burma ... 3 Sept., "
Gungaram, C. I. E., Rai Bahadur ... Public Works Department, Punjab ... 19 Oct., "
Hammond, A. J. ... Indian Police, Punjab ... 15 May, "
Handcock, W. E. F. ... Public Works Department, Punjab ... 5 Dec., "
Harding, Hony. Capt. W. H.,
I.S.M.D. ...		 Civil Surgeon, United Provinces ... 27 Nov., "
Harkness, J. ... Public Works Department, Railways ... 10 Feb., "
Harris, R. L. ... Indian Civil Service, Punjab ... 1 April, "
Hatch, Lt.-Col. W. K., I.M.S. ... Principal, Grant Medical College ... 15 Nov., "
Hawkes, G. ... Public Works Department, Railways ... 1 July, "
Henderson, H. J. H. ... Indian Police, Bombay ... 10 May, "
Hendley, Col. T. H., C.I.E., I.M.S. ... Inspector-General of Civil Hospitals, Bengal ... 10 April, "
Herron, H. G. W. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 9 Jan., 1904.
Higgins, A. W. B. ... Indian Civil Service, Madras ... 16 May, 1903.
Hobson, G. R. A. ... Assistant Supervisor of Stamps, Stamp
Branch, Store Department, India Office ...		 1 Aug., "
Hultzsch, E. ... Epigraphist, Madras (resigned) ... 26 Nov., "
Hunter, D. C. H. ... Indian Police, Punjab ... 14 March, "
Hutchinson, Rev. F. E. ... Chaplain, Royal Indian Engineering College ... 24 Sept., "
Hutchinson, F. T. ... Public Works Department, Accounts Branch ... 7 Oct., "
Irwin, G. R., C.S.I. ... Indian Civil Service, Bengal ... 27 Nov., "
Johnston, J. C. ... Public Works Department, Madras ... 26 April, "
Kali Kumar Bose ... Provincial Civil Service, Bengal ... 15 Sept., "
Kali Prasanna Sinha ... Provincial Civil Service, United Provinces ... 8 Feb., "
Kennedy, H., C.S.I. ... Commissioner of Police, Bombay ... 1 Jan., 1904.
Lang, R. D. M. ... Public Works Department, United Provinces ... 8 Dec., 1905.
Larkins, Miss A. S. ... Typist, India Office (resigned) ... 17 June, "
Leahy, Lt.-Col. A. W. D., I.M.S. ... Civil Surgeon, Bengal ... 5 June, "
Lee, E. A. ... Public Works Department, Accounts Branch ... 22 Oct., "
Loch, Lt.-Col. W., C.I.E., I.A. ... Principal, Mayo College ... 7 March, 1903.*
Lushington, Lt.-Col., E. C. M., I.A. ... Military Secretary to Resdt., Hyderabad ... 8 Aug., 1903.*
Lyon, Hony. Capt. W. F. ... Public Works Department, Bombay ... 12 Oct., 1903.
Mac Alister, R. ... Superintendent, Roorkee Workshops,
United Provinces ...		 29 Dec., "
MacArthur, Right Rev. J., D.D. ... Lord Bishop of Bombay (resigned) ... Oct., "
